Advocate: Champion for public improvements, capital investments and economic development initiatives in Downtown Jamaica.

The Downtown Jamaica Partnership (legally known as the Sutphin Boulevard District Management Association, Inc.) is a newly expanded Business Improvement District (BID) that is made up of three former BIDs: the Jamaica Center BID, the Sutphin Boulevard BID and the 165th Street Mall BID.

Name: Sydney Planners Society
Description:
Welcome to the Sydney Planners Society (SPS), the premier hub at Western Sydney University for passionate individuals dedicated to the multifaceted world of urban planning and development.

 About Us:
At SPS, we are committed to bridging theory and practice, providing a collaborative space for students, staff, alumni, and enthusiasts keen on shaping the future of our cities. Established to address the absence of a dedicated planning community at WSU, SPS is a pioneering initiative that seeks to enrich our members with global perspectives, critical thinking, and hands-on experiences in the dynamic field of urban planning.

 Our Focus Areas:
Diving deep into planning, placemaking, urbanism, and related disciplines, SPS explores critical themes such as micromobility, spatial and data analysis, social participation, accessibility, and sustainability. With a commitment to the United Nations' 2030 Sustainable Development Goals, we aim to create a space that fosters inclusivity, diversity, and excellence in planning practices.

 Annual Calendar of Events:
Our meticulously curated calendar features a diverse range of activities, from Sydney Planners Conferences and speakers' sessions to career days, excursions, and interactive events. We believe in continuous learning and offer bi-monthly articles, book club meetings, and a yearly journal release to keep our members informed and inspired.

 Leadership Opportunities:
SPS provides a platform for individuals to take on leadership roles within the executive team. Whether you're interested in event management, marketing, operations, or community relations, SPS offers a breadth of opportunities for personal and professional development.

 Global Connections:
Embracing the ethos of being 'Urbanist Global Citizens,' SPS actively collaborates with international organizations, renowned experts, and creators allowing our members to connect with knowledge from around the world!

Contact Us: sydplansoc@gmail.com

Name: Jane Jacobs Society
Description:
De Jane Jacobs Society houdt het gedachtegoed van Jane Jacobs levend in de stad van vandaag en morgen 

We brengen mensen samen die zich inzetten voor een leefbare stad: met oog voor de menselijke maat, levendige buurten en bewonersinvloed.
We delen kennis, organiseren ontmoetingen en verkennen hoe Jane’s ideeën ook vandaag nog richting (zouden moeten) geven aan stedelijke ontwikkeling.

Onze vereniging wil een platform bieden om kennis en ervaring uit te wisselen, inspirerende voorbeelden beschikbaar te stellen, samen een bibliotheek op te bouwen en gelijkgestemden te vinden. Met elkaar vormen we een beweging die het gedachtegoed van Jane Jacobs en haar opvolgers uitdraagt, verspreidt, praktijkvoorbeelden deelt, en ijvert om formele en informele instituties die de daadwerkelijke implementatie van het gedachtegoed in de weg staan om te vormen.

Cada história nos move.

Name: podium project
Description:
The Podium Project was born because of a truth we can’t ignore: even today, most stages are dominated by men. But every time a woman’s voice is missing, we lose something — a perspective, a story, a lived experience. We (Maggie, Kristine, and Sheana) believe the reason so many women aren’t on stage isn’t because they lack ideas or passion; it’s because they often lack the tools to be seen as speakers: polished reels, compelling bios, branding photos, feedback, and a community that supports visibility.

The Podium Project exists to shift that. We help women gain those assets. We give them space to practice, feedback to grow, and confidence to step forward. We will track what happens: how many stages they step onto, how many speaking deals they land, how many voices are amplified. Because when women speak, when we all hear more of the stories, the insights, the leadership, everyone benefits.

Our vision is bold: a future where when you walk into any conference lineup, you don’t even think about “where are the women?” because you see them in leadership, as keynote speakers, at panels, across fields. 

The Podium Project is here so that every woman who wants to speak has the assets, the confidence, and the support to do so.

Enkele leden van die werkgroep zijn vanaf 2018 aan de slag gegaan om te kijken wat de nieuwbouw voor kansen voor de buurt zou bieden. Dat spitste zich toe op werk en ontmoeten.

Daarvoor zijn twee stichtingen in het leven geroepen:

BOB Helpt: doel is om mensen uit de buurt aan werk te helpen in de buurt. Er komt veel werkgelegenheid bij op Oostenburg Noord. Daar kunnen mensen aan de slag die anders niet zo makkelijk aan een baan komen.

Over de Brug: doel is ontmoeting van buurtbewoners zowel de nieuwe bewoners met elkaar als met de huidige bewoners. Daarvoor is er een Buurtkamer, wordt een sociaal culturele programmering bevorderd in samenspraak met zoveel mogelijk bestaande buurtinitiatieven en nieuwe initiatieven van de nieuwe bewoners van Oostenburg Noord.

Description:

Its projects and activities promote sustainable inclusive education, personal and collective resilience, intergenerational mental health and inner development, collaborative urban regeneration and engagment of human and biodiversity. It empowers young people through gamified e-portfolios - a reflective learning tool used collaborativly by students and multistakeholders of changemaking. It bridges the industires of higher education and business (especcially social entrepreneurship) with innovation camps, service learning, project hatchieries, appreciative inquiry and mockathon sessions. It helps multiple stakeholder alliences with collaborative intent for community development and urban regeneration. It offers support with challenge-based methods of research and student learning to HIgher Education institutions, especcially embedding changemaking and social innovation into curriculum design.

INNOCAMP PL helps top executives to transform themselves and their organizations into empathy & compassion driven subjects. It offeres training retreats and audits to encourage true sustainability attitudes, ethical fibre, prevent burn-out and mobing/dominance. It engages service leaders in regenerative communities, catharthis improv sessions, forest baths and other well-being rituals which are followed by reflective learning laboratories. One of its programs - LICO empowers women by reflexology (SORENSEN System) and taping techniques to help them reclaim and be their own faces. INNOCAMP PL works with powerful clients on their diversity and inclusion in the context of multicultural uncertainty. Currently it leads two Erasmus Plus European Comission funded projects (SUMED and JOY in SPORTS) with partners in 8 countries and participates in 3 more (PUNC, ACIIS, JOIN IN) and 2 Creative Europe projects (Future Divercities, Getting Unstuck).

Name: Australian Walking and Cycling Conference
Description: The simple acts of walking and cycling have the potential to transform the places we live, our economies and how we engage with our environment. The Australian Walking and Cycling conference explores the potential for walking and cycling to not only provide for transport and recreation but solutions to challenges of liveability, health, community building, economic development and sustainability. As one of Australia’s longest running, best regarded and most affordable active travel conferences, we bring together practitioners and researchers from Australia and across the world to share their work and engage with conference participants.

Name: LGBTQ and Planning Division of APA
Description:
The LGBTQ and Planning Division of APA is a forum for the exchange of ideas and information of interest to people in the planning profession who identify as part of the LGBTQ community and anyone who is interested in LGBTQ community issues.

The division addresses planning topics and issues that relate to the LGBTQ community by providing a forum for exchanging ideas and information. It provides a professional network for planners who are division members and a mechanism that allows friends of the division and the LGBTQ community to collaborate and support the division's mission.

The LGBTQ and Planning Division evolved in 2016 from the Gays and Lesbians in Planning (GALIP) Division that was created as a formal APA division in 1998 at the APA National Planning Conference in Boston. We've been an informal network since 1992 when we met for the first time at the national conference in Washington, D.C.

Name: Partnership for Research on Informal and Shared Mobility
Description:
Our Vision
The Partnership for Research on Informal and Shared Mobility (PRISM) is a global research consortium hosted at the Columbia Climate School's Center for Sustainable Urban Development that focuses on better understanding informal transport and shared mobility (ISM) with a focus on Asia, Africa, and Latin America. We explore pathways for enhancing access, services, working conditions, and reducing emissions from ISM, particularly for lower-income residents and their communities. Recognizing that vast number of people across the globe rely on popular transport modes that are informal and shared such as rickshaws, motorcycle taxis and minibuses, our aim is to explore how to work with and improve these services as well as support integration into institutionalized public transport modes as part of a focus on equity and justice, including climate justice.

Name: Shrinking Cities International Research Network SCiRN
Description: SCiRN was founded in 2004 under the aegis of the Institute of Urban and Regional Development at the University of California, Berkely. Although city decline was a worldwide issue, there was little comparative research on this topic at that time. Since then, SCiRN has aimed to advance international understanding and promote scholarship about "shrinking cities',"  causes, manifestations, spatial variations, and effectiveness of policies and planning interventinos to stave off decline.

Name: Big Al Connolly
Description:
I provide Strategic Advice to Micro/Small/Home-Based Business Owners, Councils and Community Organisations in Civic Wealth Creation and Community Wellbeing.

Communities grow when we start with what’s strong, not what’s wrong

When we talk about Civic Wealth Creation, it doesn’t need to start big. In fact, the most powerful transformations often begin small, in the inner circle of a Community, where trust lives and local talent already exists.

The heart of Civic Wealth Creation is a simple wheel:

Entrepreneurial Development (the spark)
One person’s idea, skill, or lived experience grows into a micro-enterprise.

Economic Development (the system)
Local councils, anchor institutions, and networks provide the soil and scaffolding to help it grow.

Community Development (the impact) 
The benefits include jobs, housing, wellbeing, staying local, and strengthening the whole neighborhood.

This cycle is the essence of Civic Wealth Creation. What starts small, can grow infinitely because each turn of the wheel builds new capacity for the next.

If this resonates with you, then we are a fit and you should reach out to me here...... http://bigalconnolly.com

Kindest regards

Allan 'Big Al' Connolly

Description:

Its main aim is to provide a focal point regarding urban underground space that will gather experts from multidisciplinary scientific fields and will allow them to form coherent initiatives regarding the promotion of subsurface development and use of underground infrastructure that will eventually benefit city life.

University of Belgrade (UB), the Faculty of Transport and Traffic Engineering of the University of Belgrade (FTTE), the Urban Institute of Belgrade (URBEL) and the Institute of Architecture and Urban & Spatial Planning of the Republic of Serbia (IAUS) organize the ACUUS 2025 BELGRADE Conference.

With this conference, we want to engage with all of you in an open and constructive discussion about the further development and improvement of the use of the UUS and its progress towards its recognition and implementation in the current municipal and legislative systems.

More information on the conference web site.

Or you can contact us via: info@acuus2025.com

Name: Ipswich Social Mobility Alliance
Description:
Let’s start with what the Ipswich Social Mobility Alliance (ISMA) isn’t. We are not a new organisation that will seek funding for delivery – we recognise and respect there are lots of organisations with experience, expertise and networks already doing good work in our town.

We aspire to be a true alliance – organisations and individuals that work better together across sectors and hierarchies for the benefit of the children and young people of Ipswich, with the ambition to put their voice and needs at the centre of everything that we collectively do.

The dichotomy we have in Ipswich (as with many places in the UK) is that while we appear to have the assets needed for strong social mobility: good schools and colleges, a university, local services and voluntary sector organisations that meet the needs of the most vulnerable, opportunities for children to gain social and cultural capital, and visible career and labour market opportunities – children and young people in our town are less likely to go as far in their education and on to the careers and lives that they hope for as in other places.

With resource and capacity as squeezed as ever it has never been more important to be joined up in how we use them. 

The ISMA is a growing alliance of existing organisations and networks in Ipswich from local heavy weights to small grass roots organisations, all committed to challenging this status quo, by looking at how we can work better together. 

We are in the process of strategy development - and to do this we need everyone at the table, bringing a true understanding of the barriers and opportunities that make up a system in our town that we believe can work better, and deliver brilliant outcomes for our young people.

Children and young people themselves need to drive our thinking and our strategy building plan between September 2022 and 2023, and our “Ipswich Voices Fringe Festival” in Spring 2023 will put them font and centre in the decision making process.

Welcome to CryptoCity, a groundbreaking initiative redefining urban communities. Located on Isla de Margarita, Venezuela, CryptoCity merges advanced technology, sustainability, and a crypto-based economy, creating an ideal environment for high-net-worth entrepreneurs and innovators.

Our Vision

CryptoCity aims to be a global hub for entrepreneurial activities and networking. Our community offers a luxurious retreat and an active role in a dynamic society.

Our Mission

We build a secure, transparent, and sustainable environment where modern technologies and innovative business models thrive. By integrating blockchain technology and renewable energy, we create a self-sustaining and eco-friendly community.

Key Features of CryptoCity

Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO): Structured as a DAO, members receive NFTs with land purchases, granting voting rights on community decisions, promoting democratic governance. In the DAO, new business ideas will be voted, financed and executed. Take a Real Estate project worth 1 Million Dollars for example. Split in 100 Sharesand it is 10k for everyone. 

Comprehensive Master Plan: The plan includes residential areas, a beach club, office complexes, retail spaces, and high-end dining, all close to amenities like a hotel, golf course, and port.

Sustainable Infrastructure: Renewable energy and advanced storage technologies ensure a sustainable, eco-friendly community.

Global Networking: CryptoCity provides unique networking opportunities and top-tier infrastructure, fostering business collaboration and innovation.

Join CryptoCity

Become a part of our community and help shape the future of urban development. Experience the perfect blend of luxury, innovation, and sustainability. Visit our website www.cryptocity.land to learn more and join us.

Reach us at info@cryptocity.land for more information and inquiries.

#SmartCities #Blockchain #Innovation #Emigrate # Venezuela

Name: Future City Circle
Description:
Future City Circle (FCC) is a community founded by students of the Smart City Solutions Master’s program at HFT Stuttgart. What started as a simple idea to stay connected beyond graduation has grown into a platform where students, alumni, lecturers, and industry experts meet to share knowledge, opportunities, and experiences.

We believe that the strength of a smart city lies not only in technology or infrastructure but also in the connections among the people shaping it. FCC bridges education and professional practice, offering a space to continue learning, collaborate across disciplines, and co-create ideas that make cities smarter, greener, and more inclusive.

Our vision is to connect the Smart City Solutions community at HFT Stuttgart and build a strong professional network that extends internationally, linking universities, cities, and companies across Europe and beyond.

At Future City Circle, we share stories, support career growth, and inspire collaboration, because:

A smart city starts with community.

Name: Urban GreenWorks
Description:
Urban GreenWorks (UGW) started over 10 years ago with the goals of providing south Floridians with improved access to healthy local foods, environmental education, horticulture therapy, and volunteer opportunities. Zoom ahead and the foundation continues to develop, adapt, and evolve yet the core remains the same: ...of the Community...for the Community...by the Community!  

Today, as UGW, we are ready to implement a sustainable vision towards three (3) main goals:

1. Personal & Social Responsibility - the ethical obligation, the actions, and the way in which we examine our individual influences to ensure the well being of all members of the community;

2. Food Security - the state of having sustainable access to available quantity, affordable, and nutritious food; and

3. Planetary Health - the health of human civilization and the natural systems on which it depends.

We exist to support and restore the economic, social, & physical health of marginalized communities,  where most of the "food deserts" (urban areas where access to affordable and nutritious food is limited) are located. Our work is diversified between: 

• Developing, building and planting resilient and sustainable community partnerships, and 

• Creating a new narrative of success for these partnerships to develop and grow. 

Urban GreenWorks is a 501(c)(3) organization with operations in Miami FL & Richmond VA, and partnership in Kenya.

Stay up to date with our current running projects, campaigns, and affiliations at https://donately.com/.

Welcome to UGW 2.1!

Name: Tungeni Urban Initiative
Description:
Tungeni Urban Initiative (TUI) is a social impact non- profit organisation committed to fostering inclusive and sustainable urban development in Namibia. Rooted in the principles of participatory planning, we work alongside communities to co-create solutions that enhance livelihoods, strengthen local governance and promote environmental sustainability. Established in 2023, TUI aims to bridge the gap between policy and everyday urban realities by making sustainability practical, accessible and community-centered. Through engagement, advocacy and capacity-building initiatives, we empower residents to take an active role in shaping the future of their towns and cities while fostering partnerships with local authorities to encourage sustainable growth and development in their towns and cities.

Our Mission
Is to empower local communities, advocate for inclusive urban policies and implement practical solutions that improve service delivery, living conditions, infrastructure and environmental sustainability. Through strategic partnerships, capacity building and community led initiatives, we aim to create livable, resilient and people centered urban environments.

Description: Tsiyon Urban Development is a community focused organization dedicated to improving livability in underprivileged cities and towns.  Our organization is compelled and inspired by the opportunities that exist on the continent and the many ways we have been blessed with knowledge that will benefit the growth of communities.  Our services address the many barriers and challenges that urban communities face that prohibits growth both educationally and economically. Name: Ambos Community Trust Fal Ltd.
Description:
Ambos is a Community Land Trust focused on securing land and buildings for our neighbourhood’s wellbeing. We are passionate place-shapers, champions of collective ownership and advocates of exemplary design that fosters a deep sense of belonging.
Ambos Community Trust Fal Ltd, is the Community Land Trust set-up and legally bound to secure, develop and hold strategic assets for community benefit in perpetuity. 

We rely on local land owners, local authority and social enterprises  to sell or lease land to the Ambos Community Trust to help us provide  benefits to the local area. The benefit to the neighbourhood is identified though local neighbourhood development plans (NDP) and genuine community engagement. 

Assets are usually buildings and/or land, which members determine the best use, along with the wider community. 

Our membership consists of ‘crews’ who have a passion for affordable cohousing, community owned renewable energy and wellness space. Do you have an ideas for a piece of land or building?

Membership is open to all, with voting right reserved for local residents.

We also act as an  Educational Trust, promoting cooperative design to accelerate enhancements in built environment that help neighbourhoods adapt to climate change and give agency to those who don’t normally feel they get a say as to what happens in their locality. 

Ambos Project Services Ltd., our service arm, is set up to  help facilitate and support community groups seeking to develop strategic assets for community benefit.

We offer a range of services to support organisations and individuals undertaking community-led initiatives.

Name: ParkInSync
Description:
Vi skapar ett innovativt ekosystem genom en SaaS-tjänst och molnplattform som främjar utvecklingen av städer och mobilitet. 

ParkInSync integreras sömlöst med befintliga infrastruktursystem via öppna API-lösningar och IoT-teknik, utan att störa nuvarande lösningar. Vi är i ett tidigt skede men har redan väckt intresse hos ledande aktörer inom mobilitet och stadsutveckling. Som en affärsenhet inom en organisation med partners och investerare strävar vi efter effektiv implementering. Vår ambition är att positionera Sverige som en innovationsnation inom smart urbanisering.

Tillväxtplan:
• 2028: Implementering i Sverige följt av 
• 2030: Skandinavien 
• 2030-2035: Etablering i Europa med bevisad skalbarhet 
• 2035-2040: Global närvaro 

ParkInSync byggs för att bli en central del av framtidens beslutsfattande, där realtidsdata ersätter gissningar i stadsplaneringen.

Vår mission är att leda omställningen mot klimatneutrala städer, reducera CO₂-utsläpp och ge kommuner och regioner rätt verktyg för att optimera stadsmiljö och infrastruktur.

Name: Fundación Oasis Urbano
Description: Fundación Oasis Urbano is a registered Colombian non profit organization based in Medellín which was founded by a team of community leaders from Moravia and architects from Berlin. We understand self built neighborhoods as a crucial part of the solutions for the challenges cities are facing today and in future. We center planning processes on vulnerable communities and bring urban stakeholders together on site to bridge the gap between bottom up initiatives and top down planning. We connect local communities with academia and science, cultural and civic organizations, activists, artists, businesses and public decision makers, to share empiric knowledge and experiences, build trust, and co-design inclusive and liveable public spaces. We have realized several disruptive projects with the people of Moravia and co-created a set of tools for community driven change that enables habitants to become agents in the transformation of their habitat. This approach has inspired urban transformations in informal contexts across Colombia, Latin America and beyond.

Name: Voice of the Terreiro
Description:
A Voz do Terreiro é um ecossistema de inovação social e desenvolvimento territorial focado na transformação de comunidades em "Cidades Modelo" autônomas, prósperas e sustentáveis.

Nossa atuação baseia-se na tese da Economia Ancestral Regenerativa (EAR). Rompemos com a lógica assistencialista tradicional para implementar um novo "sistema operacional" urbano, onde o maior ativo econômico é o Capital Cultural e Social ("O Terreiro") já existente no território.

Nossa Abordagem:

Atuamos na intersecção entre a Sabedoria Ancestral e a Tecnologia Social Contemporânea. Através de uma governança descentralizada e ferramentas de economia circular, transformamos identidade em prosperidade.

Nossa Missão 2045:

Não apenas geramos impacto, construímos legado. Nosso Roadmap Estratégico visa catalisar ecossistemas onde o bem-estar integral, a segurança e a riqueza financeira são consequências naturais do fortalecimento dos laços comunitários.

Somos o elo entre a potência das raízes brasileiras e o futuro das cidades inteligentes e humanas.

Junte-se ao movimento que está redefinindo o conceito de riqueza no Brasil.

Name: Brightside Collective
Description:
Brightside reimagines cities from the soil up - transforming everyday spaces into vibrant, life-giving places where people and nature flourish together.

Founded in Detroit, we’re a collective of community stewards, ecological designers, strategists, creatives, and systems thinkers working at the intersection of beauty, biodiversity, and belonging. From wildflower meadows to microforests and pollinator gardens and pathways, we help communities, landowners, and institutions bring land to life through low-cost, high-impact design that scales neighborhood by neighborhood.

At the heart of our approach is the belief that small, thoughtful actions can ripple outward - healing ecosystems, reconnecting people to place, and reshaping urban life for our shared future.

Name: J29:7 Planning & Development Corporation
Description:
Our mission is to seek the welfare of the places we've been called to serve and invite others to join us through inclusive engagement and equitable and sustainable development. J29:7 takes a holistic approach to development that focuses not just on projects and buildings but on the holistic development of the communities in which we develop and the people that live there. We believe in identifying the challenges that systematically cause people to be left behind and then working together with communities to develop sustainable and equitable solutions that solve those challenges.

In addition to developing our own pipeline of real estate projects, our services include Master planning, Real Estate Consulting, and Owners Representative services.

We are currently focused on 5 community led master plans in 5 cities, working with 5 Faith Based Organizations (FBOs) on $500+ Million of proposed investment. Land Planning Group has and is working on many exciting projects throughout Florida and offers a wide range of planning services that are driven by local knowledge and historical perspective unlike any other in the Central Florida area.  Land Planning Group consists of a variety of professionals within a client-driven organization that utilizes the “team concept" to provide effective, high-quality communication and expeditious solutions. We bridge the gap between CDPs, non-profits, businesses, and government initiatives, ensuring that social programs are effectively implemented and truly benefit communities. The Councils cooperatively share staff, and the two Boards of Directors work closely together to develop the community so that all residents benefit, especially existing neighbors and those most at risk of displacement. The Councils’ vision is to create a C.A.R.I.N.G community--one that is Collaborative, Advocating, Respectful, Inspiring, Neighborly, and Gracious.

Name: NATURO_project
Description:
NATURO is a three year project (2023-2025) financed by the European Driving Urban Transitions Partnership (DUT).

In NATURO we study how quality of life can be increased for marginalised groups by using the potential of urban nature and integrating them in public management organisations daily work.

Our work will develop a strategic framework for capacity building and empowerment of both local government and civil society stakeholders to co-create Nature Based Solutions that enable the inclusion of marginalized groups in long-term management of public urban green spaces.

The NATURO framework will be used, evaluated and refined in real-life settings – we will establish Micro Urban Living Labs in three European cities: City of Velenje in Slovenia will focus on green transformations. Den Bosch in the Netherlands will involve diverse user groups in the development of a green and climate-adapted city. Tyresö in Sweden will focus on reflexive governance and co-creation of urban green spaces within the municipality.

NATURO research partners are: Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (lead partner), Wageningen University (NL) and Urban Planning Institute of the Republic of Slovenia.

NATURO cities are: City of Velenje in Slovenia, ‘S Hertogenbosch in the Netherlands andTyresö in Sweden. NATURO knowledge dissemination partners: Living Cities and Communities and European Urban Knowledge Network.

This project has been funded by The Swedish Research Council (Formas), The Dutch Research Council (NOW), and The Slovenian Research Agency (ARRS), under the Driving Urban Transitions Partnership, which has been co-funded by the European Commission. Project number: F-DUT-2022-0249

Name: HORIZON-URBAINE
Description:
Expert en Modernisation Territoriale & Smart City | Promoteur du Projet HORIZON | Adressage, SIG & Fiscalité Locale | Transformation Numérique des Communes

Nous accompagnons les collectivités territoriales dans leur quête de sécurité foncière.

À travers le Projet HORIZON, nous transformons la gestion urbaine par la création de 'jumeaux numériques' territoriaux.

Notre approche brise le cloisonnement des données en créant des passerelles entre l'adressage métrique, le cadastre numérique et le Registre Foncier Urbain (RFU).

Notre mission est l'élaboration de la stratégie de mise en place de la plateforme "Horizon" (SIG interactif, adressage, RFU).

Pour assurer :
- l'unification des référentiels territoriaux (Géoportail, OTR, Cadastre) afin de garantir une "source unique" d'information.
- Un déploiement d'un système de numérotation métrique et pose de signalétique en acier émaillé.
- Gérer la formation des agents municipaux pour assurer une gestion autonome et pérenne des outils numériques.

Nous souhaitons mettre en avant :
- Les Système d'Information Géographique (SIG)
- Clarifier les Registre Foncier Urbain (RFU)
- Accompagner la Gouvernance Locale & Décentralisation

A travers : 
- l'ingénierie Topographique
- La Fiscalité Locale & l'optimisation des Recettes
- L'accompagnement à l'aménagement Urbain, villes recentrer sur nos réalités

Name: Empower Queen
Description:
About
Empower Queen envisions a world where every woman is not just a beneficiary of empowerment but a catalyst for positive change, breaking barriers and reshaping the future for generations to come.

Mission:
Empower Queen is dedicated to fostering positive change in the lives of women globally by focusing on the key pillars of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Our mission is to empower women through initiatives that align with SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being), SDG 4 (Quality Education), and SDG 5 (Gender Equality).

Vision:

Name: FSP Stadtplanung
Description:
Wir sind ein etabliertes Stadtplanungsbüro im Herzen von Freiburg im Breisgau. Unser engagiertes Team besteht inzwischen aus 24 Personen, darunter Stadtplaner/innen und -entwickler/innen sowie Mitarbeiter/innen im Bereich Organisation. Wir setzen uns leidenschaftlich für die Gestaltung lebenswerter und nachhaltiger Städte und Gemeinden ein.

Unser Büro zeichnet sich durch umfassende Expertise in allen Bereichen der Stadtplanung aus. Von der städtebaulichen Konzeption über die Quartiersentwicklung, bis hin zur Bauleitplanung - wir bieten maßgeschneiderte Lösungen für die vielfältigen Herausforderungen unserer Gemeinden und Städte.

Name: Licuadora Gestora
Description:
We are a collective of architects, urban planners, cultural managers, designers, and artists dedicated to developing cultural and urban innovation projects from a gender perspective.

Since 2017, we have been studying the city through feminist approaches and exploring the relationship between bodies and territory. We have carried out mapping exercises and walking tours with local residents, architects, artists, cultural managers, migrants, mothers, and adolescents in various cities across Ecuador.

We promote projects and training programs that incorporate art, mediation, and pedagogy as tools for researching and intervening in urban spaces.

We are interested in analyzing the impact of artistic practices on urban dynamics and local governance while actively engaging in and transforming public and heritage spaces. To this end, we have developed several participatory action-research methodologies to expand perspectives and narratives about territories.

Additionally, through these methodologies, we have collaborated with cultural actors to recover intangible heritage and the social memory of both urban and rural parishes in the city of Quito.

Furthermore, we create and manage platforms for dialogue and exchange, fostering discussions on architecture, the city, gender, art, culture, and heritage.

We have worked with various organizations and public institutions, including the International Organization for Migration, Arte Actual-FLACSO, the University of the Arts, and the Secretariat of Culture of the Municipality of Quito.

Name: X.BLOCK
Description:

Name: Wirtschaftsförderung der Stadt Büren
Description:
WIRTSCHAFTSFÖRDERUNG IN BÜREN

Die Wirtschaftsförderung der Stadt Büren ist darauf ausgerichtet, die wirtschaftliche Entwicklung der Stadt zu unterstützen und zu fördern. Das heißt, wir helfen bestehenden Unternehmen bei ihrer Weiterentwicklung und unterstützen neue Unternehmen bei der Ansiedlung in Büren.

AUFGABEN UND ZIELE
Wir fördern die Vernetzung von Unternehmen und Institutionen, um Synergien zu schaffen und Kooperationen zu ermöglichen, und wir arbeiten eng mit Kammern, Verbänden und politischen Institutionen zusammen, um Ihre Interessen zu vertreten und Sie über lokale und regionale Entwicklungen zu informieren.

DIE BELEBUNG DER INNENSTADT
Die Bürener Innenstadt soll gestärkt werden und dem Anspruch an eine zeitgerechte Zentrenentwicklung gerecht werden. Hierzu erhält die Stadt Büren rund 250.000 Euro an Fördermitteln aus dem Landesprogramm „Zukunft Innenstadt Nordrhein-Westfalen“. Geplant ist unter anderem die Anmietung von leerstehenden Ladenlokalen im innerstädtischen Versorgungsbereich der Stadt Büren. Weiterhin soll auch die „Schaffung von Innenstadtqualität“ gefördert werden, was beispielsweise die Bereitstellung von Spielangeboten für Kinder oder die Anschaffung von generationengerechten Möblierungselementen einschließt.

WEITERE DATEN ZUM WIRTSCHAFTSSTANDORT
● 22.000 Einwohner
● TOP-Verkehrsinfrastruktur
● Attraktive Gewerbeflächen

NEHMEN SIE BEI INTERESSE GERNE KONTAKT ZU UNS AUF

Name: Back of the Yards Neighborhood Council
Description:
The Back of the Yards Neighborhood Council is a community organization whose mission is to enhance the general welfare of all residents, organizations, and businesses in our service area. We accomplish this by integrating social service and economic development programs into a single community-based intermediary organization, the BYNC leads a holistic approach to the social problems that have plagued the Back of the Yards neighborhood and surrounding communities.

We have a variety of activities, programs, and services that are provided to our constituents. These programs include a Mexican Folklore Dance Troupe, Computer Technology Center (CTC), Industrial Retention activities, Special Service Area (SSA) management, senior support services, maintaining a high level of participation with local CAPS program, retail development, employment placement assistance, and a variety of other services that assist the participants. Building the future of consenting

ALGIM Excellence in Innovation Award

It’s just more proof of our ongoing dedication to promoting regulatory excellence in New Zealand.

Name: Mujawarah for Life and Development
Description:
Who We Are
Mujawarah is An Arabic term that is most closely related to the term’s "neighbourhood" or "neighbours," and that embodies the concept of close proximity and mutual support among people in an educational and participatory context. It fosters opportunities to invest in knowledge, giving, and community values within a cooperative and developmental environment, enabling individuals to enhance their skills, expand their knowledge, and positively impact their lives within their local communities.

Mujawarah for Life and Development is a development-focused organisation registered as a Community Interest Company in the United Kingdom under registration number 16329583. It offers innovative and transformative learning pathways for individuals and communities within a lifelong learning framework, leveraging best educational and participatory practices to engage with people. Mujawarah collaborates with local community partners to develop and support cooperative educational, cultural, social, and economic programmes that are responsive to community needs in various circumstances and crises. The organisation operates based on a philosophy of community giving and the utilisation of local resources to contribute to improving living conditions for all members of society, ensuring the right to education for all, and supporting sustainable and inclusive development.
Main Workstreams: 
- To empower individuals by providing lifelong learning opportunities that enhance their personal and professional capacities
- To economically empower individuals and communities through sustainable and cooperative productivity opportunities.
- To enhance community Organising and foster a culture of community campaigns and initiatives.

Name: BizWish LLC.
Description:
BizWish is an organization that transforms business wish ideas, needs and improvement suggestions into reality. It starts with our patented platform (Economic Development and Collaboration System - Patent # 11334850) created for communities and the general public to express and exchange business ideas and recommendations. Those ideas are directed towards business professionals in effort to foster change, development and create economic growth for cities and rural areas. Those business professionals (BizWish Associates) are city, county & state leaders, commercial real estate professionals, investors, lenders, entrepreneurs, financial institutions, etc. who consume those ideas for valuation and conceivably for development.

BizWish users can propose new or model existing businesses as a means of gathering and evaluating community support and interest levels. Real estate professionals can propose business ideas as a means of leveraging community interest for marketing and valuation purposes. The data collected is assessed and then used for business expansion and location determination. The voting feature provides community collaboration and validation.  It permits users with the opportunity to vote and comment on those proposals and other business ideas.

Business owners and entrepreneurs can also advertise requests for financing in order to find low-cost, negotiated funding opportunities. This transformative business model for financing allows business owners and entrepreneurs single or mixed funding request options such as; loans, crowdfunding & donation requests, bank & brokerage, profit sharing, equity sharing and royalty offerings of their business. This revolutionary method aids in reducing or eliminating painstaking efforts such as; calls to multiple financial and lending institutions for funding.  It provides competitive, negotiated low-cost business funding opportunities to find optimal funding options, as well as, investment opportunities.

Name: კომლი • Komli
Description:
“Komli” is a co-creation space for local community members, freelancers, researchers, artists volunteers, and organizations from around the globe.
The idea of a Co-creation space is based on the principles of empathy and co-living. It offers a space for thinking, Observing, and Researching, which translates into new knowledge that is beneficial for the wider community.
Our today’s society, which has become increasingly individualistic and focused on the individual as opposed to the collective, this co-creation space offers an opportunity for reshaping and shifting our gaze from individual care to collaborative care, and from human centered-care to environment-centered care.
Co-creation space is an alive and dynamic entity, which aims to create, in partnership with locals and guests, a framework-free learning practice while offering its services to the local community that might have less access to learning opportunities and broader community services. In this co-creation space, guests understand the principle of “togetherness within a community”.
To summarize our idea, the co-creation space is open for the members of the local community, young people, researchers, artists, and organizations that want to utilize it for meetings, consultations and putting their ideas into practice.

Utöver projektledning av bygg- och detaljplaneprojekt jobbar vi med hjälp och rådgivning till företag och privata fastighetsägare som önskar utveckla fastigheter. Vi hjälper och stöttar er gärna under en kommunal detaljplaneprocess eller med framtagande av bygglovshandlingar. 

Äger du en fastighet som du önskar utveckla eller ett projekt du behöver hjälp med? Vi hjälper gärna dig att titta på förutsättningarna för din fastighet och driva ditt projekt i mål.

Our vision is a world that champions the wellbeing of all humanity over our differences. 

Untitled Discourse is a Mbowa Holdings non-profit initiative that serves its overarching mission to “build lasting communities of value”–Through recurring group discussions where people engage in candid unscripted conversations about important matters affecting our shared human experiences.  

In a world of fleeting connections and digital distractions, Untitled Discourse brings people together to rediscover humanity’s oldest traditions–storytelling and sharing–the magic of real talk and the profound impact of shared circumstances, because when we begin to know, understand and appreciate each other’s experiences, we can forge healthy lasting communities of value. We can harness the power of humanity in us and make the world a better place.

Currently, Untitled Discourse sessions take place on the last Saturday of every month in the American Space inside the Manzini Public Library. They are led by guest moderators–individuals with great stories to share or life changing ideas, industry insights, unconventional perspectives, etc. 

Anyone interested in guest-moderating an Untitled Discourse event can email the topic they are inspired to lead in a perceptive and fruitful dialogue to untitled@mbowa.com. If their submission is selected, management will take care of the organization and marketing of the event. Topics cover a range of themes and socio-economic aspects of society and social wellbeing. At the event coffee or tea may be served as we dive into vibrant conversations with peers where we challenge perspectives, broaden horizons, and harness the deep connections and realities that bind us to do good. 

Contact Person: Zito Mbowa, zito@mbowa.com ; +268 76202158

Name: Westchester Municipal Planning Federation
Description:
The Westchester Municipal Planning Federation (WMPF) is a voluntary association of officials representing the county’s 45 municipal governments, zoning and planning boards and land use consultants including lawyers, engineers, architects and planners. Authorized under New York’s State General Municipal Law in 1962, and governed by a 15-member Board of Directors, the organization’s purpose is to “create an effective and permanent countywide organization for the exchange of information on matters of planning interest.”

WMPF works closely with the Westchester County Department of Planning to arrange programs of interest to the Westchester planning community. These programs include an annual land use training institute, annual planning and zoning board seminars, and a variety of breakfast, luncheon and evening programs on topical planning issues. In addition, WMPF hosts an annual planning achievement awards dinner and offers a scholarship to an undergraduate or graduate student seeking a degree in planning or a closely-related field, such as landscape architecture, architecture and public administration.

Name: Strong Towns Nanaimo
Description:
Strong Towns Nanaimo advocates for a new approach to urban planning and development that is financially resilient, sustainable, and equitable. We are a grassroots organization that is not affiliated with the City of Nanaimo. 

Strong Towns are critical to the sustainable development of cities and towns. They represent a way of thinking about urban planning and development that prioritizes the needs of the community, rather than those of developers or other stakeholders. Strong Towns are characterized by a number of important features, including productive land use, wise infrastructure investment, incremental building, people-focused planning, and bottom-up decision-making. The Strong Towns movement represents a new way of thinking about urban planning and development that prioritizes the needs of the community, rather than those of developers or other stakeholders. A Strong Town is characterized by a number of important features, including productive land use, wise infrastructure investment, incremental building, people-focused planning, and bottom-up decision-making.

By prioritizing the needs of people over the needs of cars or other infrastructure, Strong Towns are able to create communities that are more inclusive and accessible to everyone, regardless of income or ability. This is especially important in today’s increasingly unequal society, where many people are struggling to make ends meet and access essential services.

Name: SlowDown Interreg Europe
Description:
Tourism is one of the most important drivers of many economies in Europe, providing 10,3% of the EU’s GDP and employing 27 million EU citizens. In the past decades many EU, national and regional initiatives have been called to life to support the development of tourism, thereby boosting regional development.

However, development efforts affected European locations on different levels: some suffer from overtourism and tourism-triggered environmental and social issues, while other regions are not able to exploit their tourism potentials and opportunities.

Slow tourism is an approach to travel that emphasises a slower, more immersive, and sustainable experience. It is a response to the fast-paced and superficial nature of modern tourism, aiming to create a deeper connection with the destination, its culture, and its people.

This is the form of tourism SLOWDOWN partners wish to promote, exploring how local/regional policies can best address the development needs of this novel and underexploited sector.

Partners believe that making the promotion of slow tourism a policy priority in their regions would be the most comprehensive and effective way for the growth of this segment. Therefore they joined forces to improve their development, planning and funding policies so that these better support slow tourism initiatives, thereby contributing to sustainable local/regional development as well. This will be done by an intensive inter- and intra-regional learning process and exchange of experience, enhancing the capacities of partner institutions and leading to policy improvement. Partners will immensely involve relevant stakeholders into this process, whose input and insight are key to meaningful policy enhancement.

The consortium includes 9 partners from 8 EU countries, as well as 2 Associated Policy Authorities, representing various policy-implementing levels and also a wide variety of regional specificities. The EoE will be further enhanced by an Advisory Partner.

Name: Solidaria Studio
Description:
We’re a creative urbanism practice with a focus on inclusion and participation.

If you want to do more to include a wider variety of people, especially women and girls, in the way you plan, design and manage public spaces and programs, we’d like to show you how fun and easy it can be.

Inclusion Workshops
Briefings for leaders and staff to learn more about including girls and women, understand the tools available and build their inclusion capabilities and systems.

Participation Processes
Exploratory workshops and co-creation processes to discover what girls and women need from places and programs in their communities, and meaningfully include them in planning, design and use.

Public Space Experience Studies
International best practice methods and novel techniques for gathering human experiences of place, translated into locally generated knowledge and ideas for inclusive, evidence-based planning, design and place-making.

Inclusion Audits + Impact Assessments
Critical but kind analysis of how policies, programs and places will meet different people’s needs, with recommendations and support to make your work more equitable.

Creative Urban Experiences
Extraordinary and delightful experiences, events and projects which connect people and generate meaningful dialogues about making places healthier, safer, fairer, more social, and more joyful.

Workshops + Presentations
Customised workshops, walkshops and presentations to inspire, inform and entertain audiences at conferences and events, from sensory memory experiments, to gender mainstreaming in mobility planning, to place-love via urban wandering.

Name: StreetCo - Urban Planning | Walkable Urbanism
Description:
Vision -

To work worldwide to create walkable, and accessible neighbourhoods, anchored by high-quality, timeless design. We are dedicated to enabling the development of beautiful places that promote community engagement, sustainability, and well-being—places where people not only live but thrive and feel a sense of belonging.

Our Commitment to You

Lean In: Embrace creativity, innovation, and fresh ideas. We believe that open-mindedness drives progress.

Listen First: We prioritise understanding by starting with active listening, ensuring we approach every conversation without preconceived notions.

Show Respect: We value every individual and commit to treating everyone with dignity and respect, fostering an inclusive environment.

Be Honest: Transparency is key. We promise to provide honest advice and maintain openness in all our interactions.

Name: Placemaking Austria
Description:
Placemaking Austria is an open platform and network dedicated to advancing placemaking as a driver of inclusive, vibrant, and people-centred communities across Austria. We bring together residents, designers, municipalities, researchers, planners and changemakers who share a passion for transforming public spaces into places where people feel welcome, connected, and cherished.

Our mission is to put people at the heart of urban life. Grounded in participation, collaboration, local leadership, creative design, and integrity, we see placemaking as more than better urban design. It is a collaborative process that strengthens social and cultural identity, supports local innovation, and allows places to evolve with and for their communities.

Placemaking Austria is part of the wider Placemaking Europe movement and believes in open knowledge and collaboration. We host events, share tools and stories, and build cross-sector partnerships through workshops, urban labs, and professional exchange. Our community is open to everyone.

Why does placemaking matter? Because good public spaces build community and belonging, improve quality of life, support local economies, foster civic engagement, and promote more sustainable, walkable, and resilient cities. In Austria and beyond, placemaking helps turn streets, squares, and neighbourhoods into living places that people truly care for.

Description: Nos encargamos de crear soluciones y proyectos inmobiliarios factibles, que contribuyan al mejoramiento de la zona, así como un valor agregado al cliente brindando soluciones y productos en tiempo y forma con una calidad de excelencia, del mismo modo generar un rendimiento adecuado con el fin de satisfacer a nuestros inversionistas y generar utilidad para el continuo desarrollo y creación de nuevos proyectos en distintos puntos estratégicos para continuar contribuyendo a la sociedad.

Description: D A COOP is a research and design office that takes a problem-focused approach to create culturally nuanced urban solutions. Name: Lyra nord
Description:
Lyra nord är ett medarbetarägt bolag som har stor erfarenhet av den fysiska planeringens samtliga nivåer och skeden. Vi arbetar i alla led; från strategisk planering, konceptutveckling till detaljplaner och genomförande samt uppföljning, rådgivning och utvärdering. För privata aktörer kan vi utgöra en brygga i kontakt med myndigheter och kommuner för att skapa ett effektivt samarbete. 

Vi har ett brett nätverk av specialister och kontakter som vi samarbetar med för att kunna erbjuda våra kunder en komplett lösning utifrån projektets behov. Vår erfarenhet från branschen har gett oss en stor kunskap i hur vi skapar värde i den fysiska miljön både för användare och förvaltare. Vi ser stor nytta i att kunna erbjuda våra kunder de bästa och mest effektiva experterna oberoende av företagsgränser och geografi. 

Vi har gedigen kunskap om den kommunala planprocessen såväl som god insikt i projektekonomi. Med vår erfarenhet, vårt driv och vår samarbetsförmåga hjälper vi er att navigera i den kommunala planeringsprocessen samtidigt som vi förmedlar och fokuserar på projektekonomin utifrån effektivitet och genomförbarhet.

Name: Urban Heat Watch
Description:
Ένας κόμβος αναφοράς και παρακολούθησης δεδομένων αστικού πρασίνου. Περιλαμβάνει εκτεταμένη εργασία πεδίου, καταγραφές με drone, καταγραφή και αξιολόγηση Αστικών Οικοσυστημικών Υπηρεσιών, ερωτηματολόγια σχετικά με την επισκεψιμότητα και τις δραστηριότητες στους Χώρους Πρασίνου. Δοκιμάζονται καινοτόμες τεχνολογίες (αισθητήρες/μίνι μετεωρολογικοί σταθμοί, θερμικές κάμερες, GIS, RS κ.α.) και πρακτικές για την χαρτογράφηση του αισθήματος δυσφορίας στον καύσωνα, της τρωτότητας του ευάλωτου πληθυσμού στο φαινομένου της Αθήνας και των ανισοτήτων των διαφορετικών γειτονιών απέναντι στη ζέστη.

Υλοποιείται συμμετοχική Καμπάνια Χαρτογράφησης της Ζέστης στις τρωτές γειτονιές στην ΑΘΝ της Μητροπολιτικής περιοχής της Αθήνας. Τέλος, θα δημιουργηθεί ψηφιακό εργαλείο οπτικοποίησης και παρακολούθησης του αστικού πρασίνου και της τρωτότητας σε κλίμακα γειτονιάς, ενώ θα ενσωματωθούν και τα αποτελέσματα των χαρτογραφήσεων της ζέστης. Τα αποτελέσματα του έργου θα διασυνδεθούν με το «Παρατηρητήριο Κλιματικής Αλλαγής» της Περιφέρειας Αττικής για μέγιστη διάχυση και αξιοποίηση. 

*Το έργο "URBAN HEAT WATCH. Παρατηρητήριο Αστικού Πρασίνου και Κλιματικής Ανθεκτικότητας " χρηματοδοτείται από το Πράσινο Ταμείο στο πλαίσιο του Άξονα Προτεραιότητας 3: «Έρευνα και Εφαρμογή» του Χρηματοδοτικού Προγράμματος: «Φυσικό Περιβάλλον & Καινοτόμες Δράσεις 2023».

Name: Just Space Collective
Description:
WHO WE ARE

We are a women and minority owned small business leading the way for a human-centered and justice-driven approaches in planning.

OUR VALUES

We value co-producing knowledge and developing platforms and tools for evidence-based and inclusive policy making. We apply bottom-up perspectives, amplify the voices of communities, and activate spaces of collective ownership that support spatial justice.

We recognize the need for justice-centered planning where communities are actively engaged in the co-production of knowledge, where their lived experiences and expertise are at the heart of practice.

CO-DESIGN just and equitable spaces with us.

We guide you from ideation to execution. Together we can achieve inclusive and spatially just community practice for your organization and community needs.

Elaboramos estudos e propostas para a estruturação da mobilidade urbana e sistema viário em escala municipal e regional. Desenvolvemos ações e atividades visando à gestão territorial (urbana e rural) democrática, gerenciando a governança urbana constituída por habitantes, empresários, trabalhadores, entidades, acadêmicos, pesquisadores, movimentos sociais, órgãos públicos e toda a sociedade, de forma transparente, colaborativa e inclusiva, provendo a todos o direito à cidade.  Desenvolvemos também estudos de projetos urbanísticos, paisagísticos, de edificações, de sistema viário urbano e rural, e de patrimônio histórico, importantes para o Planejamento Urbano e Gestão Territorial. Por fim, trabalhamos para garantir a continuidade das políticas públicas urbanas de longo prazo com planos e projetos que antecipem problemas decorrentes do crescimento da cidade de Maringá.

Mit unserer Expertise und unserem Einsatz möchten wir nachhaltige Infrastrukturlösungen entwickeln, die den Bedürfnissen der Gemeinden und der Sportakteure gerecht werden.

Kommunen stehen häufig vor dem Problem, dass eine gesicherte Datengrundlage für die  tatsächlichen Bedarfe der Sportakteure und somit für eine nachhaltige Sportinfrastrukturplanung fehlt. Unser Unternehmen schließt diese Lücke durch umfassende Analysen und ermöglicht so fundierte Entscheidungen zur Verbesserung der Sportlandschaft in Ihrer Kommune.

Durch unsere wissenschaftlich fundierten Berechnungen Ihrer Sportinfrastruktur sind wir in der Lage, präzise Empfehlungen auszusprechen. So stellen wir sicher, dass die Bedürfnisse Ihrer Sportakteure bedarfsgerecht gedeckt und Ihre kommunalen Finanzmittel ökonomisch nachhaltig eingesetzt werden.
Vertrauen Sie auf unsere Expertise, um die optimale Entwicklung Ihrer Sporteinrichtungen zu fördern.

Jede Kommune ist einzigartig! Deswegen bieten wir Ihnen eine individuelle Beratung und entwickeln maßgeschneiderte Konzepte, um Ihre Herausforderungen im Bereich der Sportinfrastruktur zu meistern. Durch unsere gründlichen Analysen finden wir die passende Lösung, die genau auf Ihre Bedürfnisse abgestimmt ist.

Wir sind für Sie da!
Unser Team steht Ihnen auch nach Abschluss des Projektes zur Seite und hilft Ihnen bei aufkommenden Fragen während des Umsetzungsprozesses, damit Sie Ihre Ziele langfristig und nachhaltig erreichen.

Name: Rippling Nature
Description:
Our Rippling Nature Story: From Urban Innovation to Rural Revival
The bustling metropolis of Houston, driven by energy and innovation, is facing the same global challenges as countless other cities: food insecurity, environmental sustainability, and ensuring a thriving future for its residents. Yet, just a short drive away lies the rural town of Livingston, where economic opportunity and access to modern, sustainable practices are needed. These vastly different communities could hold the key to a blue green print addressing critical issues—if they could be connected.

Being Seers: A team of passionate individuals, fueled by data, analysis, and a belief in community-driven solutions believe problems of this scale require a new kind of Platform, a data-driven hub of Innovation they call AGINC. Powered by AI & fueled by collaboration, it goes beyond existing models. Data & Intel: AGINC goes to work: Public data, private partnerships, and sensor networks turn HTown SOUND into a living dataset. AI analyzes trends, optimizes systems, creating the KPIs to produce Roadmaps which will make this a replicable model.

But tools are nothing without action, which is where HTown SOUND a dynamic ideation & innovation ecosystem comes in...

Being Sages: Community Connection: HTown SOUND isn't just about technology. It's about engaging with Houston's diverse communities, becoming a hub for education, job training, and a space where social innovation meets environmental progress.

Being Shepherds: Urban Proving Ground (HTown SOUND)
Innovation Showcase: HTown SOUND becomes a vibrant incubator ecosystem on the Buffalo Bayou Trails— a blend of urban gardens, modular greenhouses, aquaculture, & more. It doesn't just produce food, it's a showcase of renewable energy, water purification— a model of sustainable urban living.

Beyond Houston: HTown SOUND, powered by a dynamic Innovation Engine features & connecting with researchers at Rice University, innovators at Greentown Labs, and Houston

Name: Pedestrian Pride Project
Description:
Step into a Safer, More Dignified Future with the Pedestrian Pride Project - Columbia, Missouri! 

At the Pedestrian Pride Project, we're on a mission to transform the way we walk, one step at a time. Our streets should be safe havens for everyone, especially those in marginalized communities. Here's how we're making a difference:

Pedestrian accidents and fatalities are more than just numbers. They're stories untold, lives interrupted. We're diving deep into the statistics, shedding light on the need for change.

We're not in this alone. By studying successful pedestrian advocacy projects worldwide, especially using social media, we're harnessing the power of collective wisdom to drive meaningful change in our community.

This is more than just a project; it's a movement. We're rallying stakeholders and community members alike to champion safer streets for all. Together, we can turn our sidewalks into pathways of pride and dignity.

Your voice matters. Through storytelling campaigns and community events, we're creating spaces for you to share your pedestrian experiences, driving empathy and understanding.

From social media campaigns to grassroots activations, we're leveraging the power of technology to mobilize support and ignite conversations that matter.

Name: Worthing Community Chest
Description:
Worthing Community Chest is a registered, local, independent charity (Charity number: 1098284) that enables local groups and charities to achieve community enrichment. 

Since 2001, Worthing Community Chest (WCC) has awarded almost £450,000 in grants to diverse and phenomenal local groups, who have made a huge impact in Worthing. To celebrate our communities, Worthing Community Chest are launching some Community Awards which take place on Thursday 23rd November 2023 at Rooms in the Guildbourne Centre. The awards have a special twist.

Our Vision
To be the “go-to” funder and partner of new and developing community groups in Worthing. Together we improve, transform and enrich lives.

Our mission
Worthing Community Chest is your local funder. We fund and nurture early-stage community groups and charities operating in Worthing through an effective and accessible small grants process. We use the UN Sustainable Development Goals, ask known as the Globals Gloss, as the frame work to inspire ideas and innovation. Please read our Global page and share which goals your work helps to meet. You can choose up to three goals.

Our Values

Together: We partner and collaborate with local groups and great people to do amazing things.
Enabler: Our grants enable the Worthing community to connect.
Accessible: We make the grants process as accessible and engaging as possible and talk our groups through the process. 
Global Local: Our funds are making magic in Worthing and also helping the UN SDGs (Global Goals) to meet their targets.
Diversity: Our groups are diverse and the projects and people we fund vary greatly.
Harmonious Communities: Enabling innovation opportunities to unite communities, improving wellbeing and inclusion.

The landscape
We are changing because our staff and communities need resilience not more efficiency. Extraordinary things can happen when people are able to come together and take part, as we saw during the height of the coronavirus pandemic.

Name: Triparty  Development
Description:
Future real estate value in University Circle will be driven less by conventional fundamentals and more by transportation infrastructure — particularly light-rail, multimodal connectivity, and station-oriented development.

University Circle already functions as Cleveland’s most important institutional district, anchored by CWRU, Cleveland Clinic, and an expanding Innovation District. What now promises to change the equation is transit--specifically light-rail--enabling higher density, reduced reliance on cars, and stronger regional connectivity. 

GCRTA’s system-wide investments — including new rolling stock and track standardization now underway — point to long-term rail modernization. When combined with the transit alternatives and ongoing planning studies outlined 30 years ago, this reinforces the case that University Circle will ultimately be served by enhanced light-rail connectivity and future station infrastructure.

The Carnegie Avenue / Stokes corridor stands out as a logical multimodal gateway into the Circle. Its location and proximity to major Circle institutions make it well-suited for transit-oriented development, including higher-density residential, medical office, research, and mixed-use formats that align with institutional growth and evolving urban mobility patterns.

Properly positioned TOD at this gateway would not simply add density — it would improve access, walkability, and long-term resilience for University Circle as a whole.

#TransitOrientedDevelopment #LightRail #UniversityCircle #MixedUse #Multimodal 

Location matters. As a potential transit-oriented development, parcels in this corridor could support a significant mixed-use project within a growing Innovation District… 
• 5-minute walk to Case Quad and the new ISEB (opening late 2026)
• 6-minute walk to Cleveland Clinic Main Campus
• 8-14-minute walk to the Cleveland Clinic Ne
#Multifamily Highrise   #Mixeduse Development   #Universitycircle Real-estate

Name: Landra Developments Limited
Description:
Landra Developments promotes residential and commercial land through the planning system, at our expense and risk, to gain the best possible planning permissions for our landowner clients.

Unlike a conventional housebuilder option agreement, where the land is sold to a single option holder at a “negotiated” value which may be significantly less than the true market value, Landra Developments offers land with planning permission to the open market, thereby ensuring that optimum price is achieved for our landowners.

Name: Falls City EDGE
Description:
Falls City is the Midwest community strategically positioned and building for the future.  You'll find businesses located here benefit from our:

Strategic central U.S. location, ideally situated in southeast Nebraska to serve the Nebraska, Missouri and Kansas tri-state market area
Access to transcontinental interstates/U.S. highways and rail systems
Advanced Fiber-to-the-Home telecommunications network and utility infrastructure with capacity to meet increased growth demands
Progressive, pro-business climate and performance-based incentives
Job-ready workforce and customized, flexible training assistance
Diverse business and employment base, including manufacturing, healthcare, education and government
Competitively priced available sites and buildings
Quality of living attractive to workers and families
Falls City Economic Development and Growth Enterprise (EDGE) is a 501(C)6 nonprofit, private corporation whose members are private and public investors.  Our mission is to encourage economic development and growth and improve the business conditions of the greater Falls City/Richardson County area.

EDGE is your first stop to exploring business investment opportunities in Falls City.  We provide services to help existing businesses grow and expand, to new businesses exploring location opportunities in the area and to entrepreneurs starting-up new business ventures.  Learn more about the advantages of doing business in Falls City and Richardson County by exploring our website.  You'll find we are strategically positioned for business growth and development and building for the future.

Name: MapX
Description:
MapX erbjuder en innovativ digital plattform för medborgardialoger, vilket underlättar kommunikation mellan stadens invånare och beslutsfattare. 

Genom att möjliggöra effektiva dialoger och insamling av viktig data, bidrar MapX till att forma städer som inkluderar alla. Plattformen kombinerar kartbaserade funktioner med användarvänliga verktyg för att skapa, genomföra och analysera medborgardialoger, vilket främjar socialt hållbar planering och demokratiskt engagemang.

Name: Platform i-Tree Nederland
Description:
Platform i-Tree Nederland stimuleert het werken met i-Tree in Nederland. Het platform heeft daarbij drie doelstellingen:

1. Het borgen en bevorderen van het op de juiste wijze toepassen van i-Tree in Nederland
2. Uitwisselen van kennis en ervaring tussen de betrokken adviesbureaus over ecosysteemdiensten in het algemeen en i-Tree in het bijzonder. 
3. Het delen van kennis over i-Tree en ecosysteemdiensten met de markt. 

Platform i-Tree Nederland bestaat uit 4 adviesbureaus; idverde Bomendienst, Pius Floris Boomverzorging, Cobra Groeninzicht en Terra Nostra.

Name: OLA Planners & Colleagues, LLC
Description:
At OLA Planners & Colleagues, we take pride in being a professional service provider specializing in municipal and community planning, federal funds management, and project management. Founded in October 2022 and established under the Department of State of Puerto Rico, our company is committed to delivering high-quality services to our clients.

Our focus lies in providing strategic and personalized solutions for each client. We have an experienced and qualified team that encompasses a wide range of skills and knowledge in the field of planning. We are dedicated to serving diverse communities, state institutions, and municipalities with the aim of driving sustainable and equitable development.

At OLA Planners & Colleagues, we strive to make a difference through a combination of experience, professionalism, and a customer-centric approach. We work closely with our clients to understand their unique needs and offer tailored solutions to ensure successful outcomes.

Our track record demonstrates our commitment to excellence and integrity in every project we undertake. We are dedicated to continuing as a trusted partner for those seeking effective solutions in the field of planning.

We are delighted to collaborate with you and be your reliable business partner in achieving success in your planning projects. Welcome to OLA Planners & Colleagues!

Mission: Provide personalized attention to municipal and state clients, communities, and corporate customers. Deliver high-quality services by integrating our team as facilitators, utilizing our knowledge, friendly approach, and expertise. We optimize our clients resources to deliver quality products within the required timeframe.

Vision: Establish ourselves as the premier woman-owned company nationwide, leading the planning to transform our surroundings into empowered communities, resilient municipalities, and businesses committed to excellence.

Name: TractorBeam
Description: TractorBeam is developing a grassroots, open social innovation platform supporting a rural-led, solutions to rural America's complex social & economic challenges.

Name: Trondhjems Bys Vel
Description: Selskabet for Trondhjems Bys Vel er byens velforening med formål å være til «Gavn for og Forskjønnelse av Trondhjems By». Foreningen skal være uavhengig og balansert og arbeide for at Trondheim skal være en god by å bo i. Direksjonen som i dag leder Selskabet har prioritert å arbeide med følgende områder: Byutvikling, arkitektur/forskjønnelse, kulturhistorie og sosiale- og allmennkulturelle tiltak.

Name: Community Gardens Australia
Description:
Community Gardens Australia (CGA) is a not- for-profit, volunteer-run organisation that exists to support, provide resources to, network and advocate for community gardening activities in Australia.

Our vision is to see connected, nourishing, accessible and resilient community gardens for all Australians and our mission is to improve the health and wellbeing of Australians by providing networking opportunities, education, resources, advice and advocacy to support community gardening activities.

Name: CambiaMo S.p.A.
Description: CambiaMo S.p.A. è la Società di Trasformazione Urbana costituita fra Comune di Modena e ACER della Provincia di Modena

Name: Rákosrendező Projekt
Description:
A Rákosrendező Projekt célja, hogy bemutassa és átláthatóvá tegye a Rákosrendező térség átalakulásával kapcsolatos folyamatokat. 

Oldalunk a projekt hivatalos szakmai kommunikációs felülete, ahol: 
- tájékoztatunk a fejlesztés aktuális eseményeiről, 
- megosztjuk a várható szakmai eseményeken történő vagy média megjelenéseinket, publikációinkat, 
- bemutatjuk a projekt hátterét, folyamatait és eredményeit, 
- támogatjuk az átlátható, hiteles és hosszú távú szakmai párbeszédet. 

A célunk, hogy a projekt előrehaladását minden érintett – szakmai partnerek, önkormányzatok, várostervezők és érdeklődők – nyomon tudja követni, és megismerje a fejlesztés alapelveit. 

///
The aim of the Rákosrendező Project is to present and make transparent the processes related to the transformation of the Rákosrendező area.

Our page serves as the official professional communication platform of the project, where we:

- provide information on current developments,
- share our upcoming professional appearances, media features, and publications,
- present the background, processes, and outcomes of the project,
- support transparent, credible, and long-term professional dialogue.

Our goal is to ensure that all stakeholders – professional partners, municipalities, urban planners, and interested parties – can follow the progress of the project and become familiar with the guiding principles of the development.

Name: Villa
Description: O projeto Villa que é um Think Tank com foco em analisar, propor e influenciar conversas e projetos que potencializam o impacto da democracia para os ecossistemas de negócio, de inovação e da tecnologia.

Name: Ignite Youth Africa
Description:
Ignite Youth Africa (IYA) is a skills development/training and research organisation that has a clear approach in propelling organisations, both public and private to achieve their strategic plans. This is done or achieved through our various activities/services not limited to startegic designed programmes, innovations, skills training, research and concept designs.

IYA prides its self with the vast of industry expects in community development, communications, youth developments that enables us to conduct quality reserch and programmes that are impactful to both communities, youth, organisations and the economy of our country through the implemantation of strategic designed programmes that serves the need of our partners.

Description: Build UP is an activism-forward urban planning club dedicated to creating a sustainable, resilient, and inclusive Chapel Hill through community engagement and connection.

Name: Reidun Konsult
Description:
Reidun erbjuder erfarna och kompetenta ledare inom stadsbyggnad. Våra konsulter har lång erfarenhet av såväl kommunala som privata utvecklingsprojekt och tillhör de främsta inom området.
