Vi bygger inte bara en hållbar stad utan även goda relationer och långsiktiga samarbeten.

Name: Timisoara Life&Bytes
Description:
Timisoara Life&Bytes is a grass-roots platform for regional ecosystem development at the convergence of life science and information technologies, through cross-sectoral networking, facilitation of national and international collaborations, biotech entrepreneurship support and talent discovery and development.

Initiated as a BioCoderDojo Timisoara project for high school non-formal education, funded by local government bodies, Timisoara Life&Bytes expanded to other categories of individual and institutional stakeholders, becoming a wide ecosystem development platform, organizing or contributing to organizing networking events, workshops, hackathons, business incubation programs, connecting the local community with major regional and global initiatives aiming to transform engineered biology in one of the most important tools and approaches for building a better, cleaner, more efficient future from an individual to a societal and planetary scale. 

We personally act and work towards atractng others, involved life science, programming, entrepreners, business development practitioners, creators, educators, communicators with a complete trust in our capacity to build new smart specializations for Timisoara's economy, high growth potential startups and lead as a city in the quest for human and planetary health!

Join us, and let's grow together!

Name: ADICOR - Appui au Développement intègre des Communautés Rurales
Midcoast Council of Governments
Description: Midcoast Council of Governments (MCOG) is a nonprofit, municipally-driven regional planning and economic development district encompassing 66 communities in the Midcoast region. We receive funding from the local municipalities (dues), state, and federal governments to assist communities with their planning and economic development needs. MCOG evolved out of Midcoast regional planning commission, MCEDD, and the Bath-Brunswick-Topsham  Development Corporation serving Midcoast Maine since the 1990s.

Name: GORO Mountain Resort
Description:
GORO MOUNTAIN RESORT IS UKRAINE'S FIRST ALL-SEASON MOUNTAIN RESORT WITH A WORLD-CLASS HOLISTIC CONCEPT AND THE BROADEST PALETTE OF IMPRESSIONS, SERVICES AND ATTRACTIONS.

Location: GORO is 650 meters above sea level in the LVIV Carpathians, near Volosyanka and Verkhnya Rozhanka villages, within the Slavsko community of Lviv region.

1200 ha of the resort's total area
360 ha for mountain and ski infrastructure
800+ ha hotel, commercial, and recreational infrastructure

15 years of project timeline (in three phases)

SKI
360 hectares  for mountain and ski infrastructure
345 ha of snowmaking coverage
41 ski trails
75 km of ski runs
25,000 skiers capacity
Welcome and Mountain centres
2.8 km, the first and the longest gondola cable car in Ukraine 
3.8 km, the longest ski run in Ukraine

HOTELS 
800+ha for hotel, commercial, and recreational infrastructure
25 hotels
5150 rooms
13 000 guests per day

ENTERTAINMENT 
The all-season palette of attractions and services for any weather: summer | winter | rain

The construction of the Welcome Center and the lower station of the gondola cable car has already begun.

The first phase of GORO, with a total area of 127 hectares, already started in October 2024 near the village of Volosianka, located 5 km from Slavsko, Lviv region. In addition, there will be 10 and 13-km-long ski slopes with 50 hectares of snow and two modern chair lifts. The 20-hectare first phase of the resort will include five hotel complexes with 1,100+ rooms, recreational infrastructure with SPA, swimming pools, restaurants, and children's and business areas. The first phase is scheduled to be completed in 2028-2029.
GORO Mountain Resort is part of OKKO Group, one of Ukraine's largest business groups and investors. Forbes Ukraine ranked it #7 among Ukraine's largest private companies.

Name: National Union of Architects of Ukraine/Національна Спілка Архітекторів України
Description:
Національна спілка архітекторів України (НСАУ) – всеукраїнська незалежна самоврядна організація у сфері архітектури, заснована у 1918 році. З 1998 році ухвалою Кабінету Міністрів має статус національної. З 1993 НСАУ є членом Міжнародного Союзу Архітекторів, а з квітня 2022 - асоційованим членим Архітектурної Ради Європи.

Ми створюємо сприятливі умови для творчої реалізації архітекторів та створення якісної архітектури. Моніторимо та беремо участь у формуванні містобудівної політики та нормативної бази у сфері архітектури. Ведемо міжнародну діяльність для популяризації України та укрїанської архітектури у світі.   

Понад 5600 архітекторів та фахівців у сфері архітектури є нашими членами. Осередки НСАУ діють у всіх областях України. 

Структурним підрозділом НСАУ є Архітектурна Палата - професійне об'єднання сертифікованих архітекторів.

Name: Shenzhen Chunanjia Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd.

Welcome to Chunanjia, the leading manufacturer of turnstile gates and boom barrier. At Chunanjia, we understand the critical importance of reliable access control in today’s fast-paced world. That’s why we dedicate ourselves to crafting solutions that not only meet but exceed the expectations of modern businesses and public spaces. Our products are designed with precision, incorporating cutting-edge technology to deliver performance that you can trust.

Join us on our journey as we continue to set the standard in the access control industry, providing solutions that are as robust as they are innovative. Connect with Chunanjia today and experience the future of secure access.

The group aims to promote the analysis of the built environment and to raise awareness of approaches and methodologies to address the wider role of buildings archaeology with other professionals in built heritage sector.

Description:
TBA Urban is a dynamic advisory group specialising in property consultancy, development management services and urban planning.

Name: Italian Garden srl
Description:

Nel campo dell'urban design, Italian Garden si distingue per la capacità di progettare spazi armoniosi che integrano estetica e funzionalità, rispondendo alle esigenze della comunità e valorizzando il contesto urbano circostante. Ogni progetto è pensato per ottimizzare il benessere dei suoi utilizzatori, favorendo la sostenibilità e la creazione di aree verdi e sportive.

Playground e pavimentazioni in gomma colata sono i veri punti di forza dell'azienda. I playground realizzati da Italian Garden sono spazi sicuri, creativi e adatti a tutte le età, progettati per stimolare il gioco e l'apprendimento. Grazie all'uso di pavimentazioni in gomma colata, ogni area di gioco è protetta da impatti, garantendo massima sicurezza per i bambini e durabilità nel tempo.

Description:
CODE is an independent planning consultancy providing advice to promoters, developers and landowners throughout the south-east and eastern regions of England across a range of development sectors.

Our strength is in our ability to devise planning strategies which are solutions-focused, proactive and based on solid and up-to-date knowledge, with the enthusiasm and tenacity to deliver the client’s objectives in the most efficient manner.

Due to our continued growth and increasing workload we are looking to expand our team further with motivated individuals keen to develop their careers and rise to new challenges.

Associate Planner
Senior Planner
Graduate Planner

Successful candidates would be expected to have a robust knowledge of planning issues, mechanisms and guidance commensurate with the length of their experience and qualification and most importantly an enthusiasm to deliver and develop their skills and reputations. We have no pre-conceived limitations on the detailed nature or length of your existing experience. We are more interested in identifying the potential and how best to jointly develop it.

At CODE we work in partnership with team members to hone each individual’s professional and personal skills and develop careers in a supportive team environment. We are sufficiently large and experienced to attract an exciting range of instructions from new and repeat clients but small enough to focus on opportunities to individually tailor career growth.

Please send your CV and covering letter to Sarah Burch at sarahburch@codedp.co.uk. If, in the meantime, you wish to discuss any aspect of the roles then please do not hesitate to contact Mike Carpenter initially at mikecarpenter@codedp.co.uk

Name: Zuko - Reclaim the streets
Description:
Zuko is a free rental service for mobile urban furniture. It allows users to utilize public spaces more easily and flexibly according to their needs. 

Zuko creates more togetherness and sojourn quality in our neighborhoods - under the motto: “reclaim the streets”.

Name: SEAT Ecopôle du Val d'Allier
Description: L’Ecopôle du Val d’Allier est un site d’environ 140 ha situé sur les communes de Pérignat-ès-Allier et de la Roche Noire, aux portes de l’agglomération Clermontoise. Composé d’anciennes gravières, d’importants travaux écologiques réalisés en partenariat avec la LPO Auvergne ont permis de favoriser le retour de la biodiversité sur cet ancien site industriel. Ce site emblématique de la transition écologique a pour vocation de devenir un lieu pédagogique conciliant activités humaines et protection de l’environnement.

Name: Black Community Board Minnesota
Description:
Vision: The Black Community Board envisions a world where Black Americans are a liberated people with principled ambassadors and highly effective advocates throughout the State and Nation - free of suppressed potential and experiencing freedom of thought, feelings, expression, and movement.

Mission: Our mission is to move the Black community toward a liberated experience by working to eliminate racial disparities in the quality of life by providing engagement, Information, and education resources and services in an effort to close gaps in access, experiences, and connections to transformative opportunities.

Theory of Change: Disparities present in the Black community are indicators of suppressed potential. Disparities can be eliminated by cultivating collective capacity through individual development. 

This will close gaps in exposure, knowledge, and cultural competence. The elimination of racialized disparities removes the suppression of potential and creates pathways to a liberated experience.

Name: Urban Attitudes Lab
Description:
The Urban Attitudes Lab studies the land use, environmental, and psychological dimensions of urban planning, design, and public policy practice.  Through the use of sophisticated data acquisition tools, sentiment analysis, qualitative research, eye-tracking technology, and conventional psychological tools, the UAL is advancing knowledge and developing new tools to improve planning and policy.  Three key books have been published by the UAL: Urban Social Listening: Potential and Pitfalls for Using Microblogging Data in Studying Cities (Palgrave/Springer) and Cognitive Architecture: Designing for How We Respond to the Built Environment (Routledge), and Urban Experience and Design: Contemporary Perspectives on Improving the Public Realm (Routledge).

The Urban Attitudes Lab is directed by Prof. Justin Hollander.

Name: Engine of Change
Description: Innovate for Impact – SDG 9 is a student-led campaign focused on Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure. We showcase startups, sustainable technologies, and smart infrastructure ideas that drive progress and support the UN Sustainable Development Goals. Follow us for insights, case studies, and inspiring stories of innovation shaping a better future

Name: Castle Quarter Canterbury
Description: A community-led initiative to showcase one of Canterbury's hidden gems, rich in history, independent businesses and an eclectic mix of passionate locals, entrepreneurs and creatives.

Name: Garden State Community Collective
Description: Garden State Community Collective (GSCC) pioneers a community-centered investment approach, blending financial growth with societal impact. We invest in sports, entertainment, and technology ventures that align with our mission to foster inclusivity, innovation, and sustainable development. Our key initiatives include Youth Programs for mentorship and education, Tech Innovations supporting revolutionary startups, Sports and Entertainment Ventures that promote community engagement, and the Battle at the Beach sports showcase. Together, we’re building a legacy of positive change.

Name: Miti Mitaani
Description:
Miti Mitaani (which means ‘trees in our neighborhoods’ in Kiswahili) scales tree cover in African cities by creating climate jobs. We engage women and youth as tree stewards, who are paid to plant and ensure the survival of trees.

To date, Miti Mitaani has established 3 community forests in Nairobi. Women and youth can earn up to $375 by planting and caring for trees in their own neighborhoods. As a result, a tree steward increases her or his monthly household income by 50% on average.

Temperatures in cities globally are rising as tree cover declines due to urban development. Trees are the most cost effective and scalable way to mitigate the effects of climate change. We are creating greener, cooler and climate-resilient cities, one tree at a time.

Name: Green Our Walls

Green Our Walls is an Urban Inclusivity Lab that co-creates greener, softer, more welcoming, walkable neighborhoods and blended experiences that flourish where people come together as individuals, in groups to collaborate, engage and support each other and the environment.

GOW works with ever increasing diversity, inclusivity and sustainability.

Description:
We are on a mission to make a difference for communities met with urban challenges that impact their health and well-being. Designing urban infrastructure that encourages physical activity, nurtures social connections, and promotes overall community health.

Utilising a democratic, evidence-based design methodology, we are committed to unlocking the local knowledge that lies within our streets and neighbourhoods, amplifying voices and increasing access to meaningful common space.

Join us in our journey to foster healthier and more connected communities through physical activity and improved accessibility. Follow us for updates, insights, and opportunities to get involved.

Description: CMX is a vibrant community where passionate professionals come together to connect, learn, and grow. As a member, you gain access to a wealth of knowledge and expertise in community building. We provide a supportive environment for networking, sharing ideas, and collaborating with like-minded individuals. By being part of our CMX Connect community, you can enhance your skills, expand your network, and stay updated with the latest trends in community management. It's a space where you can thrive both personally and professionally, surrounded by a community that understands and supports your journey.

Name: Imaginable Guidelines
Description:
Katıl, Oyna, Paylaş
HEY! Hayal Edilebilir Tasarım Kılavuzu, kentlerin nasıl tasarlandığını keşfetmeyi sağlayan bir kart oyunu. Bu kart oyunu, kentin limitlerini ve sağladığı olanakları temel alan bilgiyle yolan çıkan bir  tasarım aracı olarak geliştirildi. Oyun ayrıca, hem herkesin yaşanabilir kent kavramını tartışabileceği hem de beraber hayata geçirilebilecek gündelik hayat çözümlerini önerebileceği yöntemler sunuyor. Paylaşım platformu; vatandaşları, profesyonelleri ve belediye aktörlerini bütünsel düşünmeye yönlendirecek uzmanlardan, tasarımcılardan ve sanatçılardan yararlanarak kitle kaynaklı bir çalışma yapar. Yaşanılabilir kamusal alan tasarımını gerçekleştirmek adına çözümler üretmek için ortak bir kelime hazinesini Hayal Edin, Keşfedin, Tartışın. 

Join, Play, Share 
HEY! Imaginable Guidelines is a card game to discover how cities are designed. The card game is a design toolkit with a base of knowledge to explore the limits and opportunities your city offers. It offers methods for everyone to discuss livable cities with each other and suggests sensible solutions to real-life problems that can be implemented together. The sharing platform crowd-sources the knowledge from experts, designers and artist to empowers designers, citizens, and municipal actors to think holistically. Imagine, Explore, Discuss a shared vocabulary to generate solutions to realize livable public space design. 

#HEY #ImaginableGuidelines #ImagineExploreDiscussShare #urbangame #imagine #join  #exploreHEY #imagineHEY #discussHEY #shareHEY

Name: IN. Alta Sabina
Description:
IN. Alta Sabina: il futuro delle aree interne passa da qui.
IN. Alta Sabina è un progetto ambizioso nato per rigenerare il territorio dell’Alta Sabina, un’area ricca di bellezze naturali, storia e cultura, ma che da anni affronta sfide come spopolamento e perdita di attività tradizionali. Grazie al supporto del PNRR e a un finanziamento di 3 milioni di euro, lavoriamo per trasformare questa area in un modello di sviluppo sostenibile, innovativo e replicabile.

 Perché l’Alta Sabina?
Abbiamo scelto questo territorio per le sue potenzialità inespresse. I suoi laghi Salto e Turano, i cammini storici di San Benedetto e Francesco e la Riserva Naturale Regionale dei Monti Navegna e Cervia rappresentano un patrimonio unico, capace di attrarre sia turisti che nuovi abitanti. È un luogo perfetto per sperimentare soluzioni che combinano tradizione e innovazione.

 Cosa stiamo facendo?
Con il coinvolgimento attivo delle comunità locali e degli stakeholder, stiamo lavorando su:
- Comunità energetiche per rendere il territorio più sostenibile.
- Valorizzazione dei cammini storici per un turismo slow e responsabile.
- Digital Twin territoriale, una piattaforma digitale per monitorare e gestire le risorse naturali.
- Rigenerazione sociale e culturale, puntando sul capitale umano e sul ritorno alle aree interne.

 Il nostro approccio
Alta Sabina è più di un progetto: è un laboratorio aperto dove istituzioni, cittadini e imprese collaborano per co-progettare un futuro migliore. Il nostro obiettivo è creare un modello di rigenerazione territoriale replicabile in altre aree interne d’Italia e d’Europa.

Name: Werkspoorkwartier
Description:
Het Werkspoorkwartier is gelegen tussen de Cartesiusweg en het Amsterdam Rijnkanaal in Utrecht. Het gebied bloeit en bruist en is volop in ontwikkeling! Het is een plek waar steeds meer ondernemers neerstrijken. Van sportscholen en autobedrijven tot horeca en vele creatieve en circulaire makers. Ook als bezoeker is er vanalles te beleven. 

De ontwikkeling van het Werkspoorkwartier wordt gesteund door HUB-IN, een door de de EU gefinancierd project. Het doel van dit project is om historische stedelijke gebieden in Europa te behouden en te transformeren door middel van innovatie en ondernemerschap. De ambitie is om van het Werkspoorkwartier een 'cultureel en circulair maakgebied' te maken. En dat krijgt steeds meer vorm!

Name: Miljøpakken
Description:
Miljøpakken promotes alternative means of travel. Our overall goal is to ensure that the inevitable growth in passenger transport is seen in public transport, cycling and walking rather than more driving. In order to achieve this, we build new roads, bridges, bicycle lanes, sidewalks and more, cutting queues and greenhouse gas emissions, as well as facilitating easy and safe travel.


The multi-party collaboration project Miljøpakken consists of the municipalities of Trondheim, Melhus, Malvik and Stjørdal, as well as the Trøndelag County Council, the Norwegian Public Roads Administration, the Railway directorate and the County Governor of Trøndelag.

Name: Shaari Planners Sdn Bhd
Description:
Established in 2022, Shaari Planners Sdn Bhd is an Urban Planning Consultant, based in Bertam Kepala Batas, Pulau Pinang.

Shaari Planners currently offers clients a professional and comprehensive consulting services in Urban Planning, Land Advisory, Planning Research and Social Planning.

Our team of dedicated professionals work closely with client groups in both public and private sectors to deliver our portfolio of projects, in Physical and Strategic Planning. These projects comprise of the preparation and submission for Planning Permission, Master Plans, Development Proposal Reports (LCP), Social Impact Assessment (SIA) Studies and Reports and Planning & Development Guideline Studies. 

Shaari Planners is committed to provide professional services that are proactive, efficient, and sustainable to its designs and planning solutions to continuously set new standards in planning and parallel to meet the client and local needs.

Description:
𝗜𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗜𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗱𝘁 𝗝𝗲𝗻𝗮 𝗲.𝗩.
 Jena liegt uns am Herzen – besonders die Innenstadt.

Deswegen haben sich 2017 engagierte Unternehmer:innen unserer Innenstadt an einen Tisch gesetzt und diesen Verein gegründet. Durch Persönlichkeit, Qualität und lokale Verbundenheit wollen wir gemeinsam unsere Innenstadt beleben. Unsere über 120 Mitglieder aus Einzelhandel, Gastronomie, Dienstleistung, Kultur und Kreativwirtschaft engagieren sich dafür, dass die Jenaer Innenstadt ein Ort bleibt, an dem man gern verweilt, einkauft, arbeitet und sich begegnet.

 Als Initiative Innenstadt Jena e.V. verstehen wir uns als starke Stimme der City. Wir vertreten die Interessen unserer Mitglieder gegenüber Politik, Verwaltung und Öffentlichkeit – konstruktiv, lösungsorientiert und immer im Dialog.

Katja Jarmer

Wir setzen auf Kooperation, Engagement und die Kraft der Gemeinschaft. Eine starke Innenstadt entsteht nicht von allein, sondern durch Menschen, die sich einbringen. Durch Ideen, die bewegen. Und durch Visionen, die verbinden.

 𝐃𝐮 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐬𝐭 𝐝𝐚𝐛𝐞𝐢 𝐬𝐞𝐢𝐧? Dann werde Teil unseres Netzwerkes und schreibe uns eine Nachricht!
 Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Zukunft der Innenstadt.

Name: The Guernsey Development Agency
Description:
The Guernsey Development Agency (‘Agency’) will become the coordinating authority for the integrated development and enhancement of the island’s east coast.

The Agency will act as a bridge between various political committees and public service departments, connecting an awareness of relevant policies to the planning and delivery of complex projects which will contribute to the island’s future economic and social success.

Daher unterstützt sie Wohnprojektinitiativen durch die Vergabe städtischer Liegenschaften im Konzeptverfahren und ein Beratungsangebot zu allen Fragen rund ums kooperative Bauen und Wohnen. Anlaufstelle und Drehscheibe für die Wohnprojekte ist das Netzwerk Leipziger Freiheit.

Name: CHÃO Assessoria Técnica Popular
Description:
CHÃO is a non-profit civil association, founded on January 15, 2022, with the aim of strengthening, fostering, and advising communities in their struggle for effective rights and public policies, aiming to reduce socio-spatial inequalities. The organization is headquartered in Aracaju, Sergipe, Brazil.

Mission and Objectives:

Strengthen processes of self-organization and resistance of territories.
Advise communities on impacts caused by large-scale projects and urban and agrarian policies.
Disseminate technical and legal knowledge, integrating it with local wisdom.
Promote public reflections and debates on space, environment, and society.
Contribute to the construction of practices in popular technical advisory, especially in the Northeast, in Sergipe.
Scope and Services Offered:

CHÃO offers a wide range of services, including technical advisory, consultancy, and project development in areas such as architecture, urban planning, engineering, geography, among others. Our work aims to ensure the right to decent housing, to the city, to land, and to the territory, driving processes of community-led spatial production and promoting access to free public technical assistance.

Partnership Network:

The entity establishes partnerships with grassroots organizations, existing networks, universities, research institutes, and similar entities, aiming to exchange experiences and develop participatory methodologies and social technology.

Future Vision:

CHÃO seeks to expand its connections and contribute to the registration and documentation of advocacy processes and struggles, strengthening the role of memory and local history, of resistances, and of popular wisdom.

Description: We blend outstanding expertise in urban planning, change management, and strategic communications, to bring leaders' visions of safe, sustainable, and inviting public spaces to life.

Name: Vaere Social
Description:
Vaere Social is a social strategy and planning consultancy based in Canberra, working with clients across Australia. We help shape places and policies that support community wellbeing, inclusion, and long-term social value.

The name Vaere (pronounced “vair”) is inspired by the Norwegian and Danish word vælvære, meaning wellbeing — a reminder that good planning is about creating places where people can simply be.

Our work brings a people-first lens to urban planning and policy, with deep expertise in understanding how social impacts unfold — and how to design for a future that reflects the reality of a changing climate, shifting demographics, and evolving community needs.

Our services include:

Social impact assessment

Social infrastructure planning

Social value and benefit statements

Place-based strategy

Placemaking and activation

Community wellbeing and engagement

Community resilience and inclusion

We combine evidence, insight and empathy to help create places that endure — socially, culturally and practically.

Name: Innovation Hub

Innovation Hubs Oman: Empowering Entrepreneurs and Fostering Innovation

We are dedicated to creating dynamic innovation hubs across Oman, providing startups and entrepreneurs with the resources, mentorship, and networking opportunities they need to succeed. Join us in driving economic growth and innovation in Oman.

Our target audience is villages and counties who have gone through longer periods of time without having a comprehensive master plan updated or created and or are having difficulty allocating time and resources towards doing so.

Longhorn Urbanists is a student-led organization at The University of Texas at Austin dedicated to urbanism.

We provide a space for students to deepen their understanding of city-specific issues, from planning and design to policy, transportation, and sustainability. 

Through engaging speakers, hands-on workshops, and meaningful networking opportunities, Longhorn Urbanists works to empower the next generation of urban leaders.

We believe in the power of design and cultural investment to shape environments that not only meet the needs of today but also cultivate a brighter future for generations to come.

Name: dencities
Description:
Dencities ist eine junge, interdisziplinäre Initiative, welche sich mit dem Thema der räumlichen Nachhaltigkeit und dem Bodenverbrauch auseinandersetzt. Wir bündeln fachübergreifende Expertise aus der Architektur, der Raumplanung, der Geoinformatik, sowie dem IT- und Kreativbereich.  
  
Seit Juli 2023 ist Dencities als Verein organisiert und verfolgt die Entwicklung einer partizipativen Wertschöpfungsinitiative für Gemeinden und Planer*innen, welche gezielt neue Lösungsansätze auf bestehende Probleme der Kommunalplanung bietet und die Umsetzung nachhaltiger Ortsentwicklung unterstützt.

Name: SYKE ry – Suomen Ydinkeskusta Kehittäjät
Description:
SYKE ry – Suomen Ydinkeskusta Kehittäjät on Helsingissä kotipaikkaansa pitävä yhdistys, jonka tavoitteena on edistää suomalaisten kaupunkikeskustojen elinvoimaa, toimivuutta ja vetovoimaa.

Toimimme foorumina keskustojen kehittämisen ammattilaisille ja kaupunkikehityksen sidosryhmille, edistäen tutkimukseen perustuvaa kehittämistä ja asiantuntijuutta. Haluamme vahvistaa keskustojen asemaa kestävän, vetovoimaisen ja toimivan kaupungin sydämenä.

 Järjestämme tilaisuuksia, tapahtumia ja koulutuksia
 Toimimme kansallisissa ja kansainvälisissä verkostoissa
 Julkaisemme tutkimuksia ja selvityksiä
 Vaikutamme viestinnällä, kannanotoilla ja yhteisellä markkinoinnilla
 Tuemme keskustojen kehittämistoimialan elinvoimaa ja vaikuttavuutta

SYKE ry kokoaa yhteen kehittäjäammattilaiset, päättäjät ja asiantuntijat – luodaan yhdessä kestävämmät ja toimivammat ydinkeskustat Suomessa.

Name: Oficina Urbana
Description:

Name: Serendi-Pals Studio
Description:
KvK number: 98859862

As a place-making project, Serendi-Pals Studio is founded on the conviction that public space, both physical or virtual, co-shapes the civic character of society. The Studio’s central ambition is to design environments in which people of different cultural backgrounds can encounter each other in meaningful and productive ways.

In our multicultural cities, diversity has become a structural condition of everyday life, yet the persistence of social fragmentation and cultural distance reveals that diversity alone does not guarantee civic cohesion. Many residents live in proximity without the relational infrastructures needed to inhabit difference with ease. Serendi-Pals Studio aims to address this structural challenge by offering an set of 4 integral design services that support inclusive, people-centered public spaces that are educative, safe, and livable 

Serendi-Pals Studio translates the abstract vision of “unity in diversity” into operational and evidence-based public-space innovations. The Studio’s name reflects its conceptual orientation. “Serendi” refers to serendipity, understood here not as random chance but as the possibility for significant encounters that could not have been predicted beforehand. Cities depend on such encounters. They form the unpredictable but necessary relational tissue that allows diverse populations to develop shared norms, negotiate differences, and imagine a common future. “Pals” signals the Studio’s belief in horizontal collaboration. It evokes the warmth, familiarity, and peer-to-peer spirit that characterizes constructive civic relationships. Together the term “Serendi-Pals” captures a dual philosophical premise: that meaningful social transformation begins with unexpected but well-orchestrated forms of encounter, and that such encounters become civically valuable when participants engage one another as co-creators rather than as strangers.

Let's connect and co-create via: serendipals@gmail.com

Name: CREST Climate-resilient coastal urban infrastructures through digital twinning
Description:
CREST is a 3-year project co-funded by the JPI Urban Europe programme and managed by a consortium of 8 partners from France (ETTIS research laboratory of the INRAe- of the French National Research Institute for Agriculture, Food and Environment 
, the Business School of Economics of the University of Bordeaux, Accent Sud association), Poland ( Gmina Kołobrzeg, INnCREASE Sp. z.o.o, IRMiR- Institute of Urban and Regional Development ) and Norway ( Augmentcity AS, Møre og Romsdal Fylkeskommune).

The project goal is to help vulnerable urban areas to set-up and roll-out collaborative platforms through the Advanced Technology and Augmented Reality of Digital Twinning and through co-creation approaches, in order to support collective environmental decision-making and innovative policy practices, enabling robust and resilient responses in 3 European urban areas for resilient urban infrastructure adaptation to climate change.

Climate change largely impacts urban life. Extreme temperatures have an impact on sea level rise and, subsequently, nefarious events such as floods, droughts and storms have costly impacts on cities' basic services, infrastructure, housing, livelihoods and health. Cities are responsible for 75% of CO2 emissions, and so its stakeholders must come forward with out of the box solutions to promote innovation and stimulate urban resilience by limiting negative impacts of climate change. 

But for a problem to be addressed, it must first be seen and felt. Visualization is a potential way of increasing engagement with climate change, and IT developments, such as Artificial Intelligence and Augmented Reality, provide significant advancements that can be transformative in engaging audiences with climate change issues.
This is at the basis of Augmentcity which developed a ground-breaking way to operate digital twins of cities, enabling data and “what-if” scenarios.

Name: UIC2
Description:
The Urban-Air-Mobility Initiative Cities Community (UIC2) is a dynamic network of European cities and regions dedicated to advancing sustainable and responsible urban air mobility (UAM). 

The initiative was established in October 2017 within EU's Smart Cities Marketplace and then it was transitioned to the European Commission’s CIVITAS Initiative in September 2022. UIC2 is an initiative composed of and benefiting cities/regions and, as of today, it counts around 40 municipalities and regions from across Europe as members.
At UIC2, the mission is clear: to accelerate the transition towards a more sustainable and secure urban mobility landscape by seamlessly integrating UAM into existing transportation systems. The aim of UIC2 is to foster collaboration, innovation, and knowledge-sharing to catalyse positive change in urban mobility, therefore acting as a pivotal platform for collaboration and advancement in the field of UAM in Europe.
 
If you are passionate about UAM and eager to contribute to a future of more sustainable and secure urban mobility, UIC2 is the community for you!

The Agora was the most urban spot within the urban fabric. Our name also hints toward our belief that public spaces are a city's greatest resource, intrinsically linked to its liveability, attractiveness, and success.

Agorapolis is founded and managed by Mihaela Pușnava, an urban planner, specializing in competitive city management. Professional urbanist for over 10 years, she has coordinated more than 100 public and private projects and strategies, cumulating investment portfolios of over 500 million Euros in implementation.

Core belief: creating beauty in everyday city life.

Name: WinterCity Edmonton
Description:
Since 2013, WinterCity Edmonton has been finding ways to enrich our experience of living in a winter city. Rather than hibernating for six months of every year, we have been engaging with citizens, researching global best practices, working with community partners, and taking creative risks to embrace the passion of Edmontonians, and the possibilities of our chilly season.

Our work has led to the creation of guidelines for winter lighting and design, as well as toolkits and supports for local businesses and community members to continue to thrive year-round.

We have hosted international events such as Crashed Ice and the Winter Cities Shakeup. We have facilitated policy change to support active lifestyles in the winter, and to change patio rules for businesses. We have partnered with local producers and Business Improvement Areas to support winter events.

We have been recognized internationally for our work in embracing and capitalizing on winter’s potential – and we’re not done.

By looking at winter through a lens of opportunity, we continue to elevate the lives and livelihoods of Edmontonians.

Name: Deep Green Consulting AB
Description:

Bakom oss har vi en lång och bred erfarenhet från olika typer av projekt, roller samt sektorer.
 
Vår högsta prioritet är att skapa hållbara värden för våra kunder och vi försöker alltid integrera någon hållbarhetsdimension i våra uppdrag för att ständigt skapa en förflyttning och utmana traditionellt tänkande. Vår styrka är att driva framgångsrika och lönsamma projekt i nära samarbete med våra kunder och partners genom arbetssätt i framkant och ett modigt ledarskap.
 
Vi erbjuder tjänster inom projektledning, projekteringsledning (inkl BAS-P), hållbarhetsledning, materialsamordning, byggledning (inkl BAS-U) och kontrollansvarig. Vid behov erbjuder vi även stöd i verksamhets- och hållbarhetsutveckling. Hos oss kan du även få hjälp med miljöcertifieringar inom LEED, WELL, BREEAM, Svanen och Miljöbyggnad.  Givetvis skräddarsys alla våra tjänster efter våra kunders behov och önskemål och varierar därmed avseende omfattning och innehåll för att skapa maximalt värde!

Välkommen att kontakta oss för att se hur vi kan göra skillnad tillsammans!

Beacon Street Consulting is a full-service community planning firm that specializes in areas of community development from creating plans, identifying funding resources, developing unique public spaces to public policy analysis. With over twenty-five years of experience in the public sector, Beacon Street brings a unique prospective to community development. It is one rooted in passionately serving the public interest, assisting communities in creating livable public spaces and places, and being a steward of public financial resources. 

Beacon Street has experience in developing community plans from long-range comprehensive plans to Parks Master Plans to County level Five Year Capital Improvement Plans. Working in the public sector, I have written and administered over fifty (50) grants securing approximately $65 million for various community projects ranging from public parks, infrastructure, transportation, housing, human services, and economic development.

Our public sector experience also includes being the project manager on public projects such as public parks, libraries, community centers, roads, bridges, and an innovation and technology campus. In the area of public policy, Beacon Street has written zoning ordinances for large and small communities, developed revenue forecasts, tracked expenditures, and formalized annual budgets. Beacon Street has also analyzed housing development trends, fiscal impact of specific development types, and tracked cost of public services in relationship to proposed land use developments.

Name: MRC de L'Île-d'Orléans
Description:
L'Île d'Orléans, le plus grand site patrimonial au Québec, est un vaste territoire de 269,4 km2. 

Créée en 1980, la MRC de l'Île d'Orléans est constituée de six municipalités :

Saint-Pierre
Sainte-Famille
Saint-François
Saint-Jean
Saint-Laurent
Sainte-Pétronille

Chaque municipalité possède un caractère patrimonial et agricole distinctif qui s'articule autour d'un cœur de village présentant une concentration de bâtiments et lieux patrimoniaux remarquables : l'église, le presbytère, la place de l'église, le cimetière, les chapelles, l'école, le couvent, les maisons, les commerces, les hangars, les ateliers, le quai... 

À l'extérieur du village, les ensembles bâtis agricoles dominent et se conjuguent à des paysages ruraux et naturels, lesquels offrent des vues exceptionnelles sur le fleuve Saint-Laurent. 

Les paysages culturels de l'Île d'Orléans sont uniques, riches et diversifiés, mais aussi très fragiles. C'est pourquoi ils doivent être protégés et mis en valeur adéquatement; voilà donc la mission de la MRC de L'Île-d'Orléans.

Name: Urban Design and Spatial Analytics Lab.
Description:
The Urban Design and Spatial Analytics Lab (UDSAL) at Hanyang University focuses on urban planning and design, urban spatial analytics, and visualization based on theoretical background of urban planning and design.

Urban space is a physical and social environment that has accumulated human activities in time and space. Therefore, it is very important to understand the characteristics of physical and socioeconomic environment in urban spatial analytics.

Our lab uses quantitative and qualitative approaches to analyze natural environment, built environment, and transportation system as well as demographic and socioeconomic environments. Our lab’s research fields are land use and urban spatial structure, commuting and urban mobility, smart growth and metropolitan planning, urban morphological analysis and walking environment, urban design and urban regeneration, bigdata and smart cities, applications of machine learning/deep learning techniques and multimodal LLM, climate change and urban thermal environment, and integrated research of land use-transportation-environment-public health.

The current research subjects are as follows: (1) the impact of built environment on walking activities, (2) the characteristics of physical environment on pedestrian traffic accidents (3) the relationship among built environment, physical activity, and health, (4) analysis of urban form using GIS and Space Syntax, (5) applications of machine learning/deep learning and multimodal LLM to urban planning and design issues (6) the impact of three-dimensional physical environment on urban thermal environment, and (7) integrated research among land use, transportation, environment and public health.

Name: CNU North Texas
Description:
CNU North Texas is a regional chapter of the Congress for the New Urbanism, a national organization dedicated to building more walkable, sustainable, and inclusive communities.

We bring together urban planners, architects, developers, policymakers, academics, and engaged citizens to advance the principles of good urbanism across the North Central Texas region. Our focus is on thoughtful growth, smart infill, transit-oriented development, and reimagining infrastructure to better serve people—not just cars.

At CNU North Texas, we advocate for local public policy reforms, host community tours, hard-hat site visits, panels, and networking events, and provide resources for professionals and laypeople alike who are passionate about shaping better places. We believe New Urbanist principles are essential to addressing some of our most pressing regional challenges: housing affordability, economic opportunity, social equity, climate resilience, and neighborhood vitality.

Guided by the Charter of the Congress for the New Urbanism, we believe:

Neighborhoods should be diverse in use and population

Streets and public spaces should prioritize pedestrians and transit

Cities and towns should be physically defined by accessible, shared places

Design should reflect local history, climate, and context

Our work aligns with CNU’s national strategic goals—including Sustainability, Transportation, Regionalism, Affordability, Financing and Entitlements, and Accessibility.

We’re always looking for new voices and perspectives. Whether you're a longtime urbanist or just curious about making your community better, there’s a place for you here.

 Follow us to stay updated on events, initiatives, and opportunities to get involved.

Sustainable garden projects • Indoor furniture for schools • Sports equipment • Imaginative play spaces • Adventure trails • Bush cubby houses • Outdoor yarning/teaching circles • Veggie gardens • Erosion control • Bush tucker gardens • Fairy houses

We need your help to grow this project, which will benefit our community into the future.

Inspired by the Javanese concept of "njajah desa milang kori" - exploring various villages to acquire knowledge - we strive to understand, internalize, and apply these learnings for a better future, grounded in local cultural wisdom.

We embark on journeys through villages, recognizing their values, assets, and potential. By directly engaging with rural communities, witnessing their activities, and identifying unique opportunities, we assist in transforming these potentials into valuable experiences. This hands-on approach empowers villagers to develop sustainable communities, fostering independence, cultural pride, and environmental stewardship.

Our mission is to invite more people to explore the "njajah desa milang kori" philosophy, immersing themselves in village life. This goes beyond hearing stories - it's about actively participating, observing, and interacting with local communities. Villages need caring individuals willing to reach out and discover how to foster self-sufficiency and prosperity, ensuring safety, peace, and happiness.

Together with Eksotikadesa, we can gain new experiences and valuable life lessons, contributing to a brighter future. Jointly, we can support inclusive movements driving prosperity in local economies, ecological improvements, and strengthened social connections for rural communities through the power of culture. With Eksotikadesa, let's transform villages into captivating, exotic, and enchanting havens for both locals and visitors alike, championing sustainable development and celebrating cultural identities.

Heritage-led regeneration
Strategic funding, feasibility and delivery of capital projects

ProMo is committed to transparent decision-making, reinvesting surplus into community benefit, and ensuring that regeneration in Montrose is led locally, benefits residents, and strengthens the town’s economic and cultural resilience.

Name: Alquimia Urbana
Description: Asesorar a líderes del sector público y privado para que incorporen la perspectiva de género en sus programas, políticas, servicios, decisiones y al interior de sus organizaciones para el desarrollo urbano sustentable.

Name: Campus District, Inc.
Description: Campus District, Inc. is the non-profit community organization serving the arts, eds, and meds of downtown Cleveland, including Cleveland State University, Cuyahoga Community College, St. Vincent Medical Center, and the Superior Arts District.

Name: KTB - Korea town Berlin
Description: A culture bridging experiment for those questioning why, where, and how.

Name: Staple Inc.
Description:
Staple is a soft developer with offices in the small town of Setoda, Hiroshima and the metropolitan hub of Nihonbashi, Tokyo. Staple undertakes projects that redefine placemaking – spanning real estate development, management, and operation.

Our definition of ‘soft’ is something flexible, not concrete. 
We are people who think of people and the environments that they feel close to. 
A soft approach is important in order to keep an openness towards diversity of ideas, culture, and living things.

A developer supports the development of a place, a property, a people and their ideas.
We believe that within development, value comes from fostering diversity and creating a sense of proximity between people and their ideas, their things, and each other.

Staple reveals the opportunities and options outside of the city by focusing on placemaking with this potential in mind. If this potential is realized, then each and every region of Japan will thrive in a unique way, and continue to be worth exploring.

Staple does not consider people who happen to interact with a place into the typical duality of ‘locals’ and ‘tourists’. In addition to ‘original locals,’ anyone who has some kind of relationship to a place can be thought of as a ‘new local,’ and even visitors and travelers should be thought of as ‘potential locals.’ 

Whether the motivation is vacation or tourism, a visitor can become a new local, and over time the three types of locals will become a strong community. That unification is one of the most significant aspects of revitalization.

Create a new infrastructure that attracts new locals.
Make the city more livable for all three types of locals by developing dynamic new infrastructure.

Developing the soft infrastructure allows people to live in a way whereby the local history, culture, land, and climate is reflected in all aspects of daily life.

Name: Lasat Life Corp
Description:
Lasat Life Corp is a dedicated non-governmental organization committed to fostering sustainable development and improving the quality of life in underserved communities. Established with a vision to create lasting change, we focus on education, healthcare, and economic empowerment to build resilient and thriving societies.

Our Mission

To empower individuals and communities by providing access to essential resources, education, and opportunities, enabling self-sufficiency and a higher quality of life.

Key Initiatives

Education for All: Establishing and supporting schools in remote areas, providing scholarships and educational materials to underprivileged children, and organizing vocational training programs to equip youth with practical skills.

Healthcare Access: Setting up mobile clinics to deliver healthcare services in rural regions, conducting health awareness campaigns and workshops, and facilitating access to clean water and sanitation facilities.

Economic Empowerment: Supporting small-scale entrepreneurs with business training, promoting sustainable agriculture by providing resources to farmers, and creating job opportunities through community development projects.

Join us in our mission to create a brighter future for all. Whether through donations, volunteering, or partnerships, your support can make a significant difference. Together, we can build stronger, healthier, and more prosperous communities.

Name: MI «KHARKIV OFFICE OF REFORM»
Description: Комунальна установа міста, яка працює за двома напрямами: фандрейзинг і проєктна діяльність

Name: Inclua-se
Description:
Para quem a cidade foi feita?
Essa é a pergunta que nos move.

Acreditamos em cidades que escutam, acolhem e respeitam quem as vive.
Promovemos uma inteligência urbana sensível, que une tecnologia, inclusão e afeto por meio de parcerias institucionais com quem age pelo mesmo que nós. 

Vem com a gente?
__ 

Who was the city made for?
That’s the question that drives us.

We believe in cities that listen, that embrace, and that respect those who live them. We promote a sensitive urban intelligence — one that brings together technology, inclusion, and care.

Want to join us?
Location: Porto Alegre , Rio Grande do Sul

Name: Bright Streets
Description: Urban planning projects can save lives, create lively public spaces, and revitalize communities. Real world examples of these benefits are powerful. At Bright Streets, we are compiling a photo library and project database of these examples to inspire change and demonstrate that great cities are possible!

Description: Die Walsergemeinde Galtür liegt am Ende des Paznauntals auf 1.600 m.ü.M. umrahmt von dem majestätischen Gipfeln der Silvrettagruppe und der Verwallgruppe.

Name: Arvysta Collective
Description:
Arvysta Collective is a creative strategy company that helps businesses build strong, authentic brand identities that stand the test of time. We work with organizations to understand their values, audience behavior, and long term goals before shaping a brand system that communicates with clarity.

Our approach is grounded in research and creative insight. Arvysta Collective studies market trends, visual patterns, and communication styles to help companies stand out in crowded markets. We develop brand frameworks, messaging systems, and visual assets that guide how a brand behaves and evolves across all digital and physical touchpoints.

We believe a brand should reflect more than a logo. It should express personality, inspire trust, and support the direction a company wants to grow. Arvysta Collective is committed to helping businesses express who they are and build stronger relationships with the people they serve.

Name: Sotonoba
Description:
Sotonoba is a collaborative dedicated to enriching public spaces, aiming at “turning outdoor spaces into great places.” To achieve this vision, Sotonoba provides various functions including online media, a community platform, consultancy, activation/experiment, training, advocacy, research, and publication on public spaces.
 
Since its launch in 2014, Sotonoba has played a key role in shaping the public space movement across Japan and has established itself as one of the leading organizations in the field. As of 2025, the platform offers over 900 online articles covering a wide range of topics and has a growing community of more than 100 members. Additionally, Sotonoba collaborates with partners to host Placemaking Japan, which can be your gateway to connect and collaborate with placemakers in Japan.

Name: OASI Desenvolvimento Urbano
Description:
Somos um escritório formado por arquitetos, urbanistas e engenheiros, especializados em desenvolvimento urbano e dedicados a promover a qualidade de vida através da cidade. 
Ao longo do tempo, conciliamos a experiência junto ao poder público com a capacidade de analisar todos os aspectos que compõem o complexo desenvolvimento das cidades. A partir disso, passamos a pensar e projetar ambientes urbanos eficientes, através de soluções inteligentes, viáveis e criativas. Ah, e o melhor: entregamos soluções completas, que vão desde os licenciamentos até a urbanização.
Ou seja, cuidamos de todas as demandas relacionadas aos projetos legais, sejam estas: aprovação de projetos, elaborações de Relatório de Impacto de Vizinhança, de Estudos de Viabilidade Legal, de regularização de obras, desdobro ou unificação de lotes. E, principalmente, criamos projetos urbanísticos e todos os demais que dele derivarem.
O resultado de tudo isso é a criação de espaços urbanos orientados para as pessoas, seu bem-estar e direitos.

Name: KIP Kunst Im Park
Description: CONNECT ART WITH LIFESYTLYE . 18 arts at 1 place at 1 event p.a. to celebrate . accessible 24/7 in 1 metaverse . THIS IS ART IN THE NEW ECONOMY .

Name: Den Helder Stad aan Zee
Description:
Stad aan Zee gaat over het Stadshart en Willemsoord. Een gebied volop in ontwikkeling, waar de Waddenzee naadloos overloopt in de stad en de natuur letterlijk om de hoek ligt. Hier ontstaat iets moois. En jij kunt er gaan wonen en werken. 

De komende tijd wordt hier gebouwd aan nieuwe en vernieuwde panden op de plek waar ooit het uitgaansleven bruiste. Ze stuwen een frisse wind vanaf de dijk door de straten naar de binnenstad. 

Ondertussen wijst groen je de weg. Met een park voor de deur, dat zich uitstrekt in een lange groenstrook tussen station en zee, is er altijd een plek om je stadshart op te halen. Volg het en je komt vanzelf langs al het goede dat het centrum van Den Helder te bieden heeft. Een compact winkelgebied, met terrassen, parkeergelegenheid, een foodcourt en een grote zaterdagmarkt met verse producten uit de streek.

Name: GHV DESARROLLADOR INMOBILIARIO
Description: GHV es una empresa del sector inmobiliario, comprometida con transformar vidas mediante la creación de comunidades innovadoras y sostenibles. Nos especializamos en diseñar y construir espacios que inspiran y conectan a las personas, mejorando su calidad de vida a través de la arquitectura y el urbanismo de vanguardia. Desarrollamos proyectos con una estructura financiera robusta, permitiendo llevar a cabo iniciativas exitosas que benefician tanto a nuestras comunidades como a nuestros socios.

Name: Mount Airy Downtown, Inc.
Description: Mount Airy Downtown, Inc. (MAD Inc.) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization responsible for supervising and executing the Main Street Program in Mount Airy, North Carolina.

Name: digital.interkommunal Kalletal.Lemgo
Description: digital.interkommunal Kalletal.Lemgo – das ist das gemeinsame Smart City-Projekt der Kommunen Kalletal und Lemgo. Mithilfe der Vorzüge von Digitalisierung ist es unser Ziel, Lemgo und das Kalletal lebenswerter, nachhaltiger und zukunftssicher zu gestalten. Smart City übersetzen wir dabei mit „intelligente Stadt“. Gemeinsam mit Bürger*innen und kommunalen Akteur*innen wollen wir die Smart City-Vision (weiter-)entwickeln: Beteiligung und Transparenz schreiben wir daher groß! Der Mensch steht im Mittelpunkt.

Name: Ikatan Ahli Rancang Kota Indonesia (IARKI)
Description:
IARKI dideklarasikan di Jakarta pada 11 Januari 2019 oleh 126 deklarator dari seluruh Indonesia. Terbentuk dari semangat kolektif para alumni sekolah Rancang Kota, IARKI lahir untuk meningkatkan wawasan dan kesadaran akan profesi ini di Indonesia. Diskusi yang dimulai sejak 2018 akhirnya melahirkan tim formatur yang membawa IARKI menjadi wadah profesional yang solid.

Sejak berdiri, IARKI terus berkembang dan aktif mengadakan berbagai kegiatan publik seperti Kopi Sore, Kosa Kata Kota, Bincang Rancang Kota, dan Bedah Buku, baik daring maupun luring. Sayembara desain logo pada akhir 2019 menjadi salah satu langkah awal memperkenalkan identitas organisasi. Melalui media sosial, IARKI terus menginspirasi dan menjangkau lebih banyak profesional dan penggiat kota untuk bergabung. Dari hanya 188 anggota di awal 2021, kini jumlah Anggota Biasa telah mencapai 412 orang.

Dalam memperkuat fondasi kelembagaan, IARKI telah melengkapi berbagai dokumen administratif, termasuk Akta Notaris dan pendaftaran di Kemenkumham (Oktober 2019) serta NPWP (Februari 2020). Penyusunan AD/ART dan Kode Etik Profesi terus disempurnakan untuk memastikan organisasi berkembang sesuai dengan kebutuhan anggotanya.

Sebagai organisasi yang terus maju, IARKI berkomitmen untuk memperkuat peran profesi Rancang Kota dan membangun sinergi yang lebih luas. Kolaborasi telah terjalin dengan berbagai asosiasi profesi seperti IAI, IAP, IALI, IABHI, dan GBCI, institusi pendidikan seperti Universitas Gunadharma, serta asosiasi serupa di luar negeri seperti Perekabandar Malaysia. Dengan semangat kebersamaan, IARKI terus bergerak maju, menjadi wadah yang semakin kuat bagi para profesional Rancang Kota di Indonesia.

Name: Fondazione Il Campo dell'Arte
Description:
Un campo della creatività e della condivisione orientato ad impiegare la forza dell’arte come valore sociale aperto alla ricerca e alla sperimentazione. Vogliamo creare interazioni, partecipazioni e forme di progettualità legate a valori di sviluppo eco-sostenibile.

Il Campo dell’Arte punta a superare la divisione tra marginalità e centralità, in quanto categorie legate al mero concetto di profitto, per dare nuovi significati ai valori economici. La produzione sarà, quindi, l’espressione non solo di opere ma anche di progetti, ricerca, esperienze, tra artisti e i diversi soggetti coinvolti.

"Diffusamentemuseo” intende riportare ad unità le componenti dell’uomo e del territorio, con una visione della vita basata sull’equilibrio tra il fare e il pensare.

Crediamo negli indirizzi artistici sensibili alla valorizzazione di contenuti etico-sociali e nel coinvolgimento creativo delle persone in attività legate ai luoghi del territorio e, in particolare, al mondo rurale ed alla natura, pur aperte ed in contatto con il mondo globale.

Name: Recife Cidade Parque
Description: Parceria entre a Prefeitura do Recife e a UFPE, na busca de soluções para os desafios do Recife diante das mudanças climáticas.

Name: Zanders-Entwicklungsgesellschaft mbH
Description: Die Zanders-Entwicklungsgesellschaft mbH (ZEG) ist zu 100 % eine Tochter der Stadt Bergisch Gladbach. Unser Auftrag: die Konversion der ehemaligen Papierfabrik Zanders zu einem zukunftsfähigen, urbanen Quartier mitten in der Stadt. Mit einem interdisziplinären Team arbeiten wir daran, das Zanders-Quartier als einen vielseitigen Raum für Wohnen, Arbeiten, Lernen und Leben zu gestalten – nachhaltig, gemeinwohlorientiert und im Dialog mit der Stadtgesellschaft.

Name: Smart City Urban Transformation (SCUT)
Description:
Established in Partnership with UEF & BBA

SCUT is the implementation platform established as a strategic partner of the Urban Economy Forum (UEF) to turn global urban-development frameworks and related initiatives/projects into real, measurable, and financially viable outcomes.
Where UEF defines the SDG-aligned policies, principles, profiles, initiatives, programs, and international standards for sustainable urban transformation, SCUT delivers the execution—designing, building, delivering, and operationalizing sustainable development solutions on the ground.

As UEF’s execution arm, SCUT bridges the gap between global standards and local implementation, ensuring that cities move from strategic vision to actionable reality with clarity, accountability, and strong financial planning.

Our Vision
To establish SCUT as a globally recognized center that drives progressive, data-driven urban transformation, enabling cities to become sustainable, resilient, and prosperous metropolitan centers.

Our Mission
To serve as a collaborative platform for cities and stakeholders to co-develop, implement, and evaluate smart urban solutions that address key challenges, promote sustainability, and enhance social, economic, and environmental well-being.

What SCUT Delivers
•	Global centre of excellence for urban transformation
•	A capacity-building and investment-enabling platform
•	A platform for Governing the execution of the all initiatives and Projects of all UEF Frontrunner cities 
•	Responsible for Implementation of all initiatives of Frontrunner Cities on Urban SDGs
•	Responsible for Global roadmap & partnerships

Name: Urban Beamers
Description:
Urban Beamers is a civic-driven urban transformation agency empowering Nigerians to shape their cities. We work at the intersection of data, mapping, and community mobilization to spotlight urban injustices, make development processes transparent, and advance policies that build livable, inclusive, and sustainable Nigerian cities.

We believe in people-centered planning—where every resident has the tools and voice to co-create safer, equitable, and climate-resilient urban futures.

What we do:
 Participatory GIS & community mapping
 Urban infrastructure tracking & budget transparency
 Civic education and urban literacy programs
 Advocacy for affordable housing, sustainable mobility, and green infrastructure
 Co-creating practical urban solutions with communities and governments

Our Flagship Initiatives:
 Urban Watch Nigeria
 Nigerian Urban Observatory
 Livable Cities Lab
 #MyCityMyVoice Campaign

 Join us. Let’s build better Nigerian cities—together.

Name: MBM Estuary Strategies
Description:
MBM Estuary Strategies is a nimble firm that builds connections, between workforce systems and infrastructure investments, between public capital and private contractors, between MWBEs, SDVOBs, unions, developers, and frontline communities.

We focus on places shaped by climate risk and economic exclusion. Our work centers around marine and stormwater infrastructure, community-rooted workforce pipelines, and the creation of local economies tied to long-term public projects.

We align projects with people. From idea to execution, we bring together the right partners to make sure infrastructure dollars stay local and create lasting opportunity.

Name: Bobcat Community Builders
Description:
Bobcat Community Builders is a passionate and dedicated organization founded on the principle of creating positive change within our local community. We are committed to addressing key social issues, including homelessness alleviation, food waste mitigation, and community service, with the belief that together, we can build a better tomorrow.

Our Mission:

At Bobcat Community Builders, our mission is threefold...

Homelessness Alleviation ~ We are committed to providing a helping hand to individuals experiencing homelessness in our community. Through volunteer work, and partnerships with shelters and support organizations, we strive to provide the support, resources and hope needed to combat homelessness.


Food Waste Mitigation ~ We are passionate about reducing food waste on campus and ensuring that no one in our community goes to bed hungry. Our dedicated team collaborates with the university, local businesses, restaurants, and food banks to rescue surplus food and redirect it to those who need it most. Plans to host seminars educating students on ways to individually make a difference in their carbon footprint. By doing so, we aim to promote both environmental sustainability and food security.


Community Service ~ We understand that the strength of our community lies in the collective effort of its members. Bobcat Community Builders offers a variety of community service opportunities for volunteers to engage with their neighbors, whether through neighborhood clean-up projects, mentorship programs, or initiatives aimed at improving community spaces.

Name: Calgary's Future
Description:
Calgary’s Future is dedicated to creating a Calgary that works for everyone. We engage everyday Calgarians, workers, municipal politicians, and community organizations to take action and help make Calgary a thriving, accessible, and sustainable city to live in.

In the last municipal election, we registered as a political action committee and helped get real community leaders elected to City Council and the Calgary Board of Education. Now, it’s up to all of us to hold our leaders accountable and advocate for solutions that work for everyone.

By joining our movement, you can help make a difference in your community. Together, we can shape Calgary’s future.

Name: Framtidens Hus, Stockholms Stad
Description:
Framtidens Hus är en offentlig ungdomsverksamhet inom Järva stadsdelsförvaltning med lokaler i både Rinkeby och Husby, som fokuserar på att skapa en positiv och inkluderande miljö för ungdomar och unga vuxna mellan 16 till 19 år. 

Framtidens Hus arbetar med tre huvudsakliga områden: Studier (och demokrati), Arbete (och entreprenörskap) och Meningsfull fritid (och välmående). 

Verksamheten syftar till att erbjuda stöd, aktiviteter och resurser som hjälper ungdomar att utvecklas, bli självständiga och engagera sig i samhället. Genom innovativa sätt att genomföra utbildningsstöd och karriärvägledning stärks ungdomarnas framtidstro och deras förberedelser inför arbetsmarknaden. Framtidens Hus arbetar även med att stärka ungas självkänsla och främja delaktighet genom olika fritidsaktiviteter- och kulturprojekt, som fungerar som en katalysator för personlig utveckling, självledarskap och meningsfull fritid.

Dessutom sker en unik samverkan med offentlig sektor, näringsliv och civilsamhället. Ett arbetssätt där privat och offentliga aktörer tillsammans skapar möjligheter för framtidens arbetskraft och stärker integrationsinsatserna i samhället för unga från socioekonomiskt utsatta områden. Ett både resultatinriktat och förebyggande arbetssätt som även vunnit innovationsutmärkelsen "Framsteget" av Stockholm Stad.

Name: Vibrant Systems
Description:
Vibrant Systems is the collection of nearly a decade of experience working and learning about what it takes to build a flourishing community.

I have curated my skills in research, writing, systems thinking, community asset mapping, and program development. I have applied and perfected these skills in communities across Alberta in several fields, from early child development to community climate adaptation.

Vibrant Systems is a consulting business focused on the entire picture of your community, its success, and how I can help get you there. Whether your team needs support on a project or a project lead, I have the dynamic skills needed to help your community bring its goals to life.

Name: Lokation Solution
Description: We help improving community development in Indonesia through collaborative action

Name: Cygnet Strategies
Description:
Cygnet Strategies helps communities embrace character and sense of place to become remarkable, resilient destinations.

Resilient communities provide safe, appealing homes for residents; supportive, dynamic places for businesses; and vibrant, enticing destinations for visitors.

Name: STADTpsychologie
Description: Die Stadtpsychologie betrachtet Städte als Wesen, mit all ihren Stärken und Schwächen. Sie arbeitet mit dem gesamten ‚Wesen Stadt‘ – also allen Menschen, die in einer Stadt wohnen und arbeiten – und achtet dabei ganz besonders auf den sozialen Zusammenhalt. Dieser ist ein wirksames Mittel, um Krisen besser zu bewältigen und gleichzeitig das positive Lebensgefühl in der Stadt zu stärken.

Name: Sitting with the Sisters
Description: Sitting with the Sisters is dedicated to empowering minority women through mentorship, education, and community support. Our mission is to break down barriers and provide women with the resources they need to thrive in both their personal and professional lives. Through tailored programs, workshops, and initiatives, we aim to foster growth, resilience, and leadership in underrepresented communities.

Name: Institute of Rural Development Planning (IRDP)
Description:
The Institute of Rural Development Planning (IRDP) is a corporate body established by the Parliamentary Act No. 8 of 1980.

This Act provides a legal framework for the Institute to be established as an important national centre for provision of training, research and consultancy services in the field of rural development planning and management with main objective of alleviating qualitative and quantitative shortage of skilled manpower within the framework of sustainable capacity building directed towards reducing poverty and attaining sustainable development

IRDP strives to enhance and strengthen capacity for rural development practitioners by providing post-secondary education and training, research and consultancy services

IRDP is thus a multi-disciplinary and multi-sectorial institute empowered to oversee and coordinate the provision of expertise to in-service and pre-service personnel involved in rural development planning in the country

Since its establishment, IRDP has been shaping destinies in planning and research methodologies focusing on the future prosperity of its students. As a result, graduates with IRDP qualifications have made valuable contributions in their fields. They are a proof of our unique approach of learning and training methodologies.

IRDP Provides a conducive environment for teaching, learning and working to both students and staff. This includes library, academic blocks and the multipurpose hall.

Name: The Farmhouse -  Plymouth
Description: The Farmhouse is a catalyst for positive change, where young minds connect with the earth and community thrives. We provide hands-on learning experiences that foster environmental stewardship and cultivate essential life skills. Join us in building a sustainable future!

Name: Bubble Switch
Description: At BubbleSwitch, we believe that relocating shouldn't mean restarting. We are on a mission to change the way digital nomads integrate into new cities. Born from personal experience and the challenges faced by our globally mobile team.

Name: Community Development Partners
Description:
Community Development Partners, LLC (CDP) is a well-rounded team of management professionals who work with you to plan, secure funds, and oversee projects that are vital to your community. CDP was established in 1994 and represents over 100 years combined experience in the project development and implementation for local governments and non-profit agencies. CDP will work with you to prioritize your local needs and help establish a means to achieve your goals. 



At CDP, we are dedicated to providing our customers with friendly service. We value your business. We have made it our mission to provide reliable products and services in our customer-driven approach.

Our courteous, professional team is able to support a range of services to meet your requirements.

Name: CEE Lombok
Description: We are a recently established nonprofit organization founded by graduate students working to foster change and amplify action-based projects within Lombok, Indonesia with the goal of enhancing the local environment, economy, and society now and for generations to come.

Name: The FAF Coalition
Description:
The FAF Coalition champions initiatives rich in arts, culture, and sustainable design as a catalyst for community development and economic revitalization. It executes those initiatives by creating engaging places and innovative experiences that transform the lives of the community. 
2016 we will ROCK the WOO once again at AND bring to you LOT 323 a POP UP PARK from July-August  featuring activities for kids and adults, live performances, food trucks, fine art and open space for everyone to enjoy. 


We are "The FAF Coalition" and continue to be South Jersey's #1 CELEBRATION OF THE ARTS with local art events full of music, artists and interactive play for the whole family! Stay tuned here for the next  FAF-tabulous event that will most definitely ROCK the WOO and bring you the dose of FAF-in local ART that you deserve.

Name: EASTWARD
Description: An investment vehicle to drive development and investment back to the Southeast of Nigeria enabling industrialisation of the region.

Name: Main Street Hanover, Inc.
Description: The mission of Main Street Hanover is to expand the economic capacity of downtown Hanover, thus improving the business environment, enhancing the quality of place, and increasing community synergy.

Name: Takso
Description:
At Takso, we've revolutionized cultural development planning and evaluation with our outcomes-driven platform. Born from CDN's groundbreaking "The Fourth Pillar of Sustainability" Framework, Takso empowers cultural producers in government entities, museums, libraries, and galleries to go beyond events – it's your compass for shaping impactful outcomes. Are you struggling to secure continuous funding or showcase the true impact of your cultural activities? Takso is your solution.

As an outcomes planning platform, Takso allows you to define planning intent, guided by the proven Fourth Pillar framework. Whether you're curating single activities, festival programs, grant programs, or multi-activity initiatives, Takso provides a comprehensive toolkit. Say goodbye to the challenge of planning without benchmarks – Takso helps you strategically shape desired outcomes and gather evidence, making funding acquisition a breeze.

Our platform features robust tools, including Outcome Reporting and Insights, a Survey Engine, and a dedicated research library. With Takso, cultural producers worldwide have found success in demonstrating the broader impact of their initiatives, not just in Australia but across the globe. Elevate your cultural development activities with Takso – the planning and evaluation tool that transforms your vision into tangible, measurable success.

Name: Delteau
Description:
Ruimte voor morgen
Adviseurs voor natuur, water en toekomstbestendige ontwikkeling

Delteau brengt belanghebbenden samen in de slag om de ruimte. Wij adviseren in groenblauwe opgaven, opgaven met een focus op water en natuur, voor een toekomstbestendige ontwikkeling van de leefomgeving. Creatief, organisch en altijd met de opgave als leidraad.

Name: Noctua Villam
Description: Urban Sustainability; Research project in earth and agricultural science & multidisciplinary experimental development. Food, health, education and economic sustainability.

Name: V K Supreme Consultants Private Limited
Table ronde du Quartier chinois de Montréal Chinatown Round Table
Description:
La Table ronde du Quartier chinois veille à la qualité de vie, de l’environnement, à la reconnaissance et à la mise en valeur de toutes les composantes du paysage culturel du Quartier chinois de Montréal. Elle s’assure que les voix des individus et des acteurs communautaires, économiques, culturels et associatifs soient entendues, amplifiées et prises en considération, et ce, dans l’optique du développement d’un Quartier chinois à échelle humaine et inclusive. 

Formée en 2022 à la suite de l’élaboration du plan d’action 2021-2026 pour le développement de Quartier chinois de Montréal, la Table ronde est née d’un désir de protéger ce lieu patrimonial. Nous sommes une plateforme de consultation à gouvernance non-hiérarchique regroupant les 13 comités et secteurs du Quartier et nous nous engageons à travailler autour des enjeux soulevés par le Groupe de Travail sur le Quartier chinois de Montréal.

Formed in 2022 as part of the 2021-2026 Action Plan for the development of Montreal’s Chinatown, the Roundtable was established with the aim of protecting this heritage site. We are a non-hierarchical consultation platform that brings together 13 committees and sectors within Chinatown, committed to addressing the issues raised by the Montreal Chinatown Working Group.

The Montreal Chinatown Roundtable is dedicated to ensuring the quality of life, environment, recognition, and promotion of all aspects of Chinatown’s cultural landscape. We work to ensure that the voices of individuals and community, economic, cultural, and associative stakeholders are heard, amplified, and considered, all with the goal of developing a human-scaled, inclusive Chinatown.

Name: TURF&CO
Description:

Located in Rodessa, Louisiana, and aligned with the UN SDGs for balanced nutrition and sustainable communities, we’re building a legacy rooted in nature, community, and covenant living.

Core Pillars:
	•	Organic Herbal Farming
	•	Holistic Wellness Education
	•	Leadership & Cultural Preservation
	•	Farm-to-Market Innovation
	•	Social Impact & Community Development

Join us in transforming lives from the ground up. Volunteer, invest, partner, or learn—together we grow well.

Name: CADUQ Charente - Pour un Urbanisme de Qualité
Description:
L'association a pour objectif, sur l’ensemble du département de la Charente (16), de :

Promouvoir un urbanisme de qualité qui : 

favorise les mobilités quotidiennes alternatives à la voiture individuelle comme la marche, le vélo, les transports en commun,

respecte les usagers en particulier les plus fragiles (enfants, personnes âgées, personnes porteuses  de handicap, …),

tient compte des bonnes pratiques écologiques et sociales,

est conforme aux dispositions réglementaires.

Réaliser une veille territoriale sur les projets d’urbanisme en cours et à venir, et diffuser les informations concernant ces projets auprès des citoyens et de la société civile,

Faire monter en compétence les citoyens et décideurs locaux (élus, techniciens, autres maîtres d’ouvrage …) pour une conception judicieuse des aménagements, notamment pour que la sécurité et le confort des usagers de la voie publique soient systématiquement pris en compte, 	

Veiller par tous moyens légaux, et notamment par voie d’action en justice, à la bonne application des lois et règlements concernant l’urbanisme.
