Name: The Net-Zero City Project
Description:
The purpose of the Net-Zero City Project is to work with corporations to design infrastructure that has a net-zero carbon footprint. We will be answering the following questions:

•	Which technologies are the most appropriate to use, and how can they be integrated to achieve emissions reductions while ensuring reliability, affordability, and generating industry-level competitiveness for each technology? 

•	Further, how will these technologies work together across functions, and how much land, power, and resources will be required to develop and run the city?

The objectives of the Net-Zero City Project:         

•	Discovering and utilizing the newest possible clean technology available in the world today.

•	To design from scratch, infrastructure that integrates technologies to deliver net-zero emissions.

•	Develop multiple avenues for profit and sustainability into the structure and development of the city.

•	Create a comprehensive technology integration blueprint for our partners and collaborators to use in their development projects ongoingly.

Committee members will be selected from the applicants who submit their applications by the April 25, 2024 deadline, and who meet the minimum criteria below:

1.	Qualified to participate.

2.	Can afford to participate (aka, their company has enough money to put into both participating on the committee and/or panel, and to implement any technology that they are recommending be used as part of the project).

3.	The applicant’s company is at minimum partially financed, or they have received grant funding to move their projects along. 

Further information is available in the application package or on our website at www.netzerocityproject.com. 

If you and/or your company fit within these categories, or you have vendor partners who are likely to fit, please download the application form and submit your application as soon as possible. You can download the application form by visiting our website.

Name: Creative Intuitions NG
Description: Our art takes a critical view of social and cultural issues.  Often referencing contemporary history, our works explores the varying relationships between popular culture and art. Having engaged subjects as diverse as the black cultural movements, afro-pop music and street-art, our works reproduces familiar visual and aural signs, arranging them into new conceptually layered installations. While we use a variety of materials and processes in each project our methodology is consistent. Although there may not always be material similarities between the different projects they are linked by recurring formal concerns and through the subject matter.   The subject matter of each body of work determines the materials and the forms of the work.  Each project often consists of multiple works, often in a range of different media, grouped around specific themes and meanings.  During research and production new areas of interest arise and lead to the next body of work.

Name: The Soap Factory SD
Description:
The Soap Factory: More Than Just an Event Venue

At The Soap Factory, we are a vibrant community project focused on sustainable development that goes beyond hosting events. Our space offers a unique blend of culture, creativity, and sustainability through a variety of programs and features:

Artist Residency in collaboration with the Gray Area Foundations for the Arts: We provide housing and a creative space for both local and visiting artists, fostering connections with the community and encouraging artistic exchange.

Gardening Program in Partnership with Urban Corps and Miramar College: Our gardening program, developed in collaboration with Urban Corps, California Conservation Corps, and Miramar College, equips young adults with essential skills in sustainability and gardening. Participants earn 2 UC/CSU college credits upon completing this 100-hour course. The program also supplies organic ingredients to local food vendors and contributes to our farm-to-table ecosystem.

Versatile Event Spaces: With over 40,000 sq ft of indoor and outdoor space, The Soap Factory can accommodate events ranging from intimate gatherings of 50 people to large-scale events with up to 2,200 attendees. Our venue is perfect for cultural events, art shows, fundraisers, private parties, music concerts, and more.

Local Food Vendors: Our venue hosts permanent food vendors who offer health-conscious meals crafted with quality ingredients. Some of these ingredients are grown on-site, supporting a farm-to-table ecosystem.

The Garden: Featuring a 4,500-gallon aquaponics system—one of the largest in San Diego—and 15 garden beds, our garden is a cornerstone of our commitment to sustainability and community engagement.

Join us at The Soap Factory, where every visit is an opportunity to experience creativity, community, and conscious living.

Name: Naagrik Foundation for Cities
Description:

We partner with organisations who want to conduct studies with a view to influencing policy in a wide range of domains such as urban mobility, labour welfare, gender equality, intersection of technology and society and inclusive public spaces. We work with civil society organisations, government agencies, academia and the private sector. 
Our particular strengths are and high-quality mixed methods research, outstanding analysis and report writing and experienced managerial talent to execute programs on the ground. 
Our work aspires to effectively improve urban development policies in South Asia through evidence-based research.

Name: Urban Management and Governance School of Human Settlements XIM University
B-Connect
Description:
The B-CONNECT Project is an innovative initiative designed to transform the city of Bistrița (RO) into a model of sustainable urban development, focusing on the integration of nature-based solutions, advanced digital technologies, and community initiatives.
B-CONNECT is driven by the urgent need to address critical environmental issues in Bistrita. The project pioneers a multifaceted solution through a new approach: biophilic-centered tactical urbanism. It uses small-scale community-driven interventions to bring impactful changes to the urban landscape. 
Despite relevant achievements for green transition, Bistrita recognised the need for bolder approaches, aspiring to be Romania's most innovative city—a biophilic city, NBS-driven, with a real-time environmental monitoring and adaptive response system, striving for clean air and a green city, achieved through intertwining: 
PEOPLE-PLACES connection: a real-time monitoring and response digital platform, which combines hard data with community-driven insights. Residents are empowered with information about their city's environment, encouraging more eco-conscious behaviour and acting pathways.
PEOPLE-NATURE connection: actions addressing challenges like biodiversity loss and GI management through innovative approaches, such as mobile hydroponic towers and pollinator-friendly and drought-tolerant NBS, which will enhance the city's green landscape, provide cleaner air and revitalize urban spaces.
PEOPLE-PEOPLE connection: a key for urban resilience, empowering actors for decision-making and creating a SHARED and ASSUMED VISION. Urban resilience is helped by enhanced collaboration through citizen platforms, citizen reward tokens, and mobile creative hubs.

Name: Coppin Heights CDC
Description:
Since 1995, the CHCDC has worked directly with neighborhood organizations and residents of West Baltimore to promote affordable housing and economic development in a historically underinvested area of the city.

For over a decade the CHCDC has relied largely on volunteer staff and a firm coalition of local stakeholders to deliver transformative results with two major redevelopment projects: the Walbrook Mill mixed-use apartment complex at 2636 West North Avenue, and the Center for Health Care and Healthy Living, also known as the Hebrew Orphans Asylum, at 2700 Rayner Avenue.

The Coppin Heights CDC has produced substantive benefits for the local community through hard work and collaboration, winning more than $250,000 in Baltimore Neighborhood Investment (BRNI) grants from the Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development (DHCD) for distribution to area homeowners for neighborhood improvement projects.

It focus has exclusively been, and continues to be, development projects in Baltimore City. Throughout its existence, the Coppin Heights CDC has acted as a unifying force and consistent voice for communities, faith-based institutions, and small businesses during times of changing political leadership and shifting investment priorities.

Coppin Heights CDC ensures that local residents have a seat at the table in its development projects and that those projects provide long-term, direct benefits.

The CDC's development projects include local hiring and Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) participation goals. Our affordable housing projects are targeted to accommodate, not displace, current residents of the area. The commercial elements of our projects are intentionally designed to encourage use by locally-owned businesses that provide goods and services needed in the local community. 

The Coppin Heights CDC was certified as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization by the Internal Revenue Service in 1995.

Name: MEASUREit.XYZ -  Digitalizing earth surfaces & structures
Description:
A smart city starts with the creation of a 3D map covering the entire city. The map needs to remain up-to-date, detailed, and precise. It becomes a living model and digital twin, providing the data needed for confident decision-making.

3D maps with a height dimension are essential for smart city applications like: IOT, flood risk monitoring, emergency response, visualization, and for calculation of solar energy potential from roofs.

Our digital twins are created from accurate point clouds, bridging the gap with Computer Aided Design (CAD) and Building Information Modelling (BIM) work.

Whether you are creating a new subdivision or a city registry, we can deliver the needed accurate measurements from the top of the structure to the bottom. We pride ourselves in delivering data sets allowing you to confidently calculate heat loss, changes in earth volumes, or inspections on buildings. 

A digital twin will reduce overall project costs, the number of reworks and contribute to a C02 reduction. 

Digital twinning is an innovative and efficient way of doing business. 

Please contact us to discuss how MEASUREit.XYZ can put it to work for you.

The Finnish Society of Urban Planning publishes a scientific journal called “The Finnish Journal of Urban Studies” four times a year.
We have about 350 members.

The Finnish Society of Urban Planning is a member of Federation of Finnish Learned Societies.

Name: Nature in Our Hands
Description:
Nature in Our Hands is a community-driven project rooted in the belief that our local outdoor spaces are vital for learning, wellbeing, and nature connection. 

With increasing development pressures, we’re concerned about the future of cherished sites. We also see this as a valuable opportunity to pioneer approaches to outdoor learning weaving in cross-curricular subjects. 

That’s why we’re working with schools, youth voice advocates, art groups and researchers to explore how these sites are used, valued, and imagined by the community.

This science-meets-art-meets-democracy approach aims to (re-)connect residents with local green/blue sites and gather a clear mandate for their protection and enhancement.

Together, we’re building a shared vision for a space that supports biodiversity, education, and wellbeing—for today and generations to come.

Follow our journey—and tell us how YOU think we should grow!

Name: Urbanistični inštitut Republike Slovenije / Urban Planning Institute of the Republic of Slovenia
Description:
UIRS usmerja pomembno pozornost raziskovanja tudi v problematiko trajnostnega razvoja na vseh ravneh prostorskega načrtovanja in regionalnega razvoja, urbanističnega oblikovanja, krajinskega načrtovanja in oblikovanja, varstva okolja ter naravne in kulturne dediščine, stanovanjske problematike, prometa in prostorske informatike.

Inštitut si tako prizadeva poiskati možnost za izvajanje operativnih nalog (npr. case studies), ki omogočajo sprotno testiranje in implementiranje raziskovalne dosežke v konkretni urbanistični praksi.

Inštitut prav tako ponuja državnim organom strokovno znanje in svetovalne storitve pri pripravi in spreminjanju zakonodaje in drugih aktov na področju urejanja prostora.

The UIRS pays particular attention to sustainable development issues, at all levels, when conducting research in the areas of spatial planning and design, environmental protection, preservation of cultural heritage, housing, transport and spatial informatics.

UIRS thus strives to search for opportunities to conduct operational projects (e.g., case studies), which enable the simultaneous testing and implementation of research results in practice. 

UIRS also provides state institutions knowledge and consultancy services during the process of drafting of or preparing amendments to legislation and other regulations concerning spatial planning.

Description:
The Northern Network is a radical new movement powered by Groundwork that connects green community spaces across the North of England.

Shared green spaces such as community farms, parks, fields, and allotments can all help to create thriving and resilient communities. They have the potential to bring people together, enhance prospects, improve health and well-being, and take positive action on the environment.

To power a vibrant new movement across the North, this new network connects communities, places, and projects, and provides learning exchanges, peer support, training, toolkits, and more.

As a result, we’re helping to create a future where every neighborhood is vibrant and green, every community is strong and able to shape its own destiny, and no one is held back by their background or circumstances.

Name: Agenda Tevere Onlus
Description: Agenda Tevere è promotore del Contratto di Fiume Tevere.

Name: Denise Nelson Advising, LLC
Description: Denise Nelson Advising (DNA) is a small, women-owned business (SWaM certified) focused on sustainable and resilient infrastructure solutions. Built upon a background in engineering design and community development, DNA provides extensive transferable knowledge for policy analysis, planning, stakeholder engagement, and grant applications as well as AI-powered productivity improvements.

Name: Inma Holding Group
Description: Inma Holding Group specializes in Community Development and Urban Planning, emphasizing the integration of construction and environmental sustainability. With a commitment to enhancing performance and quality, the Group operates not only in the United States, Kenya, and Sudan but also maintains global connections to support its subsidiaries' success.

Name: Mega Interior and Construction Ltd
Description: Mega Interior and Construction Limited is driven by a deep sense of purpose: to build a more sustainable future. We approach our work with gratitude for the opportunity to contribute to a healthier planet, recognizing the gravitas of the challenges we face. With a profound sense of patriotism for the lands we serve, we are committed to creating regenerative environments that honor the natural beauty of our region. Our work is guided by the purity of our intentions to design and build structures that not only minimize environmental impact but also enhance human well-being. Rooted in the principles of biophilic and regenerative design, we weave natural elements into the very fabric of our projects, fostering a vital connection between people and the natural world. At Mega Interior and Construction Limited, we are passionate about building a legacy of sustainability, one eco-friendly structure at a time.

Name: Phronimos LLC
Description:
About Phronimos
Welcome to Phronimos! We are dedicated to revolutionizing urban landscapes through innovative solutions that enhance sustainability and enrich lives. Founded by Carlos Tamayo, our company is driven by a passion for leveraging data analytics and strategic planning to propel smart city initiatives.

Meet Carlos Tamayo
With a background in civil engineering and enriched by master’s degrees in Environmental Engineering and Global Smart City Management, Carlos Tamayo is a visionary leader who has left an indelible mark on the landscape of urban development.

As a LEED Accredited Professional and ENV SP certified expert, Carlos brings a wealth of expertise to the table. With over 18 years of extensive experience, Carlos has championed climate resilience, smart city planning, and project management, both in the public and private sectors.

His notable roles include serving as the Infrastructure Project Manager at the City of Miami Beach, FL, and as the Innovation Manager at the City of Colorado Springs, CO, where he spearheaded transformative Smart Cities Programs.

Join us on LinkedIn to stay updated on our latest projects and insights, and be part of our journey towards creating smarter, more sustainable cities for all.

Name: Urban Initiative Peshcom
Description:
Peshcom is an urban initiative. We are for the development of a pedestrian environment, bicycle infrastructure, and public transport in Bishkek. We want Bishkek to become an inclusive city that takes into account the needs of all people without exception. We want the capital to have more convenient, accessible, and safe public spaces. We want to live in an environmentally friendly and socially just city.

A good city should be pedestrian-oriented. And pedestrian paths should be convenient and safe for everyone - not only for healthy and strong people, but also for people with limited mobility - the elderly, parents with strollers, people with disabilities, as well as children and cyclists.

Improvement of pedestrian zones encourages walking, and this has a positive effect on the health of citizens and the environment. Planning streets with a priority on pedestrians improves road safety. Walking is economically beneficial - you do not need to pay for gasoline and get a driver's license. Pedestrian cities are attractive and inspiring, they attract tourists and investment.

We are all pedestrians – there are no people who travel exclusively by car, bus or bicycle. Every day, we at least leave our homes and walk to the bus stop or car. By making streets for pedestrians, we make streets for everyone.

The city should be convenient for people, so we want Bishkek to have safe, comfortable and pleasant ways to travel for everyone.

Name: Legendary Family of Companies
Description: Since 1972, Peter Bos is the visionary behind  Legendary. As the developer of some of the largest and most prestigious resorts in Florida, Legendary name is synonymous with full-service hospitality real estate developments, including residential, retail, resort hotels, marinas, and office projects along the Gulf Coast in Florida and Alabama, plus the Bahamas.

Name: Tampere Tunnetuksi ry
Description:
Tampere Tunnetuksi ry – The Promoting Tampere Association
Tampere City Centre as an attractive and lively place

Tampere Tunnetuksi ry (The Promoting Tampere Association) aims to increase the visibility of Tampere and its businesses and to revitalize the city center. The Association plays an important role in developing the city center together with businesses, property owners and the city of Tampere. The local businesses and the city have been promoting Tampere in close co-operation for over 50 years.

The Association was founded in 1965 and currently represents around 300 local businesses. Tampere Tunnetuksi organizes the annual events Tampere Floral Festival, and Christmas Tampere . The association is involved in organizing the competition Tampere Person of the Year.

Tampere Tunnetuksi is also putting together Tampere Tunnetuksi Pass every year. This Pass is a promotion booklet application full of fantastic offers for everyone. Association is also renting places for street vendors in Kuninkaankatu.

Would you like to be involved in developing the city center and co-operate with other entrepreneurs in the area? Find out more about our activities and get involved!

Name: WEHub | Women Entrepreneurs Hub
Description:
Community for international women entrepreneurs or those striving towards self-employment to support each other in professional and personal development via:

Networking
Sharing useful information and personal experiences
Testing ideas and getting constructive feedback on their products
Celebrating each other's wins and normalizing highs and lows of entrepreneurship
Sharing expertise where it might benefit other women in the community
Peer mentoring

In person events are currently offered in Milan. Online events are open to women entrepreneurs all over the world.

Name: Kwale Tech Hub
Description: Kwale Tech Hub is a youth-centered innovation and skills development hub based in Kwale County, Kenya. Established to bridge the digital divide, we empower young people aged 16–35 from rural and underserved communities through Information and Communication Technology (ICT) and innovation

Name: BÜRO BLAU - räume. bildung. dialoge. gGmbH
Description: Dialogue and learning processes in urban and regional development have been our passion for more than 20 years. In our dialogue projects, we attach great importance to participants getting to know different perspectives, relating to each other, and broadening their horizons through exchange—in other words, learning from each other. Our educational projects thrive on dialogue between “teachers” and ‘learners’ on an equal footing, through the sharing and reflection of experiences. We call this “Dialogue L,” learning dialogue. In this way, our fields of work enrich each other – for the benefit of cities and neighborhoods, regions and villages.

Name: Creative Impact Collaborative
Description:
Creative Impact Collaborative is a minority-owned, women-led firm that integrates public art and cultural programming into development projects and public spaces. We partner with developers, cities, and community groups to support projects from strategy to completion, creating stronger social connections, boosting economic vitality, and advancing cultural equity. Our work weaves creativity and culture into the fabric of neighborhoods, so art is never an afterthought but a catalyst for connection and belonging.
We create places where people feel seen and businesses and communities thrive. Our approach combines careful evaluation with authentic community narratives, ensuring that each project not only strengthens community pride but also delivers measurable outcomes.

Name: Civitas Urbanismo
Description: Somos uma empresa inovadora no setor de Urbanismo, comprometida com a criação de cidades mais inteligentes, verdes e resilientes. Acreditamos que o futuro das cidades passa por um planejamento que alia desenvolvimento urbano à preservação ambiental, promovendo a qualidade de vida e o bem-estar das pessoas. Estamos transformando o modo como as cidades crescem, criando espaços que são tanto eficientes quanto harmônicos com o meio ambiente.

Name: 10,000 Communities Initiative
Description: The 10,000 Communities Initiative connects people, projects, and partners who understand that creating resilient communities and building equitable economies takes new initiative. This cross-sectoral coalition aims to support effective infrastructure deployment in 10,000 communities nationwide.

Name: Into Property Pty Ltd
Description:
Into Property Pty Ltd is a property development company founded by Jason Grant in 2011, proudly holding a track record of delivering high-quality product with outstanding asset returns for our partners and investors.

Into property key strengths span across multiple property sectors inclusive of residential, commercial, industrial, retail, agriculture, tourism, and childcare facilities. Considered advice, collaborative leadership and attention to detail deliver our innovative solutions and drive success. 

At the core of our business is our strategy. We work with likeminded partners to ensure all our projects are conceptualised, developed and delivered in line with our company values to achieve specific financial, social, and environmental outcomes.

Our vision has been to rethink traditional property development and embrace a more meaningful approach to projects. One which delivers real benefits with valuable contributions to our stakeholders and our communities. 

Underpinning each project is our set of values, to act as if we ourselves are the recipient, be responsible in what we do and to think about our impact on the world around us. At the core we aim to be mindful of the environment and our legacy we leave behind. Our commitment to these values guides our team to strive to do more, achieve better outcomes both socially and environmentally, and create communities which are not only profitable but strive for operational excellence. 

To achieve our vision, we actively embrace new ideas and approaches to place creation and innovation whilst working towards a more sustainable future. 

Current projects include: 
- https://www.barnslanefarm.com.au
- https://breakfastcreekquarter.com.au/

Name: CENTRO HISTÓRICO DE PONTA DELGADA - Bairro Comercial Digital
Description: Desenvolver no Centro Histórico de Ponta Delgada, um espaço que promova o comércio tradicional e os serviços existentes, mas que também crie as condições infraestruturais e tecnológicas para atrair novos empreendedores que alarguem a oferta, com produtos e serviços adaptados aos comportamentos e necessidades dos digital buyers através da revitalização, digitalização e modernização do espaço público e aliando a cultura, o património e a sustentabilidade

Name: Asociación de Estudios Urbanísticos y Territoriales Teatinos - AEUTT
Description:
El ámbito territorial en el que la asociación va a desarrollar principalmente sus actividades es el de la Comunidad Autónoma de Andalucía, siendo su ámbito de trabajo la Ordenación del Territorio, el Urbanismo y el Medio Ambiente así como la Administración y Gestión de las Ciudades y los Territorios. Los fines de la Asociación en este ámbito son los siguientes:

1. Debatir y reflexionar sobre el urbanismo, la ordenación del territorio y la sostenibilidad ambiental.
2. Impulsar el carácter interdisciplinario del urbanismo y la ordenación territorial.
3. Articular e impulsar la participación, la práctica, la formación y la investigación sobre el urbanismo.
4. Suscribir convenios y acuerdos con Universidades y con cuantas entidades públicas y privadas, estudios profesionales, órganos de difusión y comunicación y cualesquiera otras sean beneficiosas para la Asociación.
5. Tutelar a interesados/as, licenciados/as, graduados/as o estudiantes en la formación y la práctica, en los objetivos de la presente Asociación.
6. Apoyar tesis doctorales, documentos de investigación, estudios y prácticas relacionadas con los objetivos de la Asociación.
7. Cooperar con la sociedad asumiendo una postura reivindicativa en defensa del bienestar y la ética de la sociedad urbana y rural, recurriendo, interpelando o reclamando las medidas que así consideren las personas asociadas.
8. Colaborar con la Administración, y con la sociedad en general, para que el urbanismo sea entendido como un conocimiento de convivencia y desarrollo de las ciudades y de sus habitantes.
9. Mejorar la credibilidad y la ética de la actividad urbanística.
10. Impulsar ideas que promuevan un nuevo urbanismo más cercano a las necesidades y problemas de la sociedad actual.
10. Investigar opciones y características propias más acordes con el desarrollo del espacio rural, con el espacio de “no ciudad”.
11. Difundir periódicamente las reflexiones y debates que se vayan haciendo de forma habitual en el Foro Teatinos.

Name: Landmarks Planning Strategies
Description: Landmarks Planning Strategies Ltd. facilitates heritage projects and initiatives aimed at creating community landmarks. Our team collaborates with you to understand the unique connection your project has to the community. We identify potential challenges and develop strategies to ensure the success of both your project and the community it serves.

Name: UrbanVerse Planning & Design
Description:
Welcome to UrbanVerse Planning & Design. Based in NSW, Australia, we are a dedicated town planning and design consultancy with a comprehensive suite of services designed to simplify the planning process for our diverse clients.

Our services include Preliminary Planning Assessment, Architectural Plans for Council, Preparation of Statement of Environmental Effects, and Preparation and Lodgement of Development Applications. We also represent clients at Council Meetings, provide expert Planning Proposal Preparation, and carry out Development Assessments for Councils.

Our aim is to simplify the town planning process for property developers, making it clear, streamlined, and effortless. We work closely with our clients, ensuring their interests are represented, and their projects align with both regulatory guidelines and community expectations. We're not just your service provider but a trusted partner in creating sustainable, vibrant spaces that enhance community well-being.

Name: Øyer Næringsråd
Description: Vi er en nøytral og upolitisk interesseorganisasjon som jobber for å fremme næringslivsutvikling i Øyer!

Name: Vantage Developers
Description:
At Vantage Developers, we specialize in crafting exceptional residential and commercial real estate projects. With over 25 years of experience, we have been shaping landscapes and creating thriving communities that reflect our commitment to excellence, transparency, and collaboration.

Our mission is to deliver unparalleled value to our clients by blending innovative designs, meticulous planning, and sustainable practices. From conceptualization to completion, we ensure every project embodies quality, integrity, and trust.

Join us in our journey to redefine real estate and build a future that inspires.

Name: Cheeks
Description: With Kitchen Cheeks, we come together weekly, taking over unused restaurants, to cook a plant-based meal with vegetables from local farmers and learning about best professional kitchen practices. Rooted in the Slow Food, Arts and DIY movement, we tackle the hyperconsumption of ready-made meals by celebrating the craft of cooking ourselves, reusing plastic-free packaging, learning about fermentation, and sharing any leftovers.

Name: Enviroplan Consulting Ltd.
Description: Enviroplan Consulting Limited provides for integrated planning and ecological services, to accompany a wide range of planning projects, in the interest of delivering balanced and sustainable development proposals. Advice and guidance is provided by suitably qualified and experienced personnel in town planning and ecology. We specialise in planning applications and appeals.

Name: ANDRE PLANER

Name: LOIART
Description:
We transform the territories along the Loire into an open-air art gallery, combining contemporary creation with exceptional natural heritage. Our mission is to give artists the opportunity to confront their works and the transformation of materials with the extraordinary natural environment in which they will be installed.

Artists and their works contribute to enhancing the territories along the Loire by creating attractive and innovative artistic routes. We promote local artisanal expertise by involving craftsmen in the creation of the artworks, and we foster professional integration through training workshops in art-related trades.

By raising public awareness of contemporary art and environmental preservation, we bring together artists, local authorities, businesses, and residents around an ambitious cultural project. Together, we make art a vector for economic, social, and tourism development along the Loire.

With LOIART, art is a celebration in motion! Join the LOIART adventure and partake in the creative renaissance of our territories!

Name: Syndicat mixte du SCoT du Biterrois
Description: 87 communes, 5 établissements de coopération intercommunales, 280 000 habitants

Name: StreetWeave
Description:
Our streamlined process ensures that your new roads are added to maps in an average of just 10 days. We use our expertise in plat maps, mapping software, and shapefiles to provide a seamless experience. Our easy-to-use online ordering system means no more complicated contracts or endless paperwork. Simply place your order, submit your plat maps, and let us handle the rest.

At StreetWeave, we pride ourselves on competitive pricing without unnecessary add-ons. We also submit your road data to multiple mapping providers, including Google, Apple, and OSM, ensuring comprehensive coverage and maximum visibility for your development.

Name: Habitat.CoLab
Description:
Circular construction doesn’t need more ideas. It needs people who bring them to life.

That's where we come in. Habitat.CoLab turns circular visions into reality.

Accelerating the construction turnaround is complex. With over 7 years of experience in the circular economy, we know that the right strategy and targeted collaboration are the keys to closing the gap between great ideas and tangible results.

We are not a traditional consultancy; we are a laboratory for concrete implementation. We are built on three central pillars to bring projects to fruition quickly and effectively:

1. The Project Collective (Our Expertise) We operate with an agile, multidisciplinary team of experts who have deep implementation experience. This collective unites specialists across circular strategy, regulatory support, and social acceptance, proving that even competitors can cooperate to develop solutions with measurable impact.

2. The Laboratory (Our Method) Habitat.CoLab is not a glossy promise. We are committed to the highest level of professionalism, delivering effective, data-driven results that drive real change. We test, refine, and deploy solutions that work in practice, not just on paper.

3. The HabitatIQ Advantage (Our Edge) As a Habitat.CoLab customer, you benefit from exclusive access to HabitatIQ (coming soon)—a global, AI-based circularity hub. This "door opener" puts your projects on the most innovative footing, providing a unique advantage with access to cutting-edge tools, data, and analyses for healthy, equitable, and circular living spaces.

Anyone who works with Habitat.CoLab becomes part of a movement: creating projects that have a local impact, are globally networked, and accelerate the building revolution for the long term.

Name: Stirling Bridge Developments
Description:
Stirling Bridge Developments is a visionary property development firm based in Ballito, KwaZulu-Natal. Rooted in the greater Compensation area, we specialise in transforming land into vibrant, sustainable, mixed-use precincts. Our approach is not transactional, it’s transformational.

Operating across residential, retail, education, commercial, industrial, and leisure sectors, we focus on unlocking land use, delivering essential infrastructure, and shaping integrated developments that serve both local communities and regional economies.

Name: San Luis Valley Local Foods Coalition
Description: The San Luis Valley Local Foods Coalition (LFC) is a nonprofit dedicated to creating a sustainable local foods system that is accessible and affordable to families in the San Luis Valley (SLV) in rural southern Colorado. Major programs include: Rio Grande Farm Park, Valley Roots Food Hub, Cooking Matters classes, and Local Foods Local Places.

Name: SYNDICAT MIXTE DU PAYS VALLEE DE LA SARTHE
Description: SYNDICAT MIXTE DU PAYS VALLEE DE LA SARTHE est le regroupement de 3 Communautés de Communes : Val de Sarthe, LBN Communauté et Pays sabolien. Il regroupe 62 communes et environ 78 000 habitants. Le Pays est compétent pour l'animation du plan climat (PCAET) et du SCOT.

Name: Agence Régionale de Développement (ARD) de Kédougou
Montclair Bike Bus
Description:
We’re Montclair parents and community supporters who want to make it joyful, fun, and safe to bike to school together. With creativity and courage, we’re determined to make our roads accessible to active transportation. Our hope is that the bike bus route is utilized not just during school hours but offers popular routes for all to enjoy. 

WHAT WE DO
+ Build community support in town and schools
+ Create rider tools, time tables, and routes 
+ Host events and gatherings
+ Advise on transportation infrastructure redesign

Name: URBE PLAN 21
Description:
URBE PLAN 21 is an design and planning firm with offices in Bucharest and Oradea. We develop urban planning documents such as P.U.G., P.U.Z. and P.U.D. 
The company is specialized in approving urban planning documents, knowing all the details and requirements requested by the authorities.
The most important clients develop retail projects, large shopping centers, but also residential and logistic/industrial developments.
URBE PLAN 21 has learned how design can help simplify and smoothen complex urban developments, and, through that, to create space for others to engage in the city. URBE PLAN 21 has extensive experience in designing the interrelationship between the city and its infrastructure.

Name: Getrache
Description:
Getrache - Empowering Communities, Transforming Lives

At Getrache, we are dedicated to sustainable development and positive societal impact. As a leading organization, we focus on multifaceted initiatives, ranging from sustainable infrastructural development to empowering marginalized communities.

 Our Mission:
Empowering communities through sustainable solutions, bridging gender gaps, and fostering inclusive growth. We believe in creating lasting change by addressing the root causes of societal challenges.

 Key Focus Areas:
- Sustainable Infrastructural Development
- Gender Equality and Women's Empowerment
- Water, Sanitation, and Health Initiatives
- Climate Change Mitigation
- Community Education and Empowerment

 Water for All, Empowerment for Everyone:
Our projects extend beyond providing clean water; they empower communities to thrive. From nature-based flood solutions to sustainable urban development, we are driving positive change.

 Empowering Women, Transforming Futures:
We empower marginalized women economically, fostering self-sufficiency, and breaking barriers. Our commitment to ending gender inequality shapes transformative projects.

 Building Resilient Communities:
Getrache is at the forefront of building resilient communities equipped to face the challenges of today and tomorrow. Our strategies, rooted in collaboration and innovation, aim for sustainable impact.

 Join the Getrache Movement:
We invite passionate individuals, collaborators, and change-makers to join hands in shaping a better future. Together, we can create a world where every community thrives, every woman is empowered, and sustainability is at the core of our progress.

#Getrache #Sustainability #CommunityEmpowerment #GenderEquality #ImpactfulChange

Name: Queanbeyan Microforest
Description:
A community initiative to build a Micro-Forest, food forest and nature play area in suburban Queanbeyan in 2022-23.

Successfully raised over $38,000 during our crowdfunding campaign, utilising our capabilities in partnership building, business development, marketing, negotiation, and event management. 

This funding has enabled us to enter the community consultation and co-design phase before construction begins in Spring 2023.

This community-led micro-forest project aims to reduce urban heat and provide habitat for native wildlife, while also creating a beautiful and inviting green space for locals to meet, play, and relax. 

As the first of its kind in Queanbeyan, we aim to not only positively impact the environment but also have positive social outcomes for our community. 

Through inclusive engagement and design, we hope to inspire others to follow in our footsteps and create similar projects that prioritise the well-being of local stakeholders, the environment as well as the local economy.

Name: Resilient Urban Planning and Development (RUPD)
Description:
Resilient Urban Planning+Development (RUPD) GbR is a company based in Düsseldorf, Germany founded by Parisa Kloss in 2013 to provide architectural design, urban planning as well as training and capacity building services to local communities, governmental and private sectors, NGOs and academic entities in national and international levels.
 
The main goal of RUPD is to integrate climate change adaptation to extreme weather events into urban planning and design process. RUPD helps to upgrade urban planning strategies and buildings’ performance according to future climate scenarios. It strives to accomplish projects with the best conditions for a resilient and long-lasting development. In order to achieve the goal effectively, an interconnected approach to the unique and dynamic changes is applied.
 
The idea of RUPD is to respect local traditions and their identities as well as long-term partnership, strategic planning, communication, and commitment. According to these ideas, it tries to involve local and international partners equally to not only create answers to the complex and continuous changes of the urban environment, but also benefit each group with a “Know-How” transfer. Working together with these partners enables us to create and elaborate innovative and practical solutions in a way that they can be replicated on a broader scale.
 
We try to create an innovative method for effective communication and sustained engagement between local, national and global actors. We develop a common language for the different actors and help thereby to improve the internal and external communication professionally.
 
In our collaborations, we are committed to our partners and the communities affected by the problems on a long-term basis.

Name: UrbCitizenPower
Description:
Cities across Europe today face growing environmental, social, and economic challenges. UrbCitizenPower supports local and regional governments in rethinking policies by putting citizen empowerment at the heart of change. The project focuses on improving policies related to green spaces, culture, creativity, and public space – always with citizens as co-creators of their cities. Through interregional learning, thematic working groups, and local urban living labs, the project fosters collaboration between governments, citizens, and stakeholders.

Led by the Heinrich Böll Foundation Schleswig-Holstein, the partnership brings together Braga (PT), Groningen (NL), Kiel (DE), Guldborgsund (DK), Pori (FI), Košice (SK), and the Region of Central Macedonia (GR). Together, they exchange knowledge, develop innovative approaches, and strengthen social cohesion in their communities.

The project UrbCitizenPower: Empower citizens for agency as driver for change in cities is co-funded by the Interreg Europe Programme 2021-2027 of the European Union. The project runs from April 2024 to June 2028.

Name: DI Development Company
Description:
DI Development Company, based in Charleston, South Carolina, is a management and development services provider for master-planned communities. 

Our role extends beyond the building of infrastructure, residential and commercial product and amenities. It is the creation of a true and lasting sense of place that yields the greatest dividends for the stakeholders in the communities we help develop. This is always our ultimate goal.

Our in-house team consists of sales and marketing experts, land planners, project managers and accounting professionals. We also retain long-standing relationships with other key partners, web developers and other - who are an integral part of our team.

Name: Walk 'n' Roll San Diego
Description:
Follow us on Instagram & TikTok! @WalknRollSD

We walk San Diego and gather support to remake our streets into safe and inspiring places to all who walk and roll.

Name: Association pour le Développement du Village de Boukousséra (ADV-BRA)
Description:
L’Association pour le Développement du Village de Boukousséra (ADV-BRA) se veut une association solidaire, pourvoyeur d’emploi, capable de promouvoir un cadre d’émancipation et d’épanouissement moral nécessaire, base de tout développement socio-culturel et économique durable.

Elle a pour valeurs (Foi, Dignité, Travail) :
La foi : est la fidélité aux lois de la sincérité, de l'honnêteté qui offre l’assurance d’un cadre de développement durable. En effet, la foi chrétienne qui régit la population de ce village, favorise un cadre d’épanouissement morale et intellectuel, ainsi que la fraternité et la tolérance de ces citoyens.
La dignité : est cette noblesse qui inspire la considération, commande le respect et les égards. Dans cette optique tout individu est libre de mettre en œuvre ses valeurs pour un meilleur développement.
Le travail : grâce à la foi, la dignité et la détermination favorisent les activités socio-culturelles et économiques pour un développement durable. Ce village constitué de jeunes ayant un esprit de travail, sont très dynamique et dévoués pour leur réussite dans tous les domaines.

Cette association a pour objectifs de :
	Promouvoir l’éducation, l’alphabétisation, l’auto emploi et le maintien des élèves et étudiants ressortissants de Boukousséra dans le système éducatif.
	Améliorer la gouvernance des ressources naturelles protégées pour des générations à venir ;
	Participer à la sécurité économique, alimentaire et nutritionnelle de la communauté de Boukousséra.

Name: Ministry of Buildings, Utilities, Infrastructure, Labour and Transportation
Description:
Overview of Ministry:
The Ministry of Communications, Works, Labour & Energy is a vibrant Ministry whose strategic objective is to provide technical expertise in Infrastructure Services, Labour Force, Sea and Air access, Energy Services, ICTs, Equipment & Mechanical Services, and transportation. It’s main aim is to contribute to economic growth and development and also to contribute to the national outcomes of Montserrat in the following ways:

 A vibrant and diverse economy that supports sustainable private sectoral economic activity and generates employment;
~Improve energy security;
~Physical infrastructure and transportation facilities in place to support development (Infrastructure investment that are economic enablers, which are interlinked with and are vital for the success of many of the proposed investments in the visitor economy, enterprise culture, and key sectors of growth in Montserrat.)

Vision:
To be an organization providing excellent services to Montserrat, epitomizing a model to the region whilst working within a harmonious environment.

Mission:
The Ministry of Communication, Works, Labour & Energy (MCWLE) is mandated to promote the goals and objectives of Government of Montserrat; by ensuring the enhancement of the quality of life for its residents through delivery of safe, reliable, cost effective and sustainable projects, programmes and quality services in the Communications, Labour, Infrastructure and Energy Sectors.

Name: CHARLES VILLAGE COMMUNITY BENEFITS DISTRICT MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY
Description: Welcome to CVCBD, where our legacy of three decades converges with innovation. We champion Community Power through engaged advocacy, elevated standards, and diverse partnerships. Every resident, homeowner, small business owner, student, and volunteer—over 800 businesses and 11,000 members strong—shapes our vibrant Charles Village tapestry. Join us in amplifying the authentic voice of our community, where progress and empowerment intersect.

Name: Hungarian Urban Development Association
Description: Urban Development Association is the community of professionals dedicated to regional and urban development.

Name: Alloway Railway Tunnel
Description:
We want to transform an old, dilapidated tunnel in Alloway, Scotland, that has been defaced with graffiti into a safe, inspiring place, with a beautiful hand painted mural by one of the UK’s top muralists and visionaries. 

We are a Scottish charity with seven trustees from a variety of backgrounds, engineering, project management, banking and finance, social work, law, marketing and business. 

We all care about improving our community by enhancing this walk/cycle path. We want to engage with young people to help them learn new skills by giving them exciting opportunities to get involved with our project.

Why does this project matter? It matters because Alloway is an idyllic village in South Ayrshire with worldwide recognition as the birthplace of Robert Burns. We are proud of this legacy and want to ensure residents and visitors have the best experience when exploring our village. Improving the tunnel through an amazing art installation will enhance that experience and help us tell the story not just about Robert Burns, but about the history of Ayrshire. 
The mural will have interactive elements so that visitors can learn about the history of Ayrshire. It will be a celebration of our heritage and provide a lasting legacy. People will love the new space and want to revisit time and time again. 
By improving our cycle path through the village, we hope to encourage more people to enjoy cycling and walking and experience all the wellbeing benefits this brings. 
We will be visiting schools and youth groups to involve children in art workshops. They will learn creative skills through an educational process. Their work will be used to help create this massive mural and will create a sense of ownership and pride.
We will also be running creative workshops for adults who want to help paint the mural. We know it will be of interest to local art clubs, but we want it to be accessible to anyone who has an interest in helping to create the mural. It’s going to bring people

Raum- & Stadtplanung:

Umweltprüfungen:

Freiraumplanung:

 Mehr dazu: co3.lu/freiraumplanung

Mit über 25 Jahren Erfahrung sowie einem interdisziplinären Ansatz begleiten wir Planungsprozesse und setzen nachhaltige Lösungen für für lebenswerte Orte um.

Name: RARE - Remote Areas Revitalization through digital Engagement
Description:
RARE (Remote Areas Revitalization through digital Engagement) is a European project born to address one of the most pressing challenges for Europe’s future: revitalizing rural areas.

With 83% of the European population expected to live in cities by 2050, small rural towns face depopulation, economic decline, and cultural erosion. At the same time, the rise of remote work opens up new possibilities: today, living far from major urban centers is not only viable, but also sustainable and appealing.

RARE leverages this shift to transform marginal territories into attractive hubs for digital nomads, freelancers, and creative workers through training, co-design processes, digital innovation, and residency programs.

Active in four rural communities across Germany, Spain, Croatia, and Italy, the project:
• Trains local leaders to become change-makers
• Directly involves communities in the co-design of new services
• Launches a digital platform to connect territories and nomads
• Hosts professionals in temporary rural residencies to foster exchange and local innovation

Co-funded by the European Commission, RARE is implemented by a network of six partners with complementary expertise in local development, communication, social innovation, education, and participatory governance: Gespa e.V., KORAK, Miceli Rural Coop, europAledro, Lab Futura and TREE
Agency.

RARE is more than a project: it’s a European laboratory for reimagining a future where rural communities lead the change.

Our mission is to revolutionize how people interact with their environment by seamlessly fusing cutting-edge technology and innovative design with the elegance of scalable, modern eco-architecture and refined style, embracing the principles of sustainable living and a decentralized, circular economy.

Name: Proyecto Virrilá
Description:
El Estuario de Virrilá es una joya ecológica de la Costa Peruana, ha sido acreditado como Sitio Ramsar. Está ubicado en Sechura, Piura, posee una gran biodiversidad local y continental: recibe a miles de aves playeras del sur y norte de América, flamencos del sur americano, tortugas marinas desde Galápagos y otras especies del Bosque Seco y mar cercano. Lamentablemente, esta joya ecológica viene siendo degradada.

Proyecto Virrilá desarrolla propuestas de conservación ecológica, turísmo sostenible innovador y mejoramiento de actividades agropecuarias en favor del Estuario y sus habitantes, mediante proyectos de diseño paisajísticos y arquitectónicos sostenibles. El proyecto busca ser una plataforma concreta de propuestas que complemente los esfuerzos participativos de conservación ecológica que se han venido realizando por diversos actores.

La apuesta esencial de Proyecto Virrilá es reafianzarnos en el potencial de los proyectos de arquitectura, en este caso paisajístico y territorial, como plataforma de beneficios e involucramiento político-social para visionar con los pies en la tierra soluciones a grandes problemas de la sociedad, como la defensa ecológica del estuario. 

Proyecto Virrilá es una iniciativa que ha sido seleccionada y financiada por el Programa Soluciones Costeras del Cornell Lab of Ornithology. El proyecto es liderado por el Arq. Gustavo Diaz Paz, y cuenta como colaboradores al Blgo. Frank Suarez y a un equipo de diseñadores con enfoque ecológico. La institución local anfitriona del proyecto es el Centro de Investigación de la Arquitectura y la Ciudad de la PUCP.

Pueden escribirnos a la siguiente dirección de correo electrónico: proyectovirrila@gmail.com

Name: Stadt und Land München Ost e.V. - OstAllianz
Description:
Wer sind wir?
Die Gemeinden Anzing, Aschheim, Feldkirchen, Finsing, Forstinning, Haar, Kirchheim b. München, Pliening, Poing, Vaterstetten und der Markt Markt Schwaben haben gemeinsam mit der Stadt München ein räumliches Entwicklungskonzept erarbeitet, das Maßnahmen zur Förderung der Lebensqualität, wirtschaftlichen Entwicklung und der überörtlichen
Verkehrsplanung der Region vorsieht. Um die Zusammenarbeit langfristig zu sichern, haben sich die Kommunen im September 2022 auf die Gründung eines Vereins geeinigt. Der Verein bietet einen institutionellen Rahmen für eine effektive und dauerhafte Zusammenarbeit der Region.

Was haben wir vor?
Der Verein Stadt und Land München Ost e.V., kurz OstAllianz, ist Plattform für Mitglieder, Interessierte und Partner des Vereins, um die interkommunale Zusammenarbeit in der Region zu stärken und die Stadt-Umland Beziehung zu fördern. Er dient als Forum für den Austausch und die Koordination von Aktivitäten zwischen den Mitgliedsgemeinden. Es sollen auf Grundlage
des „überörtlichen Verkehrskonzeptes Raum München Ost“ gemeinsame Projekte und Maßnahmen initiiert, Informationen ausgetauscht und Lösungen für regionale Herausforderungen erarbeitet werden.

Wie sind wir organisiert?
Der Verein wird inhaltlich durch einen gewählten Vorstand geführt. Dieser stimmt seine Ziele in einer zweimal jährlich stattfindenden Vollversammlung mit allen Mitgliedern ab. Die Geschäftsstelle ist die zentrale Anlaufstelle für alle Vereinsangelegenheiten, darunter Mitgliederverwaltung, Organisation von Veranstaltungen, Öffentlichkeitsarbeit, Koordination von Projekten und Zusammenarbeit mit Partnern und Stakeholdern.

Varför välja WR GRUPPEN?

Fastighetsutveckling: Vårt team har ett skarpt öga för affärsmöjligheter och skapar värdefulla fastighetsprojekt. Genom att vara ansvariga för hela processen, inklusive projektutveckling, ägande och fastighetsförvaltning, blir helheten tydligare och mer sammanhållen.

Samhällsbyggnad: Vi förstår vikten av att skapa livskraftiga samhällen och arbetar nära myndigheter och lokalsamhällen för att forma en hållbar framtid.

Järnväg: Vi erbjuder resurser inom projektledning, byggledning, besiktning och produktion inom järnvägssektorn. Våra konsulter är specialiserade inom järnvägsprojekt och arbetar för att säkerställa smidiga projektledningsprocesser, effektiv byggledning och noggranna besiktningar. Vårt team är dedikerat till att alltid upprätthålla högsta standard och leveranser.

Affärsutveckling: Vi driver framgångsrika affärsprojekt genom noggrann marknadsanalys, strategisk planering och kundfokuserade lösningar för att säkerställa långsiktig tillväxt och hållbarhet.

Engagemang och ansvar: Oavsett storlek, bakgrund eller förutsättningar, ser vi varje kund som en unik möjlighet. Vi tar ansvar för helheten och strävar efter att underlätta och överträffa dina förväntningar.

Description:
Съюз на урбанистите  в България е създаден през 2010 година с желанието да се превърне в професионална общност и платформа на идеи и инициативи за развитието на заобикалящата ни среда. СУБ работи за обединяването на специалистите и организациите (и въобще всички онези, които по призвание или необходимост работят в сферата на урбанизма) в мрежа за обмен на опит и търсене на решения по проблемите на планирането, градската среда и професионалното развитие.  СУБ приема за своя мисия промотирането и прилагането на най-добрите съвременни практики и подходи, които да допринасят за изграждането на по-добра жизнена среда, в центъра на която са хората и местните общности.
Още от създаването си, СУБ има амбицията да предлага специализирана информация и разнообразни инициативи, свързани с широкото поле на урбанизма. Съюзът организира и съдейства за провеждането на отворени лекции, работилници за идеи, конкурси, конференции, професионални срещи. В последните две години е сред съорганизаторите на Световния ден на урбанизма, който по традиция в България се отбелязва на 8 ноември в Университета по архитектура, строителство и геодезия.

Name: Alliance ARIANE
Description:
L’alliance ARIANE travaille pour que l’aménagement du territoire et l’urbanisme soient considérés comme une priorité. Son objectif principal est que le Québec se dote, dans le cadre d’une Politique nationale de l’aménagement du territoire et de l’urbanisme, d’une vision d’ensemble assortie de principes fondamentaux qui puisse assurer la coordination de l’ensemble des lois, politiques et interventions de l’État et des instances municipales en matière d’aménagement du territoire et d’urbanisme.

Pourquoi ARIANE ?
Dans la mythologie grecque, Ariane est la fille de Minos, roi de Crète. Minos a commandé à Dédale la construction du Labyrinthe pour y enfermer le Minotaure, monstre mi-taureau mi-humain. Le Minotaure dévore régulièrement un tribut de jeunes garçons et filles d’Athènes. Thésée, fils d’Égée (roi d’Athènes), se joint au tribut dans le but de tuer le Minotaure : mais il lui faudra, ensuite, parvenir à sortir du Labyrinthe. Il obtient l’aide d’Ariane (avec un petit coup de main de Dédale) qui lui remet une pelote de fil à dérouler depuis l’entrée pour pouvoir trouver le chemin du retour. Grâce à ce fil, Thésée peut retrouver son chemin et sortir du Labyrinthe après avoir tué le Minotaure.

Aujourd’hui, on parle de « fil d’Ariane » pour évoquer :
- une ligne directrice, une conduite à tenir pour atteindre un objectif;
un moyen de se diriger au milieu des difficultés, un guide entre deux points difficiles à relier;
- une voie à suivre pour arriver à un résultat difficile à atteindre.

Name: The International Federation of Arab Businessmen and Investors Abroad الاتحاد الدولي لرجال الأعمال
Description:
The International Federation of Arab Businessmen and Investors Abroad (IFBI) is an independent international economic entity established in 2013, with the mission of strengthening economic cooperation, trade exchange, and investment integration among Arab and African countries.

IFBI works to support Arab businessmen and investors residing abroad by providing a comprehensive platform that connects them with investment opportunities, strategic partners, governmental institutions, embassies, and international organizations. The Federation aims to maximize the value of its members’ expertise, capabilities, and business networks, enabling them to expand their activities regionally and internationally while contributing effectively to sustainable economic development.

Through its branches, regional offices, and representative offices across various countries, IFBI facilitates access to new markets, supports export and localization initiatives, and assists members in overcoming regulatory and administrative challenges. The Federation also plays an active role in organizing and participating in international conferences, investment forums, exhibitions, and economic summits that promote private sector growth and cross-border cooperation.

A key initiative of IFBI is the “International Industrial Localization Initiative,” which encourages Arab and African investors to transfer and localize industries in host countries, enhancing local production, creating job opportunities, and strengthening industrial value chains.

IFBI is managed through a well-structured governance model that includes a Board of Directors, a Secretary General, an Executive Director, and an Advisory Board specializing in international relations and diplomacy. This structure ensures transparency, efficiency, and effective representation of the Federation at national and international levels.

By fostering partnerships, supporting entrepreneurship, promoting innovation, and enhancing competitiveness, IFBI

Name: Hertel Planning
Description:
Hertel Planning is a small and nimble Toronto-based city planning firm. We help our clients create and carry out plans for land use, housing, and transportation. We work at all scales—regional, city, town, neighbourhood, corridor, and block—and across a range of disciplines. We want to be known for:

• Putting people first: at the heart of it, planning is about people: we need to hear what people tell us needs to be fixed, and then make sure that our work helps. 
• Thoughtful analysis: we think things through, letting the unique opportunities and challenges of each project guide our approach.
• Clear communication: we tell it like it is, and how it can be, in a way that can be understood by pretty much everyone.
• Useful advice: we believe that a plan is good only if it can be followed and achieved. If you can’t get there, then there’s no point.

Our work is based on three pillars: ‘conhecer’ as of collectively learning about our cities ‘imaginar’ as of radically rethinking our cities, and ‘brincar’ as of taking ownership of the urban spaces through art, culture and collective action. 
We believe in the power of connecting multiple local experiences to understand the dynamics that shape our cities, therefore we are particularly interested in collaborations beyond Lisbon.

Name: POSEIDON
Description:
We connect small and medium-sized companies and municipalities in the Danish-German region through partnerships, knowledge exchange and innovation collaboration. 

When climate change increases the amount of water, the solution is no longer to drain it away, as sewers, streams and groundwater will be high. In Poseidon, we look at what we can do to prepare for water-related disasters instead of just reacting to them. We must dare to invite water in, be creative and try different things than what we usually do.

The Poseidon project is a collaboration between Danish and German universities, companies and public organisations. The project receives approximately EUR 1.875.000 millions in funding from Interreg Deutschland-Danmark with funding from the European Union and runs until March 2027.

Each CitiPod combines thoughtful design with social purpose — locally built, easy to install, and integrated into the urban fabric through partnerships with cities, corporates, and communities.

Name: Streets for Citizens Project
Description:
The project, operating within the framework of the Interreg Euro-MED program and under the mission of Promoting green living areas, addresses two primary challenges:

High rate of car ownership, traffic, and road danger
Scarce quantity and/or quality of spaces for greenery and community activities
To achieve a behavioral shift towards sustainable urban mobility and greener streets, the project focuses on raising awareness and influencing citizen behavior. The primary objective of the Streets for Citizens project is to empower the public sector and related entities to engage citizens effectively and increase their commitment to addressing mobility and public space challenges in functional urban areas. The ultimate goal is to foster more active citizen participation in interventions aimed at enhancing urban sustainability. The project will develop a tactical urbanism methodology, leveraging existing knowledge, to design territorial roll-up plans. These plans will outline long-term strategies and demonstration actions tailored to the specific needs of each partner country. By implementing pilots and learning from the outcomes, the partnership will develop and disseminate scalable solutions and policy recommendations.

Name: PlanHeat
Description:
The heating sector, Europe-wide and in the Baltic Sea Region (BSR), has huge impact on climate change as it accounts for a large share of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. One lever to positively steer the developments in this area is “local heat planning (LHP)”, an instrument to depict sustainable and economically viable transformation paths and measures towards a GHG-neutral heat supply at municipal level. The EU Energy Efficiency Directive (EED) recast 2023 now includes a new provision, which requires Member States to ensure that municipalities above 45,000 inhabitants prepare local heat plans that respond to increased EU obligations in the upcoming years. However, many BSR municipalities are not prepared for this extensive task, they lack know-how and resources for LHP as well as the awareness for their long-term responsibilities in decarbonising their local heat sector. PlanHeat addresses this challenge by bringing together municipalities and expert organisations from seven BSR countries that jointly develop a transnational manual on how to prepare local heat plans. This guiding manual comprises five sub-solutions on the topics data access, technologies, staff and skills, stakeholder participation as well as financial and organizational solutions for LHP. Relevant target groups like heat suppliers, energy consultants or ministries responsible for energy and climate policies will be strongly engaged in the project. The PlanHeat manual will be disseminated all over the BSR.

PlanHeat is a project funded by the Interreg Baltic Sea Region Programme 2021-2027.

Walkable streets, locally-owned shops, and inviting green spaces foster a sense of belonging where residents and visitors alike can gather and enjoy life’s simple pleasures. It’s a place where history and innovation blend seamlessly, creating a small town that feels both timeless and new.

Name: Creating Whole Communities
Description:
Creating Whole Communities, formed in 2014, mobilizes the assets of the University of Missouri System to strengthen Missouri communities and build civic capacity.

What is Civic Capacity? The collective strength of people in a place to respond to challenges and pursue thriving, whole communities.

We define a whole community as one that:
-Fosters a strong sense of belonging through social capital
-Activates all residents to contribute through community decision-making and civic process
-Generates civic leaders with a collective approach toward collaboration, action, and bridge-building
-Builds vitality through residents, organizations, institutions, and governments co-creating conditions for thriving and resourceful places.

How we do it: Through a partnership between UMSL, MU Extension, and Missouri resident leaders, we offer opportunities across 3 key focus areas. 

1. RESEARCH
2. CIVIC DIALOGUE
3. LEADERSHIP + CAPACITY BUILDING

Students, faculty, and community stakeholders interested in getting involved are invited to explore opportunities for collaboration with CWC.

Description:

The organisation operates on three major themes of; 
1.  Sustainable Urban Planning. Orderly development of land uses through designing and implementing innovative urban development solutions.
2.  Community Engagement. Promoting participatory approaches, facilitating community led solutions and empowering local communities.

Name: Bothwell Community Hub
Description:
Bothwell Community Hub is a beautiful, listed building in the heart of Bothwell, South Lanarkshire. Recently re-opened to the public after extensive renovations, it has been completely transformed from an under-invested in local library into a bright and adaptable focal point for the village. It has retained the community’s valued local library service, whilst also offering a much needed social space in a multi-purpose facility hosting group activities and event space for pop-up-shops or exhibitions. 

This iconic building, built in 1910 from a bequest to the village in the estate of James Donald (1830/1905) had been home to Bothwell Library since 1966. After falling into a poor state of repair, the building was saved from closure in July 2022 by the successful completion of a Community Asset Transfer which brought the building full circle, back into the safe hands of the local community. This is one of many projects taken on to tackle the current and future needs of the village of Bothwell by local community trust, Bothwell Futures.

Propozycja działań obejmuje proces kreatywnej pracy nad potencjalnymi scenariuszami rozwoju w wybranych kluczowych obszarach, atrakcyjnych z punktu widzenia społeczności lokalnych, wzmocnienia grup eksperckich oraz uruchomienia lokalnych mediów będących naturalnym ekosystemem informacji ale także  monitoringu prowadzonych przedsięwzięć.

Planning Partnership guiding principles
*  Works on behalf of our partner organisations
*  Acknowledges the unique character of the communities and places across 
   Western Sydney
*  Drives the highest levels of collaboration amongst our partner organisations
*  Strives for best practice and knowledge sharing
*  Develops evidence-based and place-specific solutions
*  Develops solutions that can be applied at the regional scale
*  Leads project coordination across the region
*  Understands community needs as a potent tool for strategic planning and

Name: 2043 Corp
Description:
Our company is founded on the belief that structural change requires long-term, specific solutions, executed in sustained partnership.

Our founder reached an unwavering conclusion after spending the past 10 years working at the intersection of entrepreneurship, economic development, and innovation. Entrepreneurship is the best way to grow the American Economy while solving for some of our worst racial economic inequities. The only method to assure shared prosperity for all people groups is through comprehensive Private-Public-Philanthropic Partnerships focused on the unique needs of local and regional markets.

2043 was founded on March 11, 2020. Two days before, the state of Minnesota shut down due to the Covid-19 pandemic. In many ways, our company represents the dynamic, ever-changing landscape of the market environment. We can achieve recovery and exponential growth better than before when we: have access to relevant and accurate information (data), lean on relationship pathways that allow for quick go-to-market strategies (connectivity), and engage in mutually beneficial outcomes (trust). 

We often use the analogy of seed and soil when it comes to entrepreneurship. Rather than focus on the seed (the venture), we spend most of our efforts on the soil (the local economic environment). We believe by working with the institutions most equipped with resources, power, and policymaking ability; we can have a sustained impact on economic growth.

We are organized as a specific benefit corporation because we believe fiduciary responsibility to shareholders is not mutually exclusive to what is best for stakeholders and the wider public. In fact, we believe sustainable mission-driven organizations provide the best impact on the public good long term.

The sole purpose of our existence can be boiled to one sentence: Achieve the possibility of broad economic prosperity for all by the year 2043.

Name: UrbanTwin project
Description:
UrbanTwin aims to develop and validate an integrated tool to support decision-makers in achieving strategic climate and energy goals such as the Swiss Energy Strategy 2050 and the vision of climate-adaptive “sponge cities” in Switzerland.
This tool will consist of two elements:
(a) a detailed multi-scale model of critical urban infrastructure, such as energy, water, buildings, and mobility, as well as underlying socio-economic and environmental drivers and their inter-dependencies.
(b) a large-scale IT infrastructure, including:
• a multi-sectoral data acquisition platform
• an energy-efficient server to run the UrbanTwin model
The developed tool aims to provide various stakeholders with tools to rationally consider the trade-offs for each scenario in a user-friendly and accessible manner while it will be modular so that it can be adapted to the various users’ needs.
It will serve planners at the urban or district level and grid operators to undertake evidence-based planning and operation of their assets as well as authorities from the regional to national level to assess the expected output of a decision towards the achievement of a specific target.

Name: Sacramento County Planning and Environmental Review
Description:
Planning and Environmental Review is a division within the Community Development Department. We plan and sustain a built and natural environment that is reflective of our diverse population while protecting quality of life, the environment, and promoting equity.
Our Division is comprised of four sections:
•	Current Planning implements the General Plan, Zoning Ordinance and manages development in unincorporated Sacramento County.
•	Zoning Administration provides technical services and general planning to the public at the front counter, as well as review of building permits and business licenses.
•	Environmental Review prepares environmental documents pursuant to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) for privately and publicly initiated projects in Sacramento County.
•	Long Range Planning develops the vision, goals, and policies for urban growth and the protection and preservation of natural and agricultural resources.

Location: Edgbaston, North Edgabston, Bartley Green, Quinton and Harborne

Als onderdeel van het DMI-ecosysteem, gefaciliteerd door het Nationaal Groeifonds, brengen we diverse partners samen, waaronder gemeenten, technologiebedrijven en onderzoeksinstituten. Name: Westfair Fairgrounds and Amphitheater
Description:
Westfair has been a cornerstone of Southwest Iowa for more than 50 years—bringing people together to celebrate agriculture, community and tradition. 

Today, we’re building on that legacy with an ambitious capital campaign to modernize our 80-acre fairgrounds and create a year-round hub for economic development, education and connection.

Our vision includes a new state-of-the-art Education & Expo Center, upgraded infrastructure and revitalized entertainment spaces that will attract regional events, conferences, and tourism opportunities—all while continuing to serve the next generation of 4-H and FFA exhibitors.

With the support of families, businesses and community champions, Westfair is creating a future the past would be proud of.

Description:
Faith for Cities is an initiative committed to fostering sustainable urban development through multi-faith collaboration and innovation. Our team comprises diverse entities, each bringing unique strengths to our mission:

Templeton Religion Trust (TRT): A global charitable trust supporting projects and storytelling enriching the conversation about religion.
Thriving Cities Group (TCG): Utilizes a multi-disciplinary approach, combining research, training, and technology to enhance community organizing.
Synergine Group Limited: An ecosystem providing full-service, turn-key solutions aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
Relight: Serves as our communication and collaboration hub, transforming dialogues into actionable insights for urban development.
Our project is led by Dr. Chris Elisara, a renowned advocate for urban design and placemaking. Dr. Elisara's experience spans from revitalizing American cities to leading international initiatives in sustainable urban development, emphasizing the integration of faith in creating thriving, inclusive environments.

At Faith for Cities, we are dedicated to creating urban spaces that are not only structurally sound but also socially and spiritually vibrant. We believe in the power of collaboration, innovation, and faith-driven approaches to build cities that flourish for all.

Wij zijn een jonge zelfstandige ontwikkelaar die zich met name richt op de realisatie van middenklasse huurappartementen en betaalbare koopappartementen en -woningen. De twee segmenten die de afgelopen jaren (veel) te weinig zijn gebouwd en waar veel behoefte aan is.

Naast betaalbaar wonen is er veel behoefte aan levensloopbestendig wonen. We bouwen dan ook veelal met het oog op de toekomst, kan een koper of huurder over een aantal jaren nog steeds in deze woning wonen? Door levensloopbestendig te bouwen zijn woningen echter voor alle doelgroepen bereikbaar, voor zowel starters als senioren.

Met het oog op de toekomst bouwen betekent ook oog voor het milieu. Onze woningen zijn duurzaam in realisatie en gebruik door te werken met circulaire materialen zoals bijvoorbeeld hout. We leggen de lat hoog want duurzaamheid is meer dan energiebesparing.
