Description:
PIIVO est un projet de recherche financé par le Ministère de l'Économie, de l'Innovation et de l'Énergie du Québec qui a pour but d'améliorer la planification d'infrastructures vertes (IV) dans les villes québécoises. Nous mettons en avant la collaboration interuniversitaire et interdisciplinaire entre Polytechnique Montréal, l’Université de Montréal, l’INRS, l'Université d'Utrecht et l’Université du Québec en Outaouais, ainsi qu'avec plusieurs organisations du milieu utilisateur incluant Ouranos, le Ministère de l’Environnement et de la Lutte contre les changements climatiques (MELCC), Réseau Environnement, le Centre d'expertise et de recherche en infrastructures urbaines (CERIU), le Conseil régional de l'environnement de Montréal (CRE-Montréal), Habitat, l’Institut national de santé publique du Québec (INSPQ), la Ville de Montréal et la Ville de Trois-Rivières.

Le projet vise à développer un outil d’analyse spatiale multicritère de planification des IV, en partant du cadre d'analyse proposé dans l’outil Spatial Suitability ANalysis TOol (SSANTO, Kuller et al. 2019), initialement développé pour la ville de Melbourne (Australie). En parallèle, le projet s'intéresse aussi au contexte social et institutionnel de la planification des IV dans les villes du Québec et aux enjeux de gouvernance qui interviennent dans cette planification. Le projet PIIVO propose donc de soutenir une approche intersectorielle pour gérer plus efficacement les conflits et compromis lors de la planification des IV et ainsi conduire à des processus décisionnels plus équitables.

En collaboration avec le ministère de l'Économie, de l'Innovation et de l'Énergie du Québec:
https://www.linkedin.com/company/ministere-economie-et-innovation

Name: Take Root Bio
Description:
Our vision is a world free from hunger and pollution.

Our approach draws on our space biospheres and life support system theory. 

Take Root Bio is an urban regeneration organisation working with communities, local authorities, and partners to bring derelict and under-used buildings back to life as TRB Connected Farms.

We transform industrial heritage and neglected sites into productive, net-zero food ecosystems — growing food, jobs, skills, and community resilience where they are needed most.

Take Root Bio also acts as the group’s applied research and innovation organisation, working in the public interest to design, test, and validate regenerative food and environmental systems for the long-term benefit of communities and society. This includes piloting new approaches, generating real-world evidence, and de-risking innovation before it is scaled commercially.

Our Connected Farm systems are informed by closed-loop biosphere and life-support principles, originally developed for extreme and isolated environments. By adapting these space-inspired principles for Earth, we create resilient, efficient food ecosystems that perform reliably in dense cities, degraded buildings, and climate-stressed locations.

Our approach integrates food, energy, water, and data into a single AgriFood Ecosystem, using commercially viable technologies. By co-locating food production with digital infrastructure, we recover waste heat, reduce emissions, and enable year-round local food production.

We design place-based solutions aligned with 15-minute city principles, net-zero goals, and long-term community wellbeing, working across architecture, economics, finance, project delivery, and environmental technology.

Take Root Bio Technologies is our sister organisation and commercialisation partner, responsible for scaling and deploying the technologies proven through our regeneration and innovation programmes.

 We grow food, jobs, and communities in the industrial heritage of the past.

Name: SAFE AT HOME bomb shelter group
Description:
We are SAVE AT HOME Bomb Shelter Group — a multidisciplinary team from Ukraine, currently based in Switzerland, rethinking and designing architectural projects for modern multifunctional shelters for civilians.

Our project wasn’t born in an office. It began in the silence after the sirens.
We are people who couldn’t leave this issue behind.
Explosions, metro stations, overcrowded basements — we’ve seen them all.
And at some point, it became clear:
Shelters shouldn’t only be about survival.
They must become a part of peaceful life, integrated into civil urban infrastructure.

Our group began its work within the international educational program CAS Rebuild Ukraine 2024 (Bern University of Applied Sciences, BFH, CH) — as a team that combines technical expertise, critical thinking, and empathy.
We don’t work with abstract architecture — we respond to concrete human needs.

Our modular shelters serve a dual purpose:
Protection in times of crisis / Fully functional space in times of peace
A place to learn, to work, to meet — or simply to be with those you care about.
Not a concrete box, but a flexible environment designed for resilient urban life.

We operate at the intersection of architecture, engineering, urban planning, and social design.
Our team — architect, engineer, project manager, designer, and videographer — brings together expertise to create scalable solutions for different global and local contexts.

We are open to collaboration with government and municipal institutions, investors, donors, NGOs, and educational organizations to develop multifunctional CBRN shelters, support civil defense architecture, and consult teams working in the fields of emergency preparedness, community protection, and safe urban space development.

Safety is not a luxury — it’s a basic human right.
We know how to make it real, adaptable, and accessible.
We envision safe public spaces that integrate seamlessly into everyday life.

Sincerely,
SAVE AT HOME Bomb Shelter Group

Acorn is a proprietary large language model driven by AI technology to crawl regional economic development data sets, along with comparable communities’ programs and outcomes, to produce Comprehensive Economic Development Strategy (CEDS) planning supplemented with current demographic and labor data.  Our cutting-edge economic development strategic planning, crafted by seasoned economic developers and enhanced with the precision of artificial intelligence, represents a groundbreaking approach in the field.

Utilizing comprehensive data sourced from public records, our strategic plans offer an unparalleled analysis of the economic landscape. We highlight key trends, potential growth areas, and sectors ripe for investment, backed by AI-driven insights that ensure accuracy and relevance. Our plans serve as an essential tool for policymakers, investors, and business leaders, providing them with a detailed overview of the current economic status and future opportunities. Our innovative use of AI in processing and interpreting vast datasets from public records not only enhances the reliability of the findings but also offers predictive insights, setting a new standard in economic analysis and strategic planning. Our plans are an invaluable resource for anyone looking to understand, invest in, or shape the future of the local economy.

Name: Cabane Partner Urbane Strategien und Entwicklung
Description:
Das Büro zeichnet sich durch langjährige und vielfältige Erfahrung im Bereich Nutzungsentwicklung, Stadtentwicklung/Stadtsoziologie, Stadtstruktur/Städtebau, Prozessmanagement/Projektsteuerung und Kommunikation aus.

Cabane Partner unterstützt Grundeigentümer, Städte und Gemeinden bei der Konzeptualisierung, Entwicklung und Begleitung von städtebaulichen Prozessen, der Umnutzung historischer Bauten, der Aktivierung von Nutzungen in öffentlichen und privaten Räumen sowie bei der Konzeption von Immobilien- und Umsetzungsstrategien. Der methodische Schwerpunkt basiert auf dem Grundsatz, dass eine zukunftsfähige Entwicklung – neben der baulichen Struktur und Form – wesentlich auf dem Engagement der dort aktiven Investoren, Immobilienentwickler und Nutzer beruht. Die Disposition und Zuordnung von Flächen zu Akteuren, sorgfältig konzipierte Evaluationsverfahren sowie kooperative Formate sind dabei entscheidende Erfolgsfaktoren.

Das von Philippe Cabane 2003 gegründete Büro Cabane Urbane Strategien & Entwicklung hat sich national wie auch international als Spezialist für Nutzungsplanung und Entwicklung etabliert. Nach einer vierjährigen Zusammenarbeit mit Philippe Cabane ist Sarah Zussy, als Gesellschafterin in das Unternehmen eingetreten. Die Firma läuft seither unter dem Namen Cabane Partner – Urbane Strategien und Entwicklung GmbH, deren Inhaber Philippe Cabane und Sarah Zussy sind.

Name: Downtown Brattleboro Alliance
Description:
Founded in 1999 as Building a “Better Brattleboro” and then renamed in 2014 to Downtown Brattleboro Alliance (DBA), we are the official non-profit Designated Downtown Organization for the Town of Brattleboro. We are one of 23 downtown organizations in the state of Vermont. This designation provides access to grants, technical assistance, and networking opportunities. Our programming is funded by grants, donations, and a special assessment tax on downtown properties in the Downtown Improvement District.

The DBA works with members, including the downtown property owners, to develop a work plan and budget. Before submitting these to the town, DBA members vote and approve the work plan and budget.

The DBA represents more than 90 businesses and organizations and is led by a volunteer Board of Directors which includes local business owners, property owners, educators, and town representatives.  The DBA has a staff of 4 and many incredible volunteers.

Description:
Sun Valley Economic Development (SVED), originally formed in 2010 as SustainBlaine, is a 501(c)(6) not-for-profit public-private partnership. Sun Valley Economic Development is the only organization focused on county-wide issues and works directly with the Idaho Department of Commerce and other Idaho state agencies.

Sun Valley Economic Development operates under the guidance of a full-time executive director and volunteer Board of Directors. SVED’s Board is a diverse and experienced set of leaders, with representatives from our region’s largest employers, public officials, and other influential community organizations.

Sun Valley Economic Development is funded on an annual basis by a mix of public and private sector monies. Anchored by a multi-year, renewable Idaho Department of Commerce grant, additional funds are contributed by Blaine County, its municipalities, and by more than 150 of the leading businesses in the private sector. These funds enable SVED to deliver relevant projects and services that provide concrete economic benefits on a cost-efficient basis. SVED continues to steadily reduce reliance on public sector funds and seeks longer-term economic sustainability through increased private sector funding and innovative fee-based mechanisms

Jest to pierwszy w Polsce tego rodzaju kierunek, stworzony z myślą o kształceniu przyszłych specjalistów w zakresie zintegrowanego podejścia do planowania i zarządzania rozwojem.

Koło skupia ambitnych i zaangażowanych studentów, którzy pragną rozwijać zarówno kompetencje twarde, takie jak analiza danych czy projektowanie strategii, jak i umiejętności miękkie, m.in. komunikację, pracę w zespole czy negocjacje. Realizujemy to poprzez aktywny udział w różnorodnych inicjatywach – konferencjach, projektach naukowych, spotkaniach z ekspertami z różnych dziedzin. 

Ponadto nawiązujemy współpracę z jednostkami publicznymi oraz przedstawicielami świata biznesu, co umożliwia nam zdobywanie praktycznych umiejętności, kluczowych w naszej przyszłej pracy zawodowej.

Name: O'Forme
Description: Le centre OForme, situe au 1194 rue des Rossignols a Boucherville propose des solutions de remise en forme physique et esthetique basees sur 2 nouvelles technologies peu presente au Quebec : Icoone et Power Plate. LIcoone est une machine de rouler-palper. Elle permet de faire des massages localises afin de : - diminuer la cellulite et leffet capiton - reduire les tissus adipeux, notamment chez les hommes - retendre les tissus de tout le corps et du visage. - desengorger les liquides corporels (circulation sanguine, lymphatique...) - assurer un bien-etre qui se ressent rapidement et pour longtemps. - aider le corps a se remettre suite a un accouchement ou une blessure... La Power Plate est une plate-forme emettant des vibrations en trois dimensions. Cette stimulation permet de solliciter les muscles (abdominaux, triceps, fessiers,...) a 97% de leur capacite contre seulement 30% lors dun entrainement traditionnel. Lobjectif de cette machine est donc de renforcer les muscles, meme les plus profonds. Une a deux seances hebdomadaires de 30 minutes, equivalant a 1h30 de pratique dans un centre de remise en forme traditionnel, suffisent pour reconstruire la masse musculaire efficacement.

Name: Associação Ovua
Description:
A OVUA – Associação Juvenil para Saúde e Sustentabilidade Ambiental nasceu em Pemba, Cabo Delgado, como uma resposta ao desejo de jovens engajados em transformar suas comunidades através da educação, da proteção ambiental e da promoção da saúde. Com foco especial na saúde sexual e reprodutiva, a OVUA procura empoderar adolescentes e jovens para que façam escolhas conscientes e seguras, reduzindo casos de gravidezes precoces e doenças sexualmente transmissíveis.

Paralelamente, a associação atua fortemente na defesa do meio ambiente, por meio de campanhas de limpeza, ações de reflorestamento e projetos de gestão comunitária de resíduos, sempre incentivando práticas de sustentabilidade que respeitem os recursos naturais e melhorem a qualidade de vida local.

A OVUA acredita que a mudança começa pelos jovens e aposta na formação de líderes comunitários, na capacitação em empreendedorismo verde e na criação de espaços de diálogo sobre direitos humanos, equidade de gênero e justiça ambiental. Com valores como solidariedade, participação comunitária e inovação, a associação visa construir uma sociedade mais informada, responsável e resiliente.

Sonhamos com uma juventude protagonista e uma Pemba mais limpa, saudável e sustentável, onde cada jovem seja agente de mudança e esperança para o futuro.

Name: Ochakai
Description:
Have you ever wondered why it can feel so difficult to connect with your neighbours?

In cities, it is easy to feel anonymous or alone. Ochakai creates spaces where neighbours can pause, meet, and share a sense of presence in their everyday surroundings. These small encounters help build the trust and everyday connections that make life feel alive.

We gather in apartment buildings, local parks, and community spaces, using the act of sharing tea to bring people closer. 

Ochakai is a practice of connection, making where you live feel more like home. 


What we offer
Ochakai organises and facilitates small-scale tea gatherings that support social connection and wellbeing in urban settings.
- Community tea events for residential buildings, neighbourhoods, and public spaces
- Workshops and co-creation sessions around belonging and placemaking
- Customised formats for housing providers, municipalities, and community groups interested in strengthening local ties

Keywords:
urban belonging, community connection, neighbourhood life, social wellbeing, loneliness in cities, participatory design, placemaking, social sustainability, social impact, community-led projects, urban inclusion, community-led innovation, active neighbourhoods

Name: Endemic
Description:
Endemic is a community R&D ecosystem developing pathways to rejuvenate society. We train communities in crucial skills to unlock and accelerate richer, more vibrant, and more alive worlds and support the healthy coordination of movements to achieve those futures.

We relate to this work in several dimensions of activity;

1. A public Virtual Community Structured for Large Scale Generative Conversation, Education, and Guidance

2. Coalitions Developing and Deploying Interventions in the World

3. New Education Institutions, Training in Critical Skills, Innovating Towards the Futures We Most Want

4. Transformative Village Rejuvenation or Creation

Our basic principle is that the most significant value and power we can cultivate is well-coordinated and self-motivated collective talent. The skills needed for our desired futures—those that underpin our innovation and cultures, from basic vocations to advanced sciences—still require dedicated training. Beyond providing this training, Endemic is developing various matchmaking processes to connect individuals to opportunities: local positions, leadership roles in organizations, interventions to contribute to, apprenticeships, and more. Members can gradually opt into these activities as they become available. 

You can join our community to learn much more at endemic.org/originals

Name: MLK Corridor Improvement Authority
Description:
MLK Drive is the first Jackson thoroughfare named for an African American person, and its renaming symbolizes how much our Southside is rooted in African American culture and history. Over generations, the Southside saw freed slaves envisioning new lives, activists and allies fighting for civil rights, entrepreneurs building thriving businesses, and African American-run newspapers sharing the successes and achievements within our neighborhood. It was vibrant, it was prosperous, and most importantly, it was home. 

In the years since, disinvestment has stripped the Southside of its landmarks and stories. MLK CIA is working with residents and business owners to restore our legacy and honor our history. Together, we can fill these streets again with the welcoming homes and bustling local businesses our residents desire. Together, we can remember our past, reinvent our future, and Revive the Southside.

The Meaning of 'Anchorpoint' in Planning

The name 'Anchorpoint' reflects the fundamental role that local government planning plays in providing stability, direction, and a strong foundation for communities. Name: Southern Puerto Rico Economic Development District
Description: Southern Puerto Rico Economic Development District (SPREDD) is the first economic development district designated by the United States Department of Commerce Economic Development Administration in Puerto Rico. SPREDD is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization working towards the strengthening of Southern Puerto Rico's regional economy with a focus on six municipalities: Guánica, Guayanilla, Juana Díaz, Peñuelas, Ponce, Yauco.  SPREDD amplifies the capacity of key stakeholders by (1) aligning and connecting economic development initiatives to ensure maximum efficiency; (2) ensuring coherent and in-depth, research and data-driven, planning process; (3) providing capacity building to municipalities, private-sector partners, and nonprofit sector organizations; and (4) facilitating access to public and private capital through intentional connections and technical assistance.

Jaax Street Partners is a community development and planning practice dedicated to helping public schools, their students and communities thrive. Our founder, Shannon Jaax, led the nationally recognized Repurposing Initiative at Kansas City Public Schools (KCPS) for over a decade, and most recently served as the project manager for KCPS' historic $474M General Obligation (GO) Bond Plan, the district's first successful bond election in almost 60 years. 

Jaax Street's services include:
●     Advisory services for surplus school/real estate repurposing and disposition
●     Project management & implementation
●     Capacity building & community engagement
●     Strategic planning
●     Policy research & analysis
●     Process improvement
●     Thought partnership

Here, we offer you the opportunity to understand before you buy. In less than 48 hours, you will receive a comprehensive score report on the building, the location, and the neighborhood.

Name: Sud Digitalǝ
Description:
Sud Digitale è un porto per chi resta e per chi torna, al Sud. Una comunità di più di 120 persone che, pur incontrandosi fisicamente ogni terzo mercoledì del mese, navigano nell’era del digitale. 
Abbiamo creato un format di meetup informali che nascono per favorire la connessione tra persone che condividono valori personali e interessi professionali. La nostra missione è fare rete e creare relazioni di valore partendo dalla Puglia e in generale dal Sud.
Dal 2023 ci riuniamo ogni terzo mercoledì del mese facendo base a Bari ma abbiamo portato Sud Digitale anche a Barletta, Mattinata, Rutigliano, Foggia, in collaborazione con altre associazioni territoriali.
Vogliamo guidare con l’esempio per creare una contro-narrazione del meridione come il luogo virtuoso per eccellenza, dove ragazze e ragazzi sono in grado di generare impatto sociale, economico e ambientale, creare nuove imprese, supportarsi vicendevolmente grazie a uno spazio sicuro di confronto, dove ciò che sei conta più di ciò che fai.

Name: Ripon Main Street Inc
Description:
Ripon Main Street Inc. is a nonprofit economic development and marketing organization dedicated to revitalizing Ripon's Central Business District. Main Street supports the interests of Ripon downtown property and business owners. The organization guides a business improvement district (BID) that oversees the revitalization of Ripon's central business district – a historic downtown comprised of more than 75 property owners and 125 businesses.

Few cities of Ripon’s size offer such an eclectic mix of architecture, shops and dining in one convenient place. Budget Travel magazine named Ripon one of the “Coolest Small Towns in America,” but don’t take their word for it. Visit Ripon and experience the excitement first-hand.

The organization envisions a Sto-Rox in which places serve people and encourage community growth, and structural oppression is replaced with empowerment.

Our mission is to unlock the full potential of an undeveloped property or existing business and enhance the economic and social fabric of the community. We are committed to excellence and integrity, working hard to improve the quality of life for our communities and create lasting value for investors.

Description: An economic development firm that supports economic growth, private sector development, competitiveness, and job creation in South Africa. It strengthens the promotion of investment and trade, catalyses productive infrastructure; develops the workforce, and improves the business enabling environment. UIS seeks to drive natural investment and growth platforms, cultivating partnerships between stakeholders in the public, private and civil society sectors, as well as emphasising an ecosystem conducive to innovation and entrepreneurship. UIS specialises in “Smart-City” developments and undertakes a central developmental role as an Implementing Agent for South African government deaprtments and municipalities.

BLK South is a place-based community development organization rooted in the historic Black South. We work alongside neighbors, churches, and elders to strengthen community life through long-term planning, cultural preservation, leadership development, and neighborhood-centered investment.

Our work centers historically Black communities as sources of wisdom, resilience, and shared responsibility—building pathways toward collective well-being, economic stability, and belonging across generations.

 Based in Durham, NC | Working across the South

Name: KHAMELA PROPERTY INVESTMENT
Description:
Khamela Property Investment (Pty) Ltd is a South African-based company specializing in town planning consultancy and supply & delivery solutions. Established to support sustainable development and efficient service delivery, Khamela works with both public and private sector clients to help shape well-planned communities and provide essential goods and materials.

Our town planning services include rezoning, township establishment, subdivision and consolidation, consent use applications, and the removal of restrictive conditions. We assist clients in navigating complex regulatory frameworks to unlock land development potential.

In addition to planning expertise, Khamela offers procurement and delivery services, supplying a range of branded and office products such as calendars, pens, diaries, and more. Our goal is to deliver value, efficiency, and quality in every engagement.

We are committed to excellence, compliance, and community-focused solutions, playing an active role in the transformation and growth of South African urban spaces.

Name: Sumner Main Street Association
Description: The Sumner Main Street Association (SMSA) is a non-profit organization with the mission of revitalizing Sumner’s historic downtown district. SMSA is a Washington Main Street Affiliate Member and uses the National Main Street Program’s Four-Point Approach to guide its historic downtown revitalization. The Main Street Four-Point Approach is a comprehensive community-based approach to community revitalization using each of the following four points: Design, Economic Vitality, Promotion, and Outreach. These four points guide our organizational structure and annual work plans.

Name: Threads of Hope
Description:
প্রজেক্ট - Threads of Hope 

তাঁত শিল্প বাংলাদেশের একটি ঐতিহ্যবাহী দেশীয় কারুশিল্প। বাংলাদেশের অন্যান্য জেলার মতো চট্টগ্রামেও স্বাধীনতার আগে-পরে রমরমা অবস্থান ছিল তাঁত শিল্পের। নানাবিধ কারণে এ ঐতিহ্য আর দেশীয় শিল্প তাঁতকল গুলো একে একে বন্ধ হয়ে যায়। শতক থেকে এখন ধুকতে ধুকতে চট্টগ্রাম শহরে টিকে আছে একটি মাত্র হস্তচালিত তাঁতকল। 

আর চট্টগ্রাম শহরে টিকে থাকা একমাত্র ও সর্বশেষ হস্তচালিত তাঁতকলটির টিকে থাকার লড়াইয়ে পাশে দাঁড়িয়েছে একদল স্বপ্নবাজ তরুণ। তাদের এ উদ্যোগের নাম 'Threads of Hope' 

হস্তচালিত তাঁতে তৈরী পণ্যকে নতুনভাবে তুলে ধরার মাধ্যমে এই শিল্পটিকে বাঁচিয়ে রাখতে কাজ করছি আমরা। তাঁত শিল্পীরা আমাদের তৈরী ডিজাইনে নতুন কাঙ্খিত পণ্য গুলো মনের মাধুরি মিশিয়ে পরম যত্নে বুনেছেন। সেই পণ্য গুলো আমরা আপনাদের সামনে তুলে ধরতে চলেছি।

অপরদিকে, চট্টগ্রাম নগরীর দেওয়ানহাট, রেলস্টেশন বস্তি এলাকা থেকে পিছিয়ে পড়া জনগোষ্ঠীর নারী ও  ট্রান্সজেন্ডার সম্প্রদায়ের কিছু সংখ্যক মানুষকে হস্তশিল্প ও বুননের কাজ বিষয় প্রশিক্ষণ দিয়ে স্বাবলম্বী করার কাজ চলছে। তাদেরকে প্রশিক্ষিত করে তাদেরই তৈরী পণ্য সামগ্রী তুলে ধরবো আপনাদের সামনে। একদিকে নতুন একটি দক্ষতা যেমন তাদের শিল্পীমনন গড়ে তুলবে, অপরদিকে এ দক্ষতা কাজে লাগিয়ে তারা নিজেদের স্বাবলম্বী করে তোলার পথে আরো একধাপ এগিয়ে যাবেন। 

প্রজেক্ট " Threads of Hope" দুইটি সম্প্রদায়ের মধ্যে সংযোগ তৈরী করে একটির জনগোষ্ঠীর জীবনমানের পরিবর্তন সাধন ও তাদের বহুবছরের ঐতিহ্যকে বাঁচিয়ে রাখার স্বপ্ন দেখছে, অপরদিকে অন্য একটি সম্প্রদায়ের নারীদের দক্ষতা বৃদ্ধির মাধ্যমে তাদের স্বাবলম্বী করে তোলার চেষ্টা করছে। 

তাদের স্বপ্নকে ডানা মেলে উড়তে সাহায্য করার ছোট্ট প্রয়াস প্রজেক্ট "Threads of Hope". আমাদের এই পথচলায় আপনাদের ভালোবাসা ও অনুপ্রেরণাই পারে আমাদের কাজে গতি সঞ্চার করে তাদের স্বপ্নপূরণ করতে

Name: Nimble Consulting, LLC
Description: Nimble Consulting, LLC, is a boutique consulting firm providing services for land use entitlement and development permitting processes. At Nimble, we specialize in identifying the most direct path to entitlement and permitting to effectively meet the client’s needs. Offering a comprehensive approach to due diligence research,  we analyze each site through direct communication and in-person research at each jurisdiction to provide clients with a complete picture of the zoning history, land use entitlements and path to permitting. We effectively prepare and manage land use entitlement strategies to obtain approval of zoning and development applications, in conjunction with consultants and attorneys.  Our approach is simple, cost effective and thorough. By closely managing timelines, consultant’s tasks and tracking jurisdictional review processes, our client’s projects stay on track. Our firm’s size, combined with our approach provides each of our clients with an advantage, where projects are completed timely by identifying and implementing the most effective path to obtain the required entitlements and permitting.

Belazaima do Chão, Águeda, in the heart of Nature in harmony with Mother Earth. Feridouro is a place at the heart of an incredible reforestation project called project Cabeço Santo. Around this project, we decided to try to live together and co-create a place of transmission and holistic regeneration of the living being (nature, human beings, animals and more). In this way, we converge together on common values such as
respect for living beings and the environment, more conscious eating, resilience, valuing diversity and the potential of each one, the joy of living, communication from the heart, inner ecology, spirituality and the creation of connections and mutual help.

Name: Turquesa Los Cabos
Description:
Turquesa Los Cabos irrumpe en El Tezal, una de las zonas más prestigiosas y codiciadas de Los Cabos, transformando este enclave paradisíaco en un espacio accesible y vibrante para todos. 

Turquesa Los Cabos no es solo un conjunto habitacional, sino la posibilidad de una vida mejor en un destino favorecido por la belleza y la abundancia. Aquí, el pasado, el presente y el futuro se encuentran para celebrar el renacer de una comunidad que ha abierto las puertas a un mundo de oportunidades. Con cada amanecer, sus residentes se despiertan con la sensación de haber encontrado un hogar donde pueden crecer, soñar y vivir plenamente, siendo parte de esta nueva comunidad en Los Cabos.

Al abrir sus puertas a los residentes locales que han soñado con establecer su hogar en este rincón privilegiado, Turquesa Los Cabos devuelve a la zona su auténtico espíritu y crea una comunidad diversa en la que cada uno de sus miembros tiene la oportunidad de proyectar su vida hacia un futuro próspero.

Description:
Whether you’re a lifelong resident or a new visitor, you can look forward to something new whenever you’re in Squirrel Hill.

A one-of-kind gift from one of our unique shops, a quiet corner and local art in a neighborhood cafe, or delicious meals at  brand new-to-you restaurants.

This is a neighborhood for exploring, wandering, and enjoying the adventure with family and friends. Come and Uncover Squirrel Hill.

Name: Remerge Institute
Description:
Earn college and graduate credits or professional development certification through experiential learning that practically equips you to creatively engage and transform your community. 

Our Unique Learning Experiences:
• Undergraduate & Graduate Courses - For-credit in person & virtual learning opportunities where students will learn community engagement and transformation through real-life projects.
• Professional Development Courses & Certificates - Non-degree courses and workshops that will help nonprofit & faith leaders make greater positive impact in your community for the long haul.
• Remerge Fellows - Students completing the Institute curriculum have the opportunity to receive mentorship and resources to further develop their skills and implement their solutions.
• Service Learning - Students travel to Atlanta to learn about and participate in Remerge’s work toward building communities of peace.
• The Arena - A talk-back series exploring a wide range of topics effecting the building of flourishing communities conducted by experts actively working on solutions for the topics discussed.

The Remerge Institute is housed within the community-building nonprofit, Remerge. Remerge is a team of values-driven reconcilers and thought leaders in the world of community-building. Along with our strategically integrated programs, we accelerate new solutions and strategies that address the ever-evolving causes of poverty and social-exclusion. Remerge programs and initiatives help serve over 10,000 people each year.

Name: FOODGaP
Description: Recent key conferences, including “Food Policies in Mediterranean Cities” in Naples, the “Second United Nations Summit on Sustainable Food Systems” in Rome, and “Food Security and Sustainability: Cooperating to Grow” in Rimini (all in 2023), have emphasized critical action points: Developing the technical and political capacity of staff in small and medium-sized cities to create and implement effective local food policies; integrating policies to bridge urban-rural gaps and ensure cohesive governance; engaging local communities in the decision-making process, facilitating the implementation of local food policies through cooperation among Mediterranean cities. The project will address these points by piloting four participatory governance models in Tyre (Lebanon), Mersin (Türkiye), Rome (Italy), and Houmt Souk (Tunisia). This will involve at least 50 local stakeholders, the co-design and testing of four local food policies, and the implementation of one key action, to demonstrate the effectiveness of the participatory governance models piloted. Co-funded by the European Union under the Interreg NEXT MED programme.

Description:
Entwicklungskonzepte für zukunftsfähige Regionen, Städte und Gemeinden - Moderation und Steuerung von Beteiligungsverfahren - Forschung und Beratung

Unser Name ist Programm: Kommunikation, fachübergreifender Austausch und das gemeinsame Handeln aller Beteiligten ist für uns die Basis einer zukunftsfähigen Stadt- und Regionalentwicklung. Hier setzt unsere Arbeit an – wir beziehen die Akteure vor Ort aus Bürgerschaft, Verwaltung, Politik, Wirtschaft, Fachinstitutionen, Vereinen und Verbänden eng ein und holen verschiedene Fachdisziplinen an einen Tisch. Unser Ziel ist es stets, individuelle Lösungen gemeinsam mit den Beteiligten zu entwickeln.

Wir verbinden unsere fachliche Expertise mit Methodenkompetenz für die kommunikative Planung. Unser Leistungsspektrum reicht von Fachplanungen und Analysen über Entwicklungskonzepte bis zu Forschungsprojekten und Qualifizierung zu Fachthemen und Methoden. Moderation und Management von Beteiligungsprozessen auf kommunaler und regionaler Ebene ist seit gut 30 Jahren der rote Faden unserer Arbeit.

Die Kompetenzen von KoRiS decken ein breites Spektrum der Stadt- und Regionalentwicklung ab. Im Sinne der integrierten Entwicklung verbindet unser Büro die verschiedenen Arbeitsfelder der räumlichen Entwicklung, um Querbezüge und Schnittstellen in den Blick zu nehmen. Genauso gehören Fachkonzepte für einzelne Bereiche zu unserem Leistungsspektrum, um gezielte Entwicklungen anzustoßen.

Wir engagieren uns in mehreren Fachverbänden, um uns an aktuellen Diskussionen zur Stadt- und Regionalentwicklung zu beteiligen und unsere Wissensbasis auf dem aktuellen Stand zu halten.

Wir verfügen über ein breites Netzwerk an Partnern, die wir bei Bedarf in unsere Arbeit einbinden. Für spezielle technische Analysen, im Bereich Marketing oder für sozialwissenschaftliche Analysen können wir auf langjährige Kooperationspartner zurückgreifen, die das KoRiS-Team mit ihren Kompetenzen ergänzen.

Our Vision
We envision a future where historically disinvested neighborhoods become healthy and vibrant places to live, work, play, and grow food. Name: POETS
Description:
We are a think tank of local professionals and academics with long experience in planning, environment and transport.

We believe the following principles should apply to planning Oxfordshire's future:

Give priority to the climate emergency - all policies, actions and investment should be directed towards mitigating and adapting to climate change.

Provide safe, secure, decent and genuinely affordable housing for all.

Support an economy that prospers to the benefit of all.

Create an environment that promotes public health (e.g. by providing for safe and convenient walking and cycling).

Promote a more equitable society.

Protect the best agricultural land from development to support food security.

Protect and enhance biodiversity and ecosystem functions.

Increase electricity from renewable sources, and provide energy-efficient buildings.

Improve water efficiency and reduce consumption, and provide excellent sewerage capacity.

Reduce travel need and demand and give priority to sustainable transport, such as walking, cycling and public transport. Abandon proposals for increased road capacity.

Ensure new development is based on the use of public transport, walking and cycling, and incorporates high quality design.

Improve democracy - decisions on development and change are taken by democratically elected and clearly accountable representatives, with genuine citizen involvement. Properly fund local planning authorities.

Name: Bay City Community Development Corporation
Description:
About Bay City CDC

Bay City CDC is a locally-focused economic and community development corporation in Bay City, Texas. We leverage sales-tax revenues and public-private partnerships to drive job creation, infrastructure improvements and downtown revitalization across Matagorda County. 

What we do

Foster business investment and growth by supporting local companies expanding or relocating to Bay City, working through tax-abatement, infrastructure financing and incentive programs. 

Maintain and enhance public infrastructure—such as water, wastewater, streets and housing—so that neighborhoods and businesses thrive. 

Spearhead community beautification and placemaking efforts to uplift the overall quality of life and encourage civic engagement. 

Administer targeted grant programs (for example, supporting small business e-commerce websites) that expand access, growth and entrepreneurship in our region. 

Our mission

To enhance Bay City’s economy by retaining and creating jobs, expanding the tax base and improving quality of life across our community. 

Why it matters

By strategically aligning infrastructure investment, business incentives and community revitalization initiatives, Bay City CDC helps transform local opportunity into sustainable growth — enabling both residents and enterprises to flourish.

To achieve this, we must understand the community’s perspective—and that’s not always easy.
Let’s Chat was founded on over 20 years of experience engaging with communities on large-scale, city-shaping infrastructure projects. We understand the challenges of effective engagement: reaching diverse audiences, gathering meaningful feedback, and ensuring that insights inform project decisions.
Our goal is simple: to make conversations with communities and stakeholders lead to positive outcomes and enduring success for every project.
We do this by providing:
•	Community and stakeholder engagement
•	Strategic engagement planning
•	Facilitation and workshops
•	Social outcomes measures
•	Social infrastructure assessment
•	Social and recreation impact assessment.
Let’s start the conversation.  Contact us to learn how we can help you.

Name: Little Tokyo Community Impact Fund
Description:
Los Angeles’ Little Tokyo is under severe gentrification and displacement pressures, the latest threat in its 140-year history, which includes seizure of Japanese American property during WWII and previous waves of redevelopment. 

The Little Tokyo Community Impact Fund (LTCIF) was created to stem the displacement of legacy small businesses, a key element to cultural and community viability. The fund’s assets come from community investors who intentionally accept well-below-market returns, which enables LTCIF to acquire commercial real estate and lease spaces at stable, below-market rents to at-risk, community-serving legacy small businesses. The investment model – rather than a donations model – encourages a sense of community ownership in Little Tokyo.

ITA

L’associazione vuole coinvolgere e stimolare il territorio, i suoi abitanti ed i visitatori con progetti, eventi ed iniziative artisticoculturali che, passo dopo passo, andranno a costruire la “Via del Colore”: allo stesso tempo un percorso verso la bellezza e un manifesto per la rigenerazione e la promozione turistica di un territorio tanto prezioso quanto strategico per la grande Milano. 

La missione di lungo termine è la creazione del brand “Magenta Città del Colore” anche attraverso l’istituzione di un Museo Internazionale dei Colori.

ENG
Fucsina, an association founded on 31 December 2020, takes the history of places and the theory of colours as its starting point to rediscover the strength of a collective identity linked to the colour - famous throughout the world – to which the city of Magenta gives its name.

The association wants to involve and stimulate the territory, its inhabitants and visitors with projects, events and artistic-cultural initiatives that, step by step, will build the "Via del Colore": at the same time a path towards beauty and a manifesto for the regeneration and tourist promotion of an area as precious as it is strategic for the great Milan.

The long-term mission is the creation of the 'Magenta City of Colour' brand also through the establishment of an International Museum of Colours.

Name: Neoscapes Lab
Description:
Neoscapes Lab is a future-facing regenerative design venture co-founded by Sana Jazeer, a material futures architect, and Mizba Pathan, an innovation strategist. We are currently at the proof-of-concept stage, developing our first innovation, UrbanBloom—a fully biodegradable modular pavement block system for temporary architecture and outdoor events. Built for events but designed for the planet, UrbanBloom reimagines how cities and communities coexist with nature by transforming temporary infrastructure into regenerative architecture.
UrbanBloom protects and nourishes soil while enabling adaptable, low-impact event design, helping the industry move beyond circularity toward true regeneration. Made from organic, biodegradable materials, each block naturally decomposes, restoring soil health and reducing landfill waste.

The concept emerged from festival sites such as Glastonbury, where recurring soil damage from footfall and structures leads to costly fallow years and long-term ecological harm. UrbanBloom offers a scalable, soil-positive alternative for festivals, pop-ups, and public installations in the UK and beyond—particularly in landscapes strained by recurring mass events.

Neoscapes Lab recently participated in the Founders Forward Perfect Pitch programme by JPMorgan Chase, where we refined our go-to-market strategy and built early-stage traction. Through Unearthodox, we aim to accelerate R&D, pilot testing, and partnerships that bring regenerative design to life at scale.

Name: City Science Lab San Francisco
Description:
City Science Lab San Francisco harnesses artificial intelligence and systems modeling to help residents, policymakers, and advocates understand complex urban challenges — and explore solutions together. The Lab brings civic innovation into the age of simulation.  Like SimCity — but for real.

Cities are dynamic systems of systems — housing, transportation, zoning, energy, and public space all interacting in real time. By making these systems visible, interactive, and data-driven, the Lab helps accelerate decision-making, reduce polarization, and foster shared understanding. Our models and visual tools give everyone — from city planners to neighborhood residents — the ability to see how policy choices ripple across the urban fabric.

As a public good organization, City Science Lab SF serves as civic infrastructure for collective intelligence. Operating as an independent, non-profit initiative fiscally sponsored by SPUR, the Lab works at the intersection of government, academia, and industry to make urban governance more transparent, participatory, and adaptive.

We believe democracy works best when everyone can see how the city works. Through rigorous, fact-based modeling and emerging technologies, the Lab helps communities explore what’s possible — from equitable housing growth to vibrant downtowns — building a shared foundation for a more affordable, resilient, and responsive San Francisco

Name: Narrative Designs
Description:
Where Place and Story Meet

Welcome to Narrative Designs, where urban planning and design collide with creativity, storytelling, and a little bit of fun. We’re all about amplifying the unique identity of neighborhoods and communities through thoughtful design and meaningful connections.

What We Do:
Think of us as part urban planner, part graphic designer, part storyteller. We bring neighborhoods to life by:
1. Providing planning and design expertise that uncovers, develops, and amplifies a sense of place.
2. Crafting hyper-local goods like custom flags and artistic maps that celebrate community pride.

Whether it’s helping your community craft its story through placemaking or creating a flag for your neighborhood we bridge the gap between vision and execution.

Why Narrative Designs?
We’re not your average consultancy. With deep expertise in planning processes and design, we bring a fresh perspective to every project—pulling inspiration from all corners of the creative world. We take our work seriously (ourselves, not so much) and believe the best ideas come when curiosity, creativity, and joy collide.

Founder:
Narrative Designs was founded by Steve Chester, a seasoned urban planner and designer with over 15 years of experience. Steve’s approach blends a love for data and details with an eye for visual storytelling and a passion for making connections—both between people and the places they call home.

Featured Services:

Visual Storytelling
Place-Based Planning & Design
Demonstration Projects

Featured Goods:
Community Cartography (artistic maps)
Neighborhood Flags
Hyper-Local Designs

Our Motto:
Every place has a story. We help tell it.

Name: S.I.D. Consulting
Description:
S.I.D. Consulting provide Strategic Advice and Technical Support in the wide fields of Strategic Spatial Planning and Urban Policies:

- Sustainable Urban Planning, also at metropolitan, regional and national level
- Urban Regeneration and Social Housing
- Sustainable Urban Mobility
- City Socioeconomic strategies for Sustainable Development and Well-being
- Urban Innovation and Competitiveness
- Climate Change & Resilience related Issues.


In mentioned areas we supply specific assistance in:

- Attracting private investments as well as securing funds from IFI, UN Agencies, International Cooperation Institutions, and other Funding institutions.
- Strategic integrated planning and policymaking to public administrators and politicians
- Supervising planning officers and experts (local and international)
- Staff Capacity Building
- Proposal, Report and Presentations drafting
- Monitoring and Evaluation of projects.

Our approach focusses on four key areas:
- Inclusive and safe cities (“People”) through the promotion of social dimension of urban development (“Peace”), for improving the quality of living and urban wellness in general.

- Horizontal and vertical urban governance (“Partnerships”) at local, regional, national levels, and between different actors such as elected officials, civil servants, citizens' groups, private companies, investors, thus ensuring inclusive participation in integrated planning.

- Green and resilient cities (“Planet”) through the sustainable use of natural resources and enhancement of natural capital (energy efficiency and biodiversity in cities), including the transition towards a low-carbon, climate resilient and circular economy.

- Prosperous and innovative cities (“Prosperity”), for attracting national and international investments, having a focus on sustainable development and job creation, making it attractive to invest in the green & blue economy and in clean technology.

Name: Stadtkümmerei Gesellschaft für integrierte Stadtentwicklung mbH
Description:
Die Stadtkümmerei GmbH ist ein berlinweit agierendes Unternehmen der integrierten Stadtentwicklung mit derzeit acht Standorten und mehr als 25 Mitarbeitenden. 

Seit der Gründung im Jahr 2016 engagieren wir uns für eine soziale, digitale, klimafreundliche und produktive Stadtentwicklung und begleiten entsprechende Projekte in unterschiedlichen Berliner Quartieren.

Wir arbeiten mit Stadtteilbezug. Die Lebenswirklichkeit der Menschen vor Ort machen wir uns täglich bewusst, erfahren ihre Sicht auf die Dinge, erarbeiten Strategien, Lösungen und Projekte kooperativ und co-kreativ.

Unsere Vision ist es, gemeinsam Städte zu lebenswerten Orten für alle zu machen und zu erhalten. Dafür setzen wir uns täglich in unserer Arbeit ein. Wir sind vor Ort, in den Quartieren und Nachbarschaften. Uns begeistern die Menschen, mit denen und für die wir arbeiten.

Im Team der Stadtkümmerei kommen Menschen unterschiedlicher Fachrichtungen zusammen. Wir unterscheiden uns in Interessen, Alter, Kenntnissen, Herkunft und vielem mehr und haben diese Vielfalt zu unserer größten Stärke gemacht. Als Team stellen wir uns Herausforderungen, als Team wagen wir uns immer wieder an neue Themen, als Team betreten wir methodisch Neuland und hinterfragen unsere Arbeit jeden Tag neu.

Wir arbeiten gemeinwohlorientiert: Die Stadtkümmerei – Gesellschaft für integrierte Stadtteilentwicklung mbH ist ausnahmslos für öffentliche Auftraggeber:innen oder gemeinnützige Organisationen (vhw – Bundesverband für Wohnen und Stadtentwicklung e.V., Stiftung Hamburger Öffentliche Bücherhallen) tätig.

Name: Stokes Croft Land Trust
Description:
Our mission at the Stokes Croft Land Trust is to acquire and steward buildings and property in Stokes Croft, with and on behalf of the local community, for the long term.

We are a community benefit society (registration number 7004) with exempt charitable status from HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC), which has been created as a grassroots institution, governed and run democratically by its members (local people) to acquire and steward buildings and property in Stokes Croft on behalf of the local community for the long term.

Our goal is to take land out of the speculative capital investment market, and into secure community ownership, thereby enabling long-term affordable and sustainable local development, putting the control of local areas back into the hands of local people.

Name: Bene Esse
Description:
Bene Esse is a small-scale, self-sustaining housing model that puts affordability in the hands of the people who live there—without relying on big developers, outside investors, or government funding.

With Bene Esse, "affordability" means "ability to afford," which is not always the same as developers use it.

Working with existing landowners, Bene Esse installs ‘Pods’—small clusters of 5–7 homes using portable infrastructure to keep start-up costs low.

Once a Bene Esse Pod is established, it becomes a local economic engine, funding essential services through cooperative decision-making.

These self-supporting Pods may someday negotiate with the land owner to purchase the property, negotiate with the land owner to create a trust, and/or move the Pod to a more suitable location if the opportunity arises.

Bene Esse could be considered a franchise for an intentional community ecovillage that emphasizes wealth production and job creation for the surrounding community.

Name: Circular City Aachen
Description:
Was ist die Circular City Aachen?
Unsere Vision: Aachen wird Modellstadt für Kreislaufwirtschaft.
Wir gestalten eine Stadt, in der Ressourcen im Kreislauf bleiben, Innovationen entstehen und Lebensqualität wächst. Unter der Dachmarke Circular City Aachen bündeln wir Kräfte aus Verwaltung, Wissenschaft, Wirtschaft und Zivilgesellschaft, um gemeinsam eine Strategie und Roadmap für Aachen zu entwickeln – orientiert an der Circular City Declaration, dem Ziel der Klimaneutralität bis 2030 und dem Integrierten Klimaschutzkonzept (IKSK).

Im EFRE-geförderten Projekt KreislaufWIRKStatt arbeitet ein Konsortium aus dem Stadtbetrieb und dem Gebäudemanagement der Stadt Aachen, der Concular GmbH und dem Collaborating Centre on Sustainable Consumption and Production (CSCP) gGmbH zusammen. Gemeinsam entwickeln wir zirkuläre Pilotmodelle, messen anhand von Nachhaltigkeits-Indikatoren den Impact unserer Bemühungen und versuchen auf unserer Reise möglichst viele Mitstreiter:innen dafür zu begeistern, sich mit uns für eine nachhaltigere Zukunft in einer lebenswerten Stadt einzusetzen. Und für all das wollen wir auch einen Ort schaffen: Die KreislaufWIRKStatt.

Name: Neighborhoods United SF
Description:
NUSF is a coalition of neighborhood, business, and civic organizations opposing extreme upzoning in San Francisco. We urge the city to pursue a community-centered, balanced approach to meeting the state’s housing mandate without compromising neighborhood identity or livability.

Mission:
Partnering with communities to promote equitable growth, protect resources, ensure public input, and prevent displacement.

Values:
San Francisco’s strength lies in its people, cultures, and neighborhoods. Growth should support, not undermine, these communities. True city-building goes beyond housing units—it requires investment in infrastructure, small businesses, the environment, and quality of life.

NUSF Supports:
- Contextual housing growth that fits existing neighborhoods.
- Enforceable height limits to protect community character.
- Density decontrol without unnecessary height increases.
- A realistic affordable housing plan from SF Planning.
- Preserving historic structures and districts.
- A thorough impact assessment of rapid population growth on infrastructure and the environment.

Description:
About us
Maavarim Northern Negev is an organization that focuses on community-based career development in the rural sector. The organization operates with the professional support of the NGO Be-Atzmi, under the supervision of The Ministry of Welfare and Social Affairs’ community work service, and in cooperation with the Sha’ar Hanegev, Bnei-Shimon and Yoav Regional Councils.
Maavarim Northern Negev is led by a steering committee of all local and national partners, together with an active management that assists in everyday operation. The program is operated by a professional team that includes the program manager, a community social worker, a Career Counselor, an Employer Relations and Partnerships coordinator, and coordinators in the entrepreneurship and training fields. Along side them, local employment promoters and volunteers operate in a wide array of fields[eh1] .

Our vision 
Our goal is to create a local employment infrastructure that responds to the everchanging needs of the job market, while implementing a threefold strategy: Developing community-based capital as a springboard for employees and employers, expanding human capital, and developing an integrated network of employment’s agencies and services.

We organize working groups partnering with organizations addressing specific campus, local, and national urban projects, curate student writing on urban issues in our bulletin, and publish a weekly newsletter with events, news, and opportunities in Cambridge and beyond. We also host a comp, speaker events, and educational workshops throughout the year.

Nous nous engageons chaque jour à vous fournir des services de qualité. Nous sommes avant tout une entreprise familiale et indépendante, à taille humaine. Nous intervenons pour les particuliers, les collectivités et les industries.

Lorsque vos éviers, WC ou lavabos se bouchent, il est nécessaire de faire intervenir des professionnels. Nous effectuons, avant tous travaux, un diagnostic de votre système d’évacuation d’eau. #Sustainability #UrbanDevelopment #Research #UniversityOfOstrava #GlobalExperts

This unique city is a harmonious blend of four contemporary thematic districts, serving as a pivotal hub on international routes that connect crucial directions such as Western Europe-West China, Central Asian Regional Economic Cooperation (CAREC), and North-South. Alatau is aligned with the global strategic initiative, the "New Silk Road."

Our city's four thematic districts – Gate District, Golden District, Growing District, and Green District – have been meticulously designed with a fervor for innovation and a commitment to an exemplary lifestyle. Each district stands as a distinct space, seamlessly integrating modern design, state-of-the-art technological solutions, and breathtaking natural landscapes. Name: *ergo | Urbanistas&Arquitectos
Description:
En ERGO, creemos que las ciudades son más que estructuras: son el reflejo de quienes las habitan. 

Nos especializamos en planificación urbana estratégica, regeneración de espacios públicos y diseño arquitectónico sostenible, con un enfoque integral que pone a las personas en el centro de cada proyecto. Nuestra misión es transformar entornos urbanos en lugares funcionales, accesibles y llenos de vida, impulsando así el desarrollo social y económico.

 ¿Cómo lo hacemos?
 Diseñamos ciudades más caminables, conectadas y sustentables.
 Regeneramos espacios en desuso para devolverles su valor y funcionalidad.
 Aplicamos metodologías innovadoras en urbanismo, en alianza con expertos como DPZ.
 Impulsamos un desarrollo urbano que respeta la historia y proyecta el futuro.

Cada proyecto que desarrollamos es una oportunidad para avanzar hacia "La Ciudad Que Queremos Ser", un modelo de ciudad pensado para el bienestar de todos. Creemos en el urbanismo responsable como herramienta para mejorar la calidad de vida, reducir el impacto ambiental y fortalecer el tejido social.

 Conectemos para transformar.
Si compartes nuestra visión y buscas soluciones innovadoras en desarrollo urbano, movilidad, regeneración de espacios o diseño arquitectónico, hablemos. Juntos, podemos hacer que las ciudades sean más habitables, eficientes y sostenibles.

 Contáctanos y seamos parte del cambio.  #UrbanismoSostenible #LaCiudadQueQueremosSer #ERGO

Name: Net Zero Vermont, Inc.
Description:
About Net Zero Vermont
Net Zero Vermont, Inc. is a 501c3 non-profit organization established to help Vermonters create a shared vision for a sustainable future that provides vibrant, people-centered downtowns and transforms our transportation and energy systems to low carbon options. We design and develop special projects that advance the vision and mission of Net Zero Vermont. One initiative is Walk to Shop, encouraging more people to walk for purpose more often. We’re elevating the importance of public investments in active and shared mobility and the 15 minute neighborhood connecting residents to services. 
With key partners (ie., Vermont Clean Cities Coalition, AALV, NOTCH, VT Foodbank, CCRPC, and Go!VT) Walk to Shop is raising public awareness and making available a stylish shopping trolley.  Target area is Burlington, Winooski, South Burlington, Williston, Richford.

Sustainable Montpelier 2030 was the organization's first project. 

For more information about Net Zero Vermont and to learn more about our community events, visit www.netzerovt.org and WalktoShop.Org

Somos una empresa líder en la rehabilitación de activos inmobiliarios para la mejora de su eficiencia energética y su plusvalía. En nuestro despacho actuamos como Agente Rehabilitador. Somos una compañía con más de 20 años de experiencia en servicios de rehabilitación, procesos de gestión integral, asesoramiento jurídico y administrativo hasta la gestión y transformación de inmuebles.

Asumimos la responsabilidad total del proyecto. Esto incluye la planificación, la obtención de permisos, tramitación de subvenciones, financiación bancaria, la supervisión de cada etapa de la rehabilitación y su posterior revisión final.

Description: Genting Highlands City Centre (GHCC) is a visionary development designed to unite existing and future projects in Genting Highlands into a seamless, world-class city centre. As a key player in the transformation of Genting Highlands, GHCC is setting new standards in high-altitude urban development. Stay connected and follow our journey as we bring this vision to life.

Description:

Cette abréviation signifie Voirie et Réseau Divers, et concerne un ensemble de travaux nécessaires pour aménager un espace ou construire un bâtiment, et le raccorder aux différents réseaux existants.
Voici quelques exemples de nos compétences :
-	Il peut s’agir, par exemple, de la viabilisation de terrains privés, de la réalisation des voies d’accès (privées ou publique)

-	Cet été vous avez envie de vous baigner ? Et vous vous dites qu’une piscine serait du plus bel effet dans votre jardin ? Ne recherchez plus en appelant Boumat Tp c’est la garantie de pouvoir vous baigner. 


-	Nous sommes aussi spécialisés dans l'assainissement (Pose de microstations si on n’a pas le tout à l’égout) et l’aménagement des extérieurs tel que : Enrobé / Béton Désactivé / Pose de pavés / Chemins d’accès privés (allée de garage).

Name: Alton Forward
Description:
Alton Forward is a 501(c)(3) non-profit with the mission of contributing to transformational change and increasing livability in the Greater Alton, IL Area.  

Alton Forward aspires to convene, connect, and work with inspiring leaders, partners, and agencies to support projects and programs that foster opportunity and build social equity.  Our objective is to work with current partners in the greater Alton area to build upon existing capacities, while bringing in new partners, resources, and opportunities for the community.  

Our vision is for the greater Alton, IL region to become a highly livable community for all its residents, with equitable access to services and opportunities.  Alton Forward believes it can access the resources and build collaborative models of success to contribute to transformational betterments across our community.

Name: Asociația Kogayon
Description:
Asociația Kogayon este organizație non-guvernamentală fondată în 2003 având ca obiectiv conservarea patrimoniului natural și dezvoltarea durabilă a regiunii Oltenia de sub Munte.

Am inițiat procesul de declarare ca arie naturală protejată a actualului Parc Național Buila-Vânturarița, ce și-a obținut acest statut în 2004. 

Între 2006 și 2016 am fost parteneri ROMSILVA în structura de administrare a parcului național, cu rol în procesul de conservare a biodiversității, dezvoltare și implementare a programelor de educație de mediu și amenajarea și mentenanța infrastructurii turistice. 

În prezent, obiectul activității noastre include și conservarea patrimoniului cultural și procesul de dezvoltare regională, proiectul principal al organizației fiind Oltenia de sub Munte - geoparc aspirant UNESCO. 

Obiectivul nostru este acela de a accelera dezvoltarea durabilă a regiunii. Pentru a reuși acest lucru, ne concentrăm eforturile pentru obținerea statusului de geoparc UNESCO pentru regiune, într-o strânsă colaborare cu comunitățile locale și cu actorii de la nivel local, regional, național și chiar internațional.
