Name: Rebel Spirit Collective
Description: Rebel Spirit Collective is decolonizing storytelling by expanding the web of storytellers and stories. We are working to move past dominant narratives within aid and development by re-patterning the role that stories play within partnerships, processes and products. In doing this, we invite everyone to take part in the bigger story of impact and global transformation moving from one to myriad pathways of actionable change.

Name: Philippine Institute of Environmental Planners - Qatar Chapter
Description: The Philippine Institute of Environmental Planners – Qatar Chapter is composed of 34 members who were all SPLE passers from 2016-2019. It was formally established in 2019 and was recognized as a full-fledged International Chapter by the National Board of Trustees in 2020 through Board Resolution No. 11-005 Series of 2020. It all started in 2016 wherein petitions for the Environmental Planners Licensure Exam were circulated and well received by the interested takers. In that same year, the first ever Special Professional Licensure Examination for Environmental Planners (SPLE-EnP) happened in Qatar!

Name: Nathan Indonesia Foundation
Description: Nathan Indonesia Foundation is a center for youth development and empowerment in Indonesian regions to build society through the grassroots by facilitating and empowering the potential of youth in their respective regions.

Name: Ville du Futur
Description:
Dans le cadre de leur scolarité chez CESI école d'ingénieur, les élèves de dernière année du cycle d'ingénieur spécialisé en informatique organisent une conférence sur la ville du futur.

Cette conférence présentera des innovations en matière d'énergie renouvelable, de conception durable des bâtiments, d'aménagement urbain intelligent et de possibilités d'application spécifiques a Pau.

Name: Geo-UrbanCity (GUC)

Name: Community Transformation Accelerator Program
Description:
The Community Transformation Accelerator Program (CTAP) is a non-profit organisation founded by André Rheeder in 2021 to empower small businesses in townships and rural areas. CTAP addresses unemployment, poverty, and inequality through technology, upskilling, and mentorship.

Empowering Through Technology CTAP enhances profit margins, improves security, and streamlines operations for 250,000 retail-related businesses with cutting-edge technology solutions, including POS systems and business management tools.

Comprehensive Skills Development Our program trains 750,000 business owners and employees in essential management and retail skills and support participants in obtaining formal qualifications.

Sustained Growth with Mentorship Mentorship by business administration students helps business owners and employees grow operations, build robust distribution networks, and increase profit margins.

Networking Opportunities CTAP connects 250,000 businesses to formal economy networks, fostering referral marketing, sales, and business opportunities.

Online Solid Presence We help 250,000 businesses establish a powerful online presence with hassle-free, all-inclusive marketing solutions.

Access to Growth Funding CTAP assists SMEs in securing stock funding through banking partnerships, focusing on compliance, credibility, competency, and consistency.

Our Vision We envision economic transformation and sustainable job creation by formalising businesses, alleviating poverty, hunger, and inequality, and creating Communities of Hope.

Our Goal CTAP aims to uplift wholesale and retail sectors in townships and rural economies, transforming informal businesses into formal entities.

Join Us CTAP prioritises South African citizens from previously disadvantaged areas, aligning with our mandate to alleviate poverty and promote equality. We are registering for Article 18A to offer investor benefits and contribute to B-BBEE points.

For more information, contact: André Rheeder

Name: Portage Place
Description: Portage Place is located in South Bend, Indiana’s Near Northwest Neighborhood, just minutes from downtown South Bend, the University of Notre Dame, St. Mary’s College, Indiana University South Bend, and the Michiana International Airport.  Pedestrian and bike friendly with easy access to public transportation as well as the toll road, it serves as a crossroads for the community and the anchor for Portage Midtown, a sustainable neighborhood development located in the center of the NNN. A historical icon if the art deco era, originally built in and around 1920, and expanded in the 1940, Portage Place served first as the Ward Baking Company, and then later as the Ford Distribution Center. Abandoned in 2012, it has been rescued and fully renovated and updated with high speed internet, a spacious and comfortable atmosphere and lots of natural light. Spaces range from 200-2000 sq/f. with rents starting as low as $350/mo. Portage Place is currently owned by The Bakery Group LLC, consisting of 5 partners.

To Beautify, Unify & Showcase the Best of Bali. Mural Fest is more than just paint on walls, it is a community driven, economic boosting, reality suspending series of events that feature: Large-Scale Public Art Pieces being created live with Markets & Art Bazaars, Art Exhibitions, AR Experiences, Film Festivals, Fashion Shows, Live Music & DJ’s, Culinary Arts, Kids Activities, Artist Panels, Masterclasses & Workshops, Scavenger Hunts, Contests, Guided Tours and Conferences on NFTs, Crypto, Blockchain, the Metaverse, Health & Wellness, Sustainability, Social Impact and so much more...

Mural Fest is produced by Bali COLLECTiVE.  The bridge from Web2 to Web3 • Developing sustainable ecosystems utilizing Web3 technology. 

The Evolution of Philanthropy
Changing the Paradigm from Giving a Donation to Owning an Asset.

We develop Creator Economies utilizing Web3 technology. We pair artists with high-impact NGOs and mint Impact NFT collections from public art projects, music, fashion and film created during Mural Fest. These NFTs generate wealth that directly support local NGOs and social impact initiatives that serve the planet, wildlife and the people of Bali.

We have created a new platform for philanthropists where we give donors greater security and transparency when supporting NGOs and social impact initiatives.

We are Social Impact Warriors!!!
NFTs, event production and app development are only tools we use in empowering high impact NGOs and supporting our artist communities.

We bring art, music, fashion and film onto the blockchain to build communities that find solutions to some of the most pressing issues we face today.

Name: Investing with North Minneapolis
Description:
The Investing with North Minneapolis project brings together the insight and optimism of Northside residents and organizations with the creative minds of University of Minnesota School of Architecture design students, youth interns, and professionals to further the goal of collaborative community design that responds to the complex history of North Minneapolis.

Together they collaborate to develop urban strategies that will incorporate the values of the North Minneapolis community members. These urban strategies take their form through thoughtful architecture and urban design.

The overall project objective is to develop over time an urban plan and projects for North Minneapolis that will be incorporated in the city plan and built to provide economic opportunities for North Minneapolis developers, builders, businesses, renters and homebuyers. This project empowers communities to foster a healthy urban environment and create equitable opportunity for long term socioeconomic growth.

Name: Unearthing Common Ground
Description:
Web3 and Community Land Trust ([CLT] practitioners have a shared interest in decentralised, collaborative approaches to solving pressing issues, such as climate change, housing insecurity, or racial equity (to name a few) but, with rare exceptions, to date they have essentially operated in isolation from one another. 

CLT practitioners are often unaware of the scale and specifics of web3 technologies and the impact web3 is already making in areas such as community-driven investment, ownership, and governance.  Similarly, web3 project-contributors often do not have strong connections to grassroots, on-the-ground efforts and therefore can be prone to developing projects that are of limited practical value, or at worst with unintended negative consequences, disconnected from the wisdom and guidance available to them.

This project will be based on the principles of learning, discussion, design, implementation, and evaluation - a tried and true approach.  Exactly what projects come out of this multi-year course is hard to say but our hope is to pilot new and innovative approaches to CLT-web3 collaborations.

Name: GAL Alto Bellunese
Description:
Il Gruppo di Azione Locale Alto Bellunese è un’associazione riconosciuta, costituita nel 1995 per gestire il programma LEADER II – un’iniziativa allora promossa dalla Commissione Europea per attuare politiche di sviluppo innovative nelle zone rurali.

Il partenariato del GAL Alto Bellunese, così com’è oggi, è il risultato del percorso di aggregazione iniziato allora, consolidatosi e allargatosi nel corso delle successive programmazioni LEADER. Al momento il partenariato è composto da 9 soci pubblici e 10 soci privati, rappresentativi del territorio Alto Bellunese. 

Il GAL non ha fini di lucro e ha per obiettivo primario la promozione dello sviluppo sostenibile del territorio in cui opera. Ovvero, l’area dei 40 Comuni che appartengono alle cinque Unioni Montane dell’Alto Bellunese: Unione Montana Comelico, Unione Montana Centro Cadore, Unione Montana Cadore Longaronese Zoldo, Unione Montana Valle del Boite e Unione Montana Agordina.

Attraverso la gestione di fondi comunitari, il GAL si fa promotore dell’attivazione di opportunità di crescita economica e di servizi per la vivibilità del territorio in cui opera, per contribuire auspicabilmente all’inversione della tendenza all’abbandono delle terre alte.

Name: CURVANISM WOMEN
Description:
¿Quién construye la ciudad que habitas?
Urbanismo con curvas, cuerpo y comunidad.

Somos una fundación feminista que transforma la ciudad desde el liderazgo de las mujeres. Diseñamos y construimos espacios urbanos con y para mujeres diversas, reivindicando el derecho a habitar, intervenir y decidir sobre el territorio.

Trabajamos en la intersección entre el urbanismo, la obra, el deporte y la acción comunitaria. En nuestros laboratorios de construcción, mujeres de distintas edades aprenden a leer planos, mezclar concreto y usar herramientas, recuperando espacios públicos como rampas, jardineras y zonas de encuentro.

Nos mueve un urbanismo no lineal, sensible y colectivo, que reconoce la experiencia, el cuerpo y la mirada de quienes han sido históricamente excluidas de los procesos urbanos. Defendemos el derecho a la ciudad desde abajo, con prácticas sostenibles, saberes compartidos y vínculos comunitarios.

Creemos en un urbanismo con curvas: diverso, habitable, hecho a mano y con sentido.

Name: BANC Black Action Network
Description:
BANC (Black Action Network Committee) is a movement built from within — shifting culture, healing trauma, and creating economic power in Black communities. We are a media-based nonprofit committed to changing the conditions that keep our people stuck by amplifying voice, building purpose, and equipping our communities to lead.

Our work focuses on three powerful lanes:

1. Youth Empowerment: Through our flagship ImpactFluence program, we mentor at-risk youth with paid opportunities to build real-world skills in media, business, and leadership. We don’t just teach — we train, equip, and embed them in relationships with mentors who look like them and come from the same environments. These young people become creators, entrepreneurs, and community leaders.

2. Community Conversations: Our media-led conversation series confronts the deep issues impacting Black communities — from mental health to masculinity, parenting, colorism, finances, and more. These aren’t panels or lectures — they’re real, raw, and healing conversations that spark cultural shifts and help people name and navigate what they’ve been silently carrying.

3. Business Support & Cultural Strategy: BANC also serves as a strategic resource for Black-led organizations and businesses, offering storytelling, campaigns, and consulting to help them grow and thrive. We believe Black brilliance deserves platforms and infrastructure — and we work to build both.

At BANC, we don't wait for systems to change — we create change from the inside out.

Name: American Impact Ventures, LLC
Description:
American Impact Ventures, LLC is proud to announce the official launch of our full-service Tax Credit Advisory practice. Backed by decades of industry experience and a commitment to driving capital into underserved communities, we’re uniquely positioned to guide developers, investors, and nonprofit sponsors through the complex landscape of federal and state tax-credit programs.

What We Offer:

New Markets Tax Credit (NMTC) advisory and placement: From initial feasibility to closing, we structure NMTC transactions that maximize community impact and investor returns.


Historic Tax Credit (HTC) solutions: We navigate the stringent compliance requirements of federal and state HTC programs, unlocking value for historic rehabilitation projects.

Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) facilitation: We partner with syndicators, housing authorities, and developers to secure LIHTC allocations, making affordable housing projects financially viable.

Opportunity Zone (OZ) advisory: Leveraging the OZ incentive, we craft equity-based solutions to channel private investment into designated census tracts in need of economic revitalization.

Why American Impact Ventures:

CDE Certification Imminent: We expect to be certified as a Community Development Entity (CDE) in the coming months, enabling us to apply for NMTC awards directly.

Proven Track Record: The leadership team has originated, structured, and closed over $800 million in tax-credit transactions nationwide.

This exciting new chapter reinforces our mission: to unlock transformative capital for projects that build stronger, more resilient communities. Whether you’re a developer planning a multifamily affordable housing complex, a nonprofit rehabilitating a historic landmark, or an investor seeking diversified, impact-driven opportunities, American Impact Ventures is your trusted partner.

Reach out today to learn how we can structure and place your next tax-credit transaction.

Als „Thinktank mit Umsetzungsmodus“ identifiziert, organisiert und konzipiert die AKöR Möglichkeitsräume, in denen die praktische und prototypische Zusammenarbeit zwischen Zivilgesellschaft, Politik und Verwaltung, Wissenschaft und Kultur erprobt und ermöglicht wird. Dabei möchte die AKöR wegweisende Initiativen dieser Akteur:innen fördern.

Dafür sind wir aktiv in der Netzwerkarbeit, Informationsvermittlung und fachlichen Erstberatung. Wir verstehen uns gleichsam als Übersetzer:in, Diplomat:in und eigene Initiative. Grundsätzlich versuchen wir, neue und bestehende Initiativen und Zielvorstellungen der unterschiedlichen Akteur:innen zu sich ergänzenden Koop-Projekten zu verbinden.

Der Name „AKöR“ steht für den Anspruch und das Ziel der Initiativgruppe, eine „Anlauf- und Koordinationsstelle für öffentliche Räume“ (AKöR) auf Bezirksebene im Rahmen der Leitlinien für Bürger:innenbeteiligung zu pilotieren und langfristig zu etablieren.

Gleichzeitig markiert der Name AKöR den aktuellen Fokus, als Anschub- und Kooperationsstruktur öffentliche Räume gemeinwohlorientiert zu schaffen und zu erproben.

Name: Sistema Città Società Cooperativa di comunità
Description:
Sistema Città è un progetto che nasce dalle pluri-annuali esperienze pregresse dei suoi soci sul territorio di Roma Capitale in un'ottica di riqualificazione degli spazi urbani condivisi, nonché da precedenti collaborazioni tra attori sociali di varia natura attivi sul territorio sui temi di ambiente, cultura, sviluppo locale e partecipazione cittadina. 
Sistema Città resta fortemente ancorata alla riqualificazione e alla valorizzazione degli spazi comuni all'interno della città metropolitana di Roma: favorendo la cultura del verde, costruendo intorno al verde e agli spazi comuni opportunità di lavoro e nuovi progetti, offrendo nuove letture e nuovi percorsi a chi vive, visita e attraversa la città.
Guardare in maniera sistemica alla città di Roma vuol dire cercare delle soluzioni integrate strettamente legate a una visione d'insieme dei problemi. Gli ambiti in cui viviamo hanno bisogno di nuovi servizi, di relazioni più forti, di cooperazione e meno isolamento e solitudine. E' in questo contesto e da questa consapevolezza che l'esperienza maturata nell'attivismo volontario per i Beni Comuni ha spinto alcuni professionisti ad unire le forze per dare vita a una cooperativa di comunità. Sistema Città ha dunque come scopo quello di rispondere al fabbisogno del territorio e della comunità, attraverso progettualità mirate innovative legate strettamente ad esperienze progettuali dal basso.
Sistema Città ha come obiettivo il miglioramento della vita della comunità di Roma, con un bagaglio già ben avviato all'interno delle associazioni partner della cooperativa di comunità, in collaborazione con le istituzioni, le università, le fondazioni, le imprese e le cooperative sociali. Il sostegno delle fasce svantaggiate con il reinserimento nel mondo del lavoro, la compensazione del CO2 e la lotta ai cambiamenti climatici, la cura degli spazi pubblici e dell'ambiente, la cultura e il turismo diffuso sono alcuni dei progetti già in atto che Sistema Città promuove e sviluppa.

Name: REURBANIZAR
Description:
A regularização fundiária é um passo fundamental para transformar realidades urbanas e garantir direitos essenciais à população. No Brasil, cerca de metade dos imóveis urbanos ainda são irregulares, o que impede o acesso dos moradores a serviços básicos como água, luz, saneamento e infraestrutura adequada.

Na Reurbanizar, entendemos que a regularização fundiária vai muito além da simples formalização da propriedade. Trata-se de um processo que envolve ações jurídicas, urbanísticas, ambientais e sociais, que asseguram a segurança jurídica aos moradores e promovem a inclusão social e o desenvolvimento sustentável das cidades.

Quando um imóvel ou bairro é regularizado, os moradores passam a ter o título de propriedade reconhecido oficialmente, o que lhes garante proteção legal e a possibilidade de investir e valorizar seu patrimônio. Além disso, a regularização facilita o acesso a políticas públicas e infraestrutura urbana, melhorando a qualidade de vida e promovendo a integração social.

A regularização fundiária também impulsiona o desenvolvimento econômico local, pois aumenta a base tributária dos municípios e estimula investimentos em áreas antes negligenciadas. É uma ferramenta poderosa para construir cidades mais justas, seguras e sustentáveis.

Na Reurbanizar, estamos comprometidos em apoiar e realizar esse processo, contribuindo para que milhares de famílias tenham acesso à moradia digna e aos direitos que merecem. A regularização fundiária é um investimento no futuro das nossas cidades e na vida das pessoas.

Entre em contato conosco e saiba como podemos ajudar a transformar sua comunidade por meio da regularização fundiária.

Name: BDITS LLC
Description:
At BDITS, we believe the future of thriving, resilient communities lies in the seamless connection between the physical and the digital. Our mission is to empower municipalities, campuses, and civic organizations with living Digital Twins and the Digital Thread that ties every project, plan, and decision together.

Unlike static maps or siloed systems, our platform creates a continuously evolving digital replica of a city or campus — integrating historical data, real-time sensor inputs, operational insights, and community feedback into a single, open, and interactive model. This evergreen digital twin is more than a visualization tool; it is a transparent decision-making environment that fosters trust, accountability, and engagement.

For citizens, BDITS provides a clear seat at the table — making urban planning transparent, interactive, and collaborative. For city leaders, it reduces risk, improves governance, and enables data-driven scenario testing for infrastructure, safety, sustainability, and economic development. For partners and investors, BDITS represents a scalable, defensible platform at the heart of the multi-billion-dollar smart cities and urban tech market.

Our solution enables stakeholders to:
	•	Visualize proposed projects in 3D and understand impacts before implementation.
	•	Run simulations for crowd safety, disaster response, or infrastructure resilience.
	•	Break down silos between departments, improving efficiency and collaboration.
	•	Build citizen trust through open data, transparency, and accountability.
	•	Reduce costly mistakes by “testing before investing.”

BDITS is not just building software — we’re building the digital backbone of tomorrow’s cities. By bridging infrastructure, community, and innovation, we help create smarter, safer, and more sustainable environments for everyone.

 Learn more: BDITS.io
 Contact: info@bdit.io

RQST is your quick, curated update on local government planning activity - policies, public documents, news, and land-use notices—all in one place.

Frustrated by the endless hours required to manually check council websites for updates, two friends with over 50 years’ combined experience in urban planning and digital innovation came together to build RQST. The platform was designed with one purpose: to make staying informed effortless.

Every day, councils across NSW publish planning notices that could impact your project, your clients, or your community. But with 100+ local government websites—and no centralised system—it’s easy to miss something important. That’s where we come in.

RQST automatically collects and organises these notices so you don’t have to. Whether you're an engineer, architect, urban planner, environmental consultant, or part of a community organisation, you’ll receive updates tailored to your location and interests.

You can discover zoning changes, infrastructure projects, and public consultations - all from one central dashboard. Save items, share with your team, and receive alerts via email as soon as something relevant is published.

We believe that critical information should be accessible, searchable, and timely - without the admin burden. That’s why RQST offers a premium experience with a free 2 week trial and no setup required.

Join the professionals already using RQST to save time, reduce risk, and stay ahead of planning decisions in their region.

Name: Gemeinde Affoltern

1146 tritt der Name Affoltern i.E. erstmals in Erscheinung. Das Wort Affoltern stammt aus dem althochdeutschen affoltera, apfolter, und bedeutet "Apfelbaum" oder "bei den Apfelbäumen".

Das heutige Gemeindegebiet umfasst eine Fläche von rund 11,5 Quadratkilometern, davon ist rund 1/5 mit Wald bewachsen. Affoltern ist eine von 39 Gemeinden des Verwaltungskreises Emmental und besteht aus den drei Hauptweilern Affoltern, Weier und Häusernmoos. Weiter gehören teilweise die Ortsweiler Kaltacker und Schmidigen-Mühleweg zu Affoltern. Der höchste Punkt befindet sich auf der Lueg (887 m ü.M.).

Nennenswert ist auch die eigene Wasserquelle, welche sich im Ortsteil Sackgraben befindet und zugleich der tiefste Punkt der Gemeinde ist. 

Anzahl Einwohner : ca. 1'100 Personen.

Name: Great Towns
Description:
Great Towns are places with a future even more exciting and attractive than its past.  Great Towns are places that are home to a thriving economy, a welcoming community, and the prospect for an even more prosperous future.


What's holding your community back or keeping it from attracting the people, ideas, and investment you imagine?  Great Towns, Inc. is a 501 (c) 3 non-profit organization founded in 2012 for one purpose:  To help smaller Indiana towns and communities become the strong, vibrant, and exciting places they were meant to be, as envisioned by the people who live there.  How can we help catalyze your community's planning process? A new website? A town hall meeting?  A community-wide survey? A signature fundraiser? Preserving buildings with a history? We can help and we want to be part of your team!

Our primary focus is on towns with 10,000 and fewer residents.  Working with your town's project directors, we deliver and implement specifically-tailored technical support and capacity-building programs to help your town become a successful great town -- one that is more inviting, economically strong, and resilient.  Our core sectors of expertise are community engagement, long-range planning, enhancing the built environment, preserving the legacy, and placemaking.

Name: Blue Sea Consilium
Description:
During the last twenty years our military and government veterans have been deploying overseas continuously to far away jungles, mountains, and cities of the world building communities in line with United States foreign policy.

Many regions of conflict and underdevelopment around the world have been shown to rebuild themselves to become safe and flourishing communities.

 

For so many of these veterans, transitioning from military or government service to the civilian world means neglecting most of that experience in order to learn a new job skill. What if instead of letting all that experience in nation building go to waste we used it to rebuild the poorest neighborhoods here in the United States?

 

Blue Sea Conslium, B.S.C. is a nonprofit organization on a mission to give our cities the same attention and focus given to the places we deployed. The core of every decision B.S.C. makes considers whether it guides the community to help itself. Our answer to changing an atmosphere of crime is not gentrification but building the community from the inside starting with the weakest points.

Our mission is to redefine the way we rent and own property by leveraging blockchain technology to purchase an array of luxury properties across the globe.

Membership to Nexus Properties is unlocked by purchasing the NFT.

Membership NFTs will generate a daily token yield that members can use to reserve nights at the luxury properties.

Being a member/owner comes with certain governance rights, such as voting on which cities the DAO expands to, what IRL events the DAO hosts, and more. 

DAO members will also have a say in key decisions the DAO makes regarding the properties and experiences going forward.

ROADMAP 

Launch Start of Q2 2022:  
The Nexus website, Discord and Twitter go live.

Minting May 1, 2022:
 Memberships go on sale!

Acquisitions Q2 2022:  
Shortly after our launch, we will begin the process of acquiring our property on 30A.

Experiences Q2 2022: 
The Nexus DAO welcomes its first guests to the 30A location.

Token Issuance Q3 2022:  
After properties have been acquired, we will begin issuing tokens to NFT holders.

Reservations Q3 2022: 
Members can begin using tokens to reserve stays at The Nexus DAO’s luxury properties.

Premium Concierge Q3 2022: 
Launch of premium concierge service. Providing Global Members with elite services and one of a kind experiences during their stay.

Expansion Q3 2022: 
After successfully launching our first location, The Nexus DAO members will decide where we expand to next.

Potential locations could include places like: 
| Maui | Cabo | Dubai | Paris | Belize | Miami | Tahiti | Vail | London | Thailand | Amalfi Coast |

הקריירה שלהם ממוקם במרכז הארץ.

כדי לאפשר להורים לפתח את הקריירה האישית בצד בניית המשפחה, החליטה עיריית לוד
ליזום תוכנית יחידה מסוגה בישראל שתעניק לכם להורים, ובעיקר לאמהות, מעטפת ארגונית
וחינוכית איכותית תומכת. לצורך זה מוקמת בלוד מנהלת עירונית ייעודית, שתרכז ותנהל
את המערך כולו.

המרכיב הלוגיסטי:
פעילות מאורגנת בתום שעות הפעילות של המסגרות הפורמליות ועד לשעות הערב.
הפעילות כוללת: חוגים, פעילות העשרה, הכנת שיעורי בית מפוקחת ואפילו הזנה.
המערך יעניק מענה מלא בחופשות, בימי חול מועד וגם בימי שביתה.
סל הפעילויות בעלות אטרקטיבית.

החינוך המטפח:
המערך כולו ילווה על ידי צוות פדגוגי מקצועי
כל קבוצת ילדים יוצמד מדריך
חללי הפעילויות במרכזים הקהילתיים יחולקו על פי גילאיי הילדים

התכנון השכונתי:
תשתית בינוי המותאמת לכלל פעילויות
מרכז קהילתי פנים שכונתי שיהווה את לב המערך
מעונות, גנים ובי"ס יסודיים, הממוקמים במרחק הליכה עד 300-200 מ' מהבית
רשת שבילי אופניים ומדרכות צל הפרוסים על כל השכונה

הביטחון האישי:
כל מקומות הפעילויות לרבות המרחבים הפתוחים, מפוקחים ומאובטחים
אפליקציית עדכון ובקרה להורים לאורך היום
תנועה ממקום למקום ללא הסעות

סיוע תעסוקתי:
נטוורקינג פנים עירוני לעובדים במגוון התחומים
מרכז השמה פנים עירוני לשילוב עובדים בחברות הפועלות בלוד
מרחבים פנים שכונתיים לעבודה מרחוק
יעוץ מסובסד לפיתוח קריירה


בשיתוף: עיריית לוד, החברה הכלכלית, משרד הבינוי והשיכון ורשות מקרקעי ישראל.

בלוד הוקמה יחידת אכלוס עירונית המסייעת למתעניינים ולתושבים חדשים בקליטה קלה ומהירה.

Name: Hawi Urban Systems Lab
Description:
Hawi Urban Systems is a digital-first urban development studio. 

We design cities as complete operating systems before physical construction begins. Our work starts with a city-scale digital twin that models land, infrastructure, economy, governance, and daily life as one integrated system.

This approach allows cities to be tested, stressed, and refined early. It reduces risk, improves capital efficiency, and protects people and land from avoidable failure.

What we do:
- Build city-scale digital twins for new developments
- Define urban operating models independent of land or capital structure
- Simulate energy, water, food, mobility, economy, and governance together
	• Translate digital truth into phased physical development
	
We do not start with buildings. We start with flows: energy, water, food, movement, and value creation. If a system fails digitally, it does not get built physically.

Who this is for:
- Developers planning large-scale or multi-phase projects
- Public and private partners shaping new urban districts
- Investors seeking long-horizon, lower-risk urban development
- Institutions exploring future-ready city models

Hawi Urban Systems is currently developing a universal city archetype and pilot applications in East Africa. If you work in city building, infrastructure, climate, housing, finance, or governance, this page documents the thinking behind the work.

Name: aester: regenerative development
Description:
We are devoted to deep rooted flowering of people, places, and projects. We orient toward the radiantly beautiful, but grounded potential that wants to come to life through people, places, and projects. This is a deliberate shift away from parsing out problems and designing technical solutions. Our work is seeking to evoke aliveness, integration, and wholeness. We bridge the known and the emerging—supporting founders, leaders, and communities ready to release outdated paradigms and cultivate radically new ways of thinking, being, and creating.

Rooted in integrative processes, adaptive leadership, and a profound understanding of living systems and the ways that power and life force flows, we illuminate pathways for regenerative development that honor complexity, wisdom, and the mysterious unfolding of life. We are committed to healing our collective relationships—with land, with Earth, and with each other—by bringing forth projects and practices that reveal our deepest collaborative potential.

Our practice is an invitation to step beyond conventional boundaries, to listen deeply, and to co-create thriving futures that emerge from reverence, intuition, and a profound recognition of the wild and sublime entanglement of ecology with our common humanity.

Name: The Pride Council Network
Description:
The Pride Council Network (PCN) is a vetted referral network of LGBTQ+ professionals and trusted allies.
PCN is structured on a Fractional Family Office model: one point of entry, a curated bench of professionals, and a human-led matching process. Instead of sending people to search through dozens of directories or cold-call providers, PCN routes community members to the right professional based on need, fit, and availability.
How it works
Submit a request through the PCN Client Concierge
PCN reviews and coordinates the connection
Typical response window: 24–48 hours
PCN remains the matchmaker, not a public directory
Who PCN connects you to Finance, legal, tax, real estate, mental health, healthcare, business services, and other high-need categories as the network expands.
For professionals
Referral Partners (service providers and connectors), active or passive
Career pathway exploration for those who want to serve the community through PCN-aligned firms, including our Financial Planning & Strategy Division (licensing/compliance may apply for compensated roles)
Flagship launch: Nashville, with expansion after the model is stable and repeatable.

Name: CityARA
Description:
CityARA is a research-led urban AI platform for cities and public institutions.

We help cities turn years of citizen participation, surveys, and urban data into usable insight and everyday analytical capacity, without losing trust, transparency, or human judgment.

CityARA combines transparent AI-supported analysis of citizen input with a no-code urban analytics workspace, enabling city staff across departments to explore data, reuse past participation, and answer practical urban questions without relying on technical specialists.

Designed for the public sector, CityARA is built on ethical AI principles: full transparency, human oversight, data ownership by cities, and privacy-first deployment aligned with GDPR.

Based in Helsinki, Finland. Built and led by researchers: Dr. Malekzadeh and his team. Focused on strengthening democratic, data-informed urban decision-making.

We are dedicated to relational repair, cultural resilience, and economic justice, advancing strategies that transform historically disinvested neighborhoods into thriving, self-determined communities.

Founded by cultural strategist and artist Angel Iset Dozier, BCD began as a grassroots movement to center Black voices and lived experiences in rural and urban environments. Over the past decade, we have evolved into a multi-layered lab for community innovation—bridging arts, research, and organizing to shape equitable development and long-term prosperity.

Our signature initiatives include:
• The Durham Marshall Plan (Hayti Community Healing Plan): a $10M blueprint for community-led restoration and wealth-building.
• The Fayetteville Street Corridor Fellows Project: cultivating neighborhood leaders through storytelling, advocacy, and economic empowerment.
• Juneteenth in the Neighborhoods: Unified by Our Pride: a week-long festival uplifting Durham’s cultural heritage across multiple historic neighborhoods.
• Unpaid Labor Exhibition: an arts-driven exploration of intellectual property, hidden labor, and community storytelling.
• Be Well, The Community Wealth-Building Lab: designing tools such as the Relational Reparations Circles to sustain cultural institutions and grassroots leadership.

At BCD, we believe culture is infrastructure. Our work connects people, resources, and imagination to build futures where Black communities are not only remembered but resourced, respected, and restored.

We partner with artists, elders, educators, policymakers, and institutions who share our commitment to justice and creativity. Together, we are rewriting the story of Durham and beyond—where community wealth is measured not only in dollars, but in relationships, memory, and legacy.

Le Groupe AMP réunit les opérateurs qui agissent pour le développement, l’attractivité et la transformation de la Métropole Aix-Marseille-Provence. FCD AMP dynamise les centres-villes en valorisant des locaux commerciaux, en soutenant le commerce de proximité et en accompagnant le renouvellement urbain aux côtés de la SPL Façonéo et de la SPLA-IN : lutte contre l’habitat indigne, permis de louer, requalification du parc ancien.

OFS AMP déploie le Bail Réel Solidaire pour faciliter l’accession à la propriété en dissociant foncier et bâti, rendant le logement abordable et durable pour les ménages.

Façonéo et SOLEAM, opérateurs historiques de l’aménagement, réalisent projets urbains, équipements publics et opérations structurantes pour améliorer durablement le cadre de vie métropolitain.

Nautismed œuvre à la structuration de la filière nautique, améliore les infrastructures portuaires et soutient un tourisme durable et innovant lié à la mer.

One Provence Promotion accompagne la promotion immobilière métropolitaine, développe des projets résidentiels et tertiaires et contribue à une offre qualitative et équilibrée sur le territoire.

Avec Énergies de Provence, le Groupe AMP renforce aussi la transition énergétique via un immobilier plus vertueux et la production d’énergies renouvelables.

En fédérant toutes ces missions, le Groupe AMP propose une réponse globale aux enjeux d’aujourd’hui : attractivité, logement, économie, cadre de vie et transition écologique.

Name: Zamia Común
Description:
Zamia Común helps cities and communities design and implement projects that align urban systems with ecological function. We work at the intersection of environmental planning, circular-economy strategy, and participatory design—translating data and community priorities into spatial, policy, and behavioral change.

Building on years of experience leading city-environmental collaborations, Zamia Común applies proven methodologies that connect technical assessment with civic engagement. Through Urban Ocean, we’ve led the design and delivery of seven pilot projects across Latin America and Asia, organized the Urban Ocean Summit, secured NOAA funding, and supported cities in mapping material flows, identifying leakage points, and advancing circular interventions such as reuse and refill systems.

In Florida, our work implementing Shores Forward connected Miami, Tampa Bay, and Orlando in advancing coastal resilience and water-quality improvements. Projects ranged from participatory environmental planning—helping communities identify and prioritize ecological goals—to a sea-level-rise story-map for Tampa, displayed in museums and major resilience forums.

The goal behind Zamia is to be creative in the way we help society advance towards an ethos of merging science, design, and culture to inspire tangible environmental action. We have created and led initiatives that engage students, families and businesses through Plastic Free Cities and Fin Fest’s Education Village.

Zamia Común envisions cities as living systems—where infrastructure supports life, biodiversity guides design, and water and soil are treated as active partners in resilience. Our services span circular-economy planning, nature-based urban design, spatial storytelling, and mapping—helping communities turn ecological vision into implementable reality.

Acreditamos que é possível unir negócios, impacto social e preservação ambiental. 

Nosso compromisso vai além do mel: ele está na transformação das comunidades, na valorização das famílias, na proteção da biodiversidade e na construção de uma cadeia produtiva mais justa, colaborativa e consciente.

 O que fazemos:
 Capacitação gratuita para apicultores.
 Fornecimento de equipamentos e suporte técnico.
 Comercialização do mel e derivados, garantindo mercado justo.
 Geração de impacto social e ambiental.

 Nossa missão:
Capacitar, conectar e transformar vidas através da apicultura sustentável e de impacto social.

 Nossos valores:
Impacto social, sustentabilidade, ética, inclusão, colaboração e compromisso com as pessoas e com o planeta.
Juntos, criamos um mundo onde o desenvolvimento econômico caminha lado a lado com a justiça social e o cuidado com o meio ambiente.

Name: Community Board of Development
Description:
The Community Board of Development (CBD) is a community-governed economic institution that plans, finances, coordinates, and oversees local development initiatives to build self-sufficient township economies.

CBD exists to do what fragmented projects, NGOs, and short-term programmes fail to do: govern development as a system, not as isolated interventions.
Why CBD Exists:

Township development fails not because of lack of ideas or funding, but because of:
1. Fragmented initiatives with no coordination
2. Weak local governance over development resources
3. External actors designing solutions without community ownership
4. No institutional memory or accountability

As a result, money enters communities and leaves without building lasting economic capacity.

CBD was created to close this institutional gap.

Name: Queens Community Board 5
Description:
Community Board 5, Queens works to improve the Ridgewood, Maspeth, Middle Village and Glendale communities, which are part of Community District 5, Queens. Within our borders, we also have a small portion of Woodside and Rego Park. There are currently 47 members of our Board. The members of the Board are all volunteers appointed by the Queens Borough President. There is a small paid staff consisting of a District Manager, two Community Associates, and a Community Assistant.

Each Board Member is asked to serve on at least two committees of the Board. Each committee is responsible for understanding and working towards improving services, or making recommendations for improvement in such areas as Transportation, Public Transit, Land Use, Parks, Sanitation, Health, Education, the Environment, Housing, Libraries, Public Safety, and Youth Services.

Every second Wednesday of the month, we conduct a full board meeting that is accessible to the public. During the month, committee meetings are also conducted. The board is meant to serve as a local representative body of City government. Board members must live within the boundaries of the board area, or have a significant employment interest in the area.

Community Board 5, Queens conducts public hearings related to the city budget, zoning issues, capital projects, and other important matters. Like the monthly board meeting and committee meetings, the hearings give the public and board members the opportunity to have input and express their views. The recommendations made by the Community Board are advisory, but the Board can play an important role in zoning matters, and in getting things accomplished for the community.

Name: Hell's Kitchen South Coalition
Description:
The Hell’s Kitchen South Coalition (HKSC) is an alliance of diverse community stakeholders, composed of residents, community service providers, business owners and elected officials, aiming to preserve, protect and strengthen the community of Hell’s Kitchen South (HKS). The primary goal of the Hell’s Kitchen South Coalition Neighborhood Plan (the Plan) is to ensure that the Hell’s Kitchen
South (HKS) neighborhood is a healthy and safe place to live and work. It covers the area from West 30th Street to West 42nd Street, 8th Avenue to 11th Avenue, located in Manhattan Community District 4 (MCD4).

The Plan, which focuses on nine Port Authority owned properties, is a companion to the Port Authority Bus Terminal (PABT) replacement project. Both the bus terminal project and the redevelopment of the Port Authority properties west of the existing bus terminal, will profoundly affect the community for generations to come.

The Plan goals are to:
• Significantly reduce air pollution, aiming for the highest possible air quality standards
• Accommodate all buses in enclosed buildings and ramps with air filtration to reduce air pollution
• Improve pedestrian safety by modifying bus infrastructure and operations
• Add a substantial amount of new public green space, reconnecting the neighborhood
• Strengthen the traditional residential core with new, permanently affordable housing
• Promote new small local retail stores with affordable rents that serve a mixed-income population
• Generate funding for the PABT replacement, new open spaces and other community improvements using a Transfer of Development Rights (TDR) mechanism.

The Plan reflects extensive input from community residents and businesses and has greatly benefitedfrom suggestions from MCB4, the Port Authority, City Council Land Use staff, the Department of City Planning (DCP) and the Department of Transportation (DOT).

Name: Community Inspiration Project
Description:
A group of Fairfield Gonzales residents have come together around an innovative tool to connect community members and inspire them to improve the local quality of life!

A map of community assets is now available online and in a printed version. Find it in our building and in various neighbourhood locations.

This is a community-based project. Each asset has been submitted by a community member, and we encourage anyone to contact us if you would like to add other assets to the map (event@fairfieldcommunity.ca)

Link to map: https://www.google.com/maps/d/viewer?mid=1azQcWhIvagYf8xzOzD1bDPxqecYCUahi&ll=48.416209764730084%2C-123.34553511813283&z=14 

Legend of assets:
-Natural assets are sources of inspiration that exist within the community naturally like Moss Rock Park. 
-Social assets are sources of inspiration that provide social benefit like November Project. 
-Economic assets are sources of inspiration that provide value for community members like Cottage Bakery. 
-Public assets are sources of inspiration that deliver benefit for community members like the Fairfield Gonzales Community Association. 
-Built assets are sources of inspiration that cultivate connection like Fair Field boulevard garden
-Intangible assets are things like undiscovered or underutilized skills, expertise, and willingness to volunteer and participate in working toward common goals. 

Background about the project: 
In the summer 2019, the Streetlife Committee decided to develop an asset-mapping project. The larger goal was to engage and inspire the community around what is positive in the neighbourhood. Local residents and stakeholders could submit feedback about how to keep improving the quality of life in Fairfield Gonzales.

Each neighbourhood has its particular assets, whether they are natural, built, economic, social…. Bringing value to where we live!

Atlanta Neighborhood Planning Units (NPU's) are citizen advisory councils that make recommendations to the Atlanta Mayor and Atlanta City Council on zoning, land-use, licensing, and other planning issues. The NPU system was established in 1974 by Atlanta Mayor Maynard Jackson to provide an opportunity for citizens to participate actively in the Comprehensive Development Plan, which is Atlanta’s vision for the next 5, 10, and 15 years

Some background on ATL Neighborhoods & NPU’s:

Atlanta is comprised of over 240 unique neighborhoods, which are grouped in to 25 NPUs

The NPUs are the official avenue for residents to express concerns and provide input in developing plans to address the needs of each neighborhood, as well as to receive updates from City government. To read more about the NPU system, see: https://www.atlantaga.gov/government/departments/city-planning/office-of-zoning-development/neighborhood-planning-unit-npu

Name: aester: regenerative development & leadership

We are devoted to deep rooted flowering of people, places, and projects. We orient toward the radiantly beautiful, but grounded potential that wants to come to life through people, places, and projects. This is a deliberate shift away from parsing out problems and designing technical solutions. Our work is seeking to evoke aliveness, integration, and wholeness. We bridge the known and the emerging—supporting founders, leaders, and communities ready to release outdated paradigms and cultivate radically new ways of thinking, being, and creating.

Rooted in integrative processes, adaptive leadership, and a profound understanding of living systems and the ways that power and life force flows, we illuminate pathways for regenerative development that honor complexity, wisdom, and the mysterious unfolding of life. We are committed to healing our collective relationships—with land, with Earth, and with each other—by bringing forth projects and practices that reveal our deepest collaborative potential.

Our practice is an invitation to step beyond conventional boundaries, to listen deeply, and to co-create thriving futures that emerge from reverence, intuition, and a profound recognition of the wild and sublime entanglement of ecology with our common humanity.

Our platform centralizes geospatial data layers and delivers real-time insights, helping teams work together more efficiently. Whether identifying opportunities, analyzing policy constraints, tracking projects and precedents, seeing a project in context, doing your due diligence, or building 3d models, Ratio.City ensures every party involved aligns around a common framework.

Key capabilities include:

- Collaborative project management: share insights, track potential sites, and build unified strategies

- Comprehensive zoning & policy analysis: powered by the largest database of its kind in Canada

- Advanced site evaluation: identify underutilized land and assess feasibility with 3D massing

- Custom data integration: overlay proprietary datasets for deeper, more tailored analysis

By reducing the time spent gathering and interpreting data, Ratio.City enables professionals to focus on making informed decisions that shape sustainable, resilient cities.

אנו פועלים יחד עם יזמים לקידום פרוייקטים ברחבי העיר, תוך שמירה על הליך שקוף ותקין אל מול בעלי הדירות.

Name: Capulet Africa
Description:
Capulet Africa | Community Infrastructure Platform

Capulet Africa is a community infrastructure company working with governments and local partners across ECOWAS and selected high potential markets. Current priorities include Sierra Leone, Ghana, Guinea and neighbouring states, along with pathfinder projects in Malawi and Tanzania. Capulet designs and delivers integrated sites that combine regenerative agriculture, cold chain warehousing, renewable power, digital connectivity, water and sanitation, schools and skills hubs, medical centres and bioclimatic housing and business parks.

Each site is structured as a platform rather than a single asset, typically anchored by around 10MW of clean power to lower energy costs, support local industry and attract private capital. The goal is to improve food security, create decent work and support inclusive, climate resilient growth while building a pipeline of bankable projects that can scale across the continent.

Name: Lighthouse Development Cooperative
Description:
Founded in the Kootenay region of British Columbia, the Lighthouse Development Cooperative enables small companies to come together to create resilient, sustainable commercial and community facilities. 
We are witnessing the escalating effects of climate-related extreme weather - prolonged drought, relentless wildfires, extreme flooding. The impact on our communities is far-reaching, calling urgently for greater resilience and regional self-sufficiency. Fostering economic and climate resilience starts with the localization of clean energy and food security, making supply chains shorter and stronger. 
At the core of our communities, our building stock is ageing and inefficient. We need to re-imagine buildings as integrated multi-use systems - whether community buildings, schools, commercial, retail and residential.  Our vision is to create blueprints for the integration of energy generation and storage, capture of energy flows, district heating, water management - using excess energy to charge electric vehicles and power controlled environment growing systems. By fabricating buildings from locally available materials such as mass timber we keep the focus on local jobs and regional capacity building.
While few of the individual technologies we propose are novel, the challenge lies in the system integration - making the whole greater than the sum of the individual parts. 
We wish to create 'lighthouse projects' that illuminate pathways for change - transparent blueprints for creating successful, secure communities. With the wellbeing of our communities at stake there is no more time for half-measures. Let's get it done - together.

Name: INDIGENOUS FOUNDATION OF ALIGNED RASTAFARI INITIATIVES
Description:
The 'Indigenous Foundation of Aligned Rastafari Initiatives' (I.F.A.R.I.) is a Federal registered Non-For-Profit based in Ontario, Canada. I.F.A.R.I. was founded to address and support a severely underserved community - the Indigenous Rastafari. The community started in the late 60's by members of the wider Indigenous Caribbean community who migrated to Canada in search of economic opportunities due to the economic wars of the 1960's, 70's and 80's throughout the Caribbean region. Amidst the marginalization and oppression due to racism and discrimination this specific was further targeted, misunderstood and alienated due to their unique cultural practices and expressions.

Our criteria will include Education, Food Security, Economic - Access/ Development/Security, Indigenous Plant Medicine and its Indigenous Taxonomy/Economic Value/Sustainability/Security, Health and Wellness, Housing, Family Support, Youth Upliftment, Support for the Elderly-Mental Health Wellness/Empowerment/Security, Technology, Trades, Arts and Culture. This fulfills the Rastafari Creed and most definitely addresses the current and relevant needs of the core community.

Name: Tapuwae Cultural Footprints
Description:
Tapuwae Cultural Footprints Ltd is a Māori development consultancy. Through our work, we help weave together cultural integrity, environmental restoration, and strategic planning. Our vision is to create pathways that are courageous and future-focused.  We bridge mātauranga Māori with contemporary practice to create pathways that are innovative, practical, and deeply grounded in Te Ao Māori. Our approach is bold, unapologetically Māori, and transformative. Kaupapa Māori is not an add-on, it is the foundation for sustainable development, resilience, and meaningful change. We partner with hapū, iwi, marae, councils, government agencies, and communities to ensure Māori voices are not only heard but embedded in the policies, strategies, and plans that shape Aotearoa’s future. 

Our services include:
Kaupapa Māori Strategy and Planning – developing frameworks grounded in tikanga and mātauranga that enable transformation
Resource Management Planning – applying Te Tiriti o Waitangi, cultural values, and legislative frameworks to protect Māori interests in environmental and resource management
Cultural Landscape and Heritage Protection – providing strategies and mechanisms to preserve cultural landscapes and sites of significance to tangata whenua, such as wāhi tapu and pā. 
Cultural Impact Assessments and Reports – preparing comprehensive assessments and reports to inform resource consent applications across Aotearoa
Historical Research – undertaking historical research drawing on Māori Land Court records, archives, and collections held in regional and national museums and libraries.
Environmental and Cultural Services – developing environmental restoration and regeneration projects that strengthen the resilience of the environment 
Māori Engagement Plans – designing kaupapa Māori-led engagement plans
Māori Media and Communications – creating innovative Māori media content and digital solutions, to preserve and revitalise Māori knowledge and narratives.

- Revenue Growth: Transform cultural spaces into economic engines that boost sales.
- Funding & Investment: Build data-backed strategies that attract long-term support.

- Quranic Theme Park attracting 100,000 visitors & approximately $500k in ticket sales for just the first week of opening.

At The Fourth Voice, we do things differently. We believe culture is an economy and every space tells a story. We co-create with our clients, shaping visions grounded in local identity, measurable impact, and community pride.

Name: Stain PRETTI Inc.
Description:
At Stain PRETTI Inc., we are not just building programs—we are crafting legacies of transformation. As a boutique community development firm, our purpose is clear and ambitious: to leave an indelible "stain" of progress and possibility on the communities we serve. This isn’t just a mark of positive change—it’s a bold disruption of cycles of inequity, a dismantling of systemic barriers, and an empowerment of individuals to rewrite their futures through initiatives rooted in excellence and equity. We are committed to creating community focused programs and organizations that shatter barriers, disrupt the status quo, and create a world where underserved populations thrive and achieve excellence.

Name: AM ADLER
Description:
AM ADLER ist mehr als ein Ort. Es ist eine Einladung, gemeinsam Wirkung zu entfalten.

Im Leipziger Westen, mitten im Wandel, liegt ein Platz, der lange still war – und jetzt laut werden darf. AM ADLER ist unsere Antwort auf die Frage, wie Raum wieder Bedeutung bekommt. Wie Stadt wieder Dialog wird. Wie 10 m² Fläche reichen können, um 500 m² zu beleben – und ein ganzes Quartier zu berühren.

Der zentrale Anziehungspunkt: der Leuchtturm am Adler. Eine 2,5 Meter hohe, illuminierte Kommunikationsskulptur, die mehr ist als nur Werbeträger – sie ist Bühne, Bühne für Geschichten mit Haltung, für soziale Anliegen, für Marken, die etwas zu sagen haben. Jede Fläche am Leuchtturm wirkt doppelt – als Impuls im Quartier und als Zeichen nach außen.

AM ADLER bietet nicht einfach Werbeplatz – sondern Kontext, Nachbarschaft, Sichtbarkeit. Wer hier bucht, wird nicht nur gesehen, sondern verstanden. Wer sich hier zeigt, verändert.

Wir glauben: In einer Zeit, in der so viel Kommunikation ins Leere läuft, braucht es wieder Orte, an denen Begegnung stattfindet – real, greifbar, sinnlich.

AM ADLER ist dieser Ort.

Für Institutionen, die Vertrauen wollen. Für Marken, die Sinn stiften wollen.

Für Menschen, die sich zeigen wollen – mit dem, was sie wirklich bewegt.

Elle s'occupe de l'accompagnement des collectivités territoriales dans leur développement stratégique et leur attractivité. 

Elle a développée une approche holistique et créée des services spécialisés qui garantissent pour celles qui les mettent en œuvre, des collectivités prospères, attractives et résilientes ; capables de relever les défis actuels et futurs.

L’AIFDT a développé l’Opération d’aménagement urbain durable (OPad) qui est une méthode de gestion de projet applicable à l’aménagement urbain. Les aménagements urbains durables constituent la base de la création des villes durables, car ils conduisent à la création d’un environnement plus sain pour les habitants de la zone considérée et à l’obtention d’une empreinte carbone plus faible pour la ville toute entière.

L’OPad est exécutable à l’échelle du quartier comme du territoire. Elle met en œuvre des plans d’aménagement qui favorisent le développement économique et social tout en préservant l’environnement, la santé et la qualité de vie des habitants. Elle augmente la taille des espaces verts, récréatifs et publics, avec l’établissement de plusieurs moyens de mobilité durable, en coordination avec les administrations ; des options de logement intégrées et diversifiées, respectant les normes de planification.

Description:

A key factor in the creation of long term green spaces is creating a positive commercial platform, which offers opportunities for local entrepreneurs, and supports our goal of providing green spaces for those communities who have no current access to them.

Name: Good Human Habitat
Description:
The Focus:

What is good human habit and where do we find it?

Why don’t we have more of it?

What must we do to create it?

The Mission:

Good Human Habitat advocates for municipal governments to pursue four objectives that are necessary to halt urban sprawl’s expansion and to consistently build at the human scale. These objectives are derived from best practices in municipalities (suburban and urban) where walkability can be validated analytically.

Consider the following two points.

1. Surveys show that most adults would prefer living in vibrant, walkable, human-scale communities if they were available. Yet urban sprawl prevails.

2. Over 60% of CO2 emissions tie back to two key things that municipal governments do, namely deciding what and where things get built. And what most municipalities build is urban sprawl, in both its vertical (high-rise) and horizontal (suburban) variants.

There’s a connection here.

Many municipal governments across the United States and Canada have ostensibly made commitments to “walkability” and “climate action.” Yet most continue to mandate development patterns that diminish the pedestrian experience and maximize emissions. Often, the gap between rhetoric and reality is wide.

Good Human Habitat (GHH) is a non-profit initiative that promotes independent, third-party assessments of local governments, helping evaluate whether their stated commitments are being realized in practice.

Local journalism and community input are key to producing a credible, effective assessment.

Importantly, these assessments bring tangible benefits to those living in established suburban areas.

Third-party assessments clarify where a municipality stands regarding the four objectives, namely critical mass, strategic vision, clarity of entitlement, and design competence. Absent these objectives, urban sprawl will prevail.

Description:
At Media Mile Development LLC, our mission is to transform underserved neighborhoods into vibrant, walkable, and sustainable communities that balance innovation, equity, and culture. We specialize in mixed-use projects that integrate affordable housing, smart and green technologies, and media-driven spaces—creating opportunities for residents, entrepreneurs, and creatives alike. Guided by a people-first approach, we are committed to building communities that empower generations and ensure long-term prosperity.

Our Approach

We believe that real estate is more than construction—it’s a generational investment. That’s why our approach combines walkable design, modern infrastructure, and shared ownership to create places where culture, business, and community grow side by side. We remain engaged long after the ribbon cutting. Through our community endowment model, project revenues are reinvested locally to support programs, maintenance, and new opportunities for the people who live there.

Our Projects

Media on Madison (MOM District) – A transformative redevelopment in West Garfield Park, Chicago, anchored by a high-tech Content Fulfillment Center, coworking spaces, and retail storefronts built for creators and entrepreneurs.

Deer Pointe Subdivision, Blue Island – Adding 58 affordable homes and a neighborhood business hub to expand access to housing and economic stability.

Pacific Rim Acoustical Research Station, Indonesia – Developed with NWAA Labs, this global partnership introduces high-precision sound testing facilities to Southeast Asia.

Our Partners

From Chicago to the Pacific Rim, Media Mile’s work is made possible by strong partnerships and a shared commitment to inclusive growth. With support from organizations like TAP, IndyBuild Corp., and NWAA Labs, we are setting a new example for development—one that is practical, inclusive, and built to endure.

Description:
The Versailles Growth Committee is a new, energetic, and committed group of people who are dedicated to creating optimism and growth in their small community. In 2022, the group formed to revitalize their village and give its residents reasons to feel connected and proud of their hometown. 

The VGC began by creating opportunities for people to come together, as in the new block party event, which provides free food and entertainment in the town park. They work to make the village more attractive, having started by painting storefronts of empty buildings along its main road. The VGC is now raising money and volunteers to bring the town’s ballpark back to playing condition, while adding benches and play structures to the park around it. We publish a monthly newsletter to help residents feel connected and get to know more about their neighbors. We have lots of ideas of what we can do to continue the momentum of building a thriving little town. Together, we can do more!

Name: OnWater Foundation
Description:
OnWater is a mission-driven social enterprise that transforms underused urban waterways into vibrant platforms for youth development, civic engagement, and ecosystem stewardship. We guide young people through a transformative journey; from gaining confidence on the water, to becoming local creative change-makers rooted in community and care.

Why? Young people, especially in high-density, multicultural, and under-resourced urban environments face intersecting challenges:

+ Disconnection from nature and public outdoor space, especially clean and accessible blue space, which are often polluted, unsafe, or associated with exclusivity.

+ Poor mental and physical health, with rising rates of anxiety, ADHD, and chronic loneliness exacerbated by digital isolation and fragmented community life.

+ Low self-esteem and belonging, with limited pathways to leadership, employment or authentic self- expression.

What’s missing isn’t just access to activity, it’s access to identity, purpose, and contribution in the places we live. Youth aren’t just lacking “engagement.” They lack safe, joyful, meaningful roles in their environment and community. And our current systems; education, leisure, civic infrastructure, often fail to meet them where they are, let alone support youth longterm. 

Our approach, rooted in paddlesport, fitness, storytelling, and leadership, helps young people cultivate resilience, self-worth, and a strong sense of community. 
Underpinning the journey are our 8 human development pillars: Dependability, Discipline, Determination, Durability, Direction, Discernment, Drive, Duty.

By regenerating access to neglected waterways and reframing them as public commons, we create tangible opportunities for youth to lead, collaborate, and care for their environments.

Name: ELLEN VERBIEST
Description:
Ellen Verbiest, architect en stedenbouwkundig onderzoeker met een passie voor printmaking, navigeert door complexe ruimtelijke concepten door middel van verbindende illustraties en cartografie. Ze ontwerpt en begeleidt participatieve processen op maat, waarbij ze uiteenlopende perspectieven samenbrengt tot ruimtelijke strategieën. Haar werk dient publieke overheden en wereldverbeteraars, en krijgt vorm via workshops, web, print en tentoonstellingen.

Ellen werkt op vraag van overheden, ontwerpteams, onderzoekers en maatschappelijke organisaties aan participatieve ontwerptrajecten, kaarten en visualisaties op maat, workshops, tentoonstellingen en ruimtelijke verbeeldingen in het kader van beleidsontwikkeling, ontwerpend onderzoek of strategische gebiedstrajecten.

Ellen werkt(e) samen met onder andere AR-TUR, het Departement Omgeving, Stadsatelier Oostende, LABO RUIMTE, Team Vlaams Bouwmeester, de provincie West-Vlaanderen, wooncoöperaties en de Stadsbouwmeester van Gent.

Ze zet zich in voor trajecten waarin ontwerp en verbeelding maatschappelijke kwesties tastbaar maken. Zo was ze mede-initiatiefnemer van We Kopen Samen den Oudaan, een collectief van geëngageerde architecten en stedenbouwkundigen om de iconische Oudaan te kopen en te ontwikkelen. Ze maakte deel uit van Onder Stroom, een creatieve broedplaats die vanuit de eigen positie onderzoek doet naar leegstandsbeheer en tijdelijke invullingen voor kunst en cultuur.

Ellen Verbiest rondde in 2025 haar doctoraatsonderzoek af aan de KU Leuven (Departement Architectuur), in het kader van het onderzoeksproject Regenerating Flemish Industry Parks (REFLIP). Haar onderzoek bestudeerde hoe socio-spatiale visualisaties zoals kaarten, wandelroutes, toekomstbeelden en stripverhalen kunnen bijdragen aan het inzichtelijk maken van circulaire transitieprocessen op bestaande bedrijventerreinen.

Name: Buckley Downtown Association
Description:
BDA is a new non-profit 503 (C) organization in Buckley. We are a part of the Washington Main Street Program, which helps communities revitalize the economy, appearance, and image of their downtown commercial districts in
Washington. The program is managed (under contract) by the Washington Trust for Historic Preservation. 

Our mission came to life when the City of Buckley organized a committee of community & business members and formed the Downtown Revitalization Committee. The purpose of this was to create a strategic plan to improve the vibrancy, commercial composition, and utilization of Buckley’s downtown. The committee saw the Main Street Program as an opportunity they could use to help implement the Downtown Revitalization Strategy, and access resources that
would help us make impactful changes.

Main Street of America has a 4 point approach that focuses on: 

Economic Vitality:  
Partner with local businesses. Provide guidance and support to them. Encourage new businesses downtown. Strategically plan for sustainable growth.

Promotion: 
Implement a marketing strategy that communicates our values and positions itself as a hub of economic activity for downtown revitalization.

Outreach: 
Intentional engagement with the community in ways that meets needs, & connect to downtown. Establish effective communication processes that serve the community & businesses.

Design:
Create an inviting, inclusive environment that celebrates history and culture.  Foster accessibility, and make downtown an experience...not just a place. 
 
BDA Board of Directors:
Elizabeth Carter - president
Matt McCollum - vice president
Lorna Anderson - treasurer
Amy Molen - board member
Ali Santman - board member

Description:
The Roots. The Dig. The Shroom. 

dig differently was founded on the story of the mighty mushroom and its heroic journey into the Industrial Involution, a period of deep revelation of the heart, mind, and soul. It is a community that helps reconnect people back to nature, believing that the greatest way to do that, is to listen and embark on the true path in which the universe is calling us upon. dig differently fosters the intrinsic side of the climate fight; the revolution of transforming the ego to eco. It is a community that reminds people that we all have the power of the mushroom in us: the power to create a new economy of reciprocity, the power to reach a state of presence, and the power to inspire new seeds of beautiful inner-connected & interconnected dreams.

This community creates immersive experiences and spaces to inspire others to search for their gifts and how they can use them for the good in the world. We are a group of time rebellions that are slowing down to become good ancestors for our future generations. Everything we do is for the planet. The sole intention is to save our species and all the others we are affecting. We don't call it Climate Change anymore; it's People Change. Mother Earth will always be okay. It is we who are suffering. dig differently is all about creating events and projects that begin to shift people's minds, showing them through intention and compassion, there are alternative choices we can make that won't hurt us or the planet. 

By envisioning these new ecosystems that integrate technology with an indigenous mentality, dig differently plants itself as the seed that reimagines life post capitalism. This community mimics the magic of mycelium and its fruiting fungi: how we must seek nourishment in the roots before we can begin to reach our destination, above ground and towards the light where we can finally stop digging and just be. 

But for now…

Keep on digging!

www.digdifferently.com 
bit.ly/diggerforlife

Name: Original Manufacturing Inc.
Description:
We’re not here to follow trends—we’re here to set the standard for a food system that serves our communities. At Original Manufacturing Inc., we are architects of change, reshaping the grocery supply chain, designing agriculture infrastructure, and fighting for equity through bold policy advocacy.

What We Do:
 Revolutionizing the Grocery Supply Chain: From mobile grocery markets to statewide distribution networks, we’re closing the gaps in food access and creating pathways for Black farmers to reach underserved communities. As the primary distributor of Ujamaa Farmer Collective produce and products, we ensure fresh, organic, and culturally relevant food gets to the people who need it most.

 Designing Agricultural Infrastructure: We don’t just imagine solutions—we build them. From climate-smart aggregation hubs to farmer cooperatives and mobile grocery fleets, we’re laying the foundation for a sustainable, equitable food system.

 Advocating for Policy Change: We know the barriers that keep communities out of the food system are systemic. That’s why we fight at every level of government to secure land access, funding, and infrastructure for Black and underserved farmers.

Our Impact:
Driving Economic Justice: By creating new supply chains and market opportunities, we empower farmers to break free from exploitative systems and build generational wealth.

Revolutionizing Food Access: Our projects, like Imani Gardens and ASYRS BRIDGE, bridge the gap between farmers and communities, ensuring fresh, affordable food is always within reach.

Pioneering Equity: We’ve championed groundbreaking initiatives, including America’s first land reparations fund for Black farmers, while distributing millions of dollars into Black agricultural projects.

Who We Are:
We are builders of food equity, architects of agricultural empowerment, and warriors for systemic justice. 

We are the trusted administrative and strategic partner of the Ujamaa Farmer Collective.

Description:

CADECOM’s mission aims at creating awareness and empowering disadvantaged men, women and the youth at all levels to undertake development which is integral, gender and environment sensitive, sustainable and which promotes Justice, Human Dignity, Self-Reliance with the active participation of the people themselves so that they take up the responsibility of their own destiny.
CADECOM works in six strategic areas: - food security and nutrition, gender (women and youth economic empowerment) relief and rehabilitation, Water, hygiene and sanitation, Climate Change adaptation and mitigation, Disaster Risk Reduction, lobbying and advocacy, Training for Transformation and crosscutting issues (HIV/AIDS, disability, and human rights).
Although CADECOM is an arm of the Catholic Church in Malawi, it does not discriminate; it works with people of all religions. Our work and targeting procedure is determined by levels of vulnerability!

Name: Center for Community Design and Preservation
Description:
We provide opportunities for our faculty and students to engage in real-world projects and put their academic pursuits into practice.

Through our Public Service Request system, we respond to a range of requests for assistance, from residential landscape design problems via our Student Job Board to large-scale community improvement challenges incorporated into CED studios and coursework.

We offer community Design Charrettes as a service-learning opportunity for students as well as outreach to Georgia communities. “Charrette” describes a rapid, intensive, and creative work session in which a design team works with local stakeholders to focus on a particular design problem and arrive at a collaborative solution.

CCDP also houses FindIt— a statewide historic resources survey program in conjunction with the Georgia Transmission Corporation. This long-standing program provides invaluable graduate assistantships to CED students, enhancing their learning experience and the programs’ successes.

Name: Emberbrook Village
Description:
Emberbrook is a visionary real estate concept designed to address the growing demand for connection, walkability, and intentional living.

In an era defined by suburban sprawl and social isolation, we are developing a blueprint for a Narrative-Driven Hamlet—a place where the built environment fosters community rather than separation.

The Concept: Storybook New Urbanism Emberbrook combines the principles of New Urbanism (human-scale design, walkability, mixed-use spaces) with the aesthetic of "Old World" craftsmanship. We are moving beyond the "cookie-cutter" model to create a village defined by timeless architecture, nature preservation, and "Third Places" that encourage organic social interaction.

Our Core Philosophy: We believe that where you live should support the story you want to tell with your life. Emberbrook trades square footage for propinquity, car-dependency for cobblestones, and isolation for a village lifestyle.

Project Status: Pre-Development & Concept Emberbrook is currently in the strategic planning and conceptual design phase. 

We are currently accepting inquiries from potential collaborators via the Strategic Partnership Form on our website or via email—hello@emberbrookvillage.com.

Name: EuFiscal
Description:
EuFiscal é a rede social que dá voz a quem quer transformar o país.

O projeto surgiu da ideia de colocar cada pessoa no centro da fiscalização, dando voz a quem vive os problemas todos os dias e muitas vezes não é ouvido.

Mais do que um espaço para denunciar, o EuFiscal é um movimento de participação. Ele conecta pessoas, amplia a visibilidade dos problemas e mostra que juntos podemos pressionar por soluções reais.  Aqui, você pode dar visibilidade a questões que impactam diretamente o seu dia a dia, desde ruas esburacadas, transporte precário, lixo acumulado, falta de médicos e remédios, até falhas na segurança e na infraestrutura da cidade.

Nosso propósito é simples. Criar uma cultura de cidadania ativa.
A transformação da cidade não começa apenas em gabinetes ou em promessas políticas, mas na atitude de criticar e buscar mudanças de quem está nas ruas e enfrenta diariamente os mesmos problemas sem ter onde de fato fazer sua voz ser ouvida.

O EuFiscal nasceu para isso. Para ser o elo entre a indignação e a mudança.

Um lugar em que sua voz ganha força, cada relato conta e a comunidade se torna protagonista em busca de um Brasil melhor.

Welcome to the Cities Expo & Symposium.  

Join us in building a better urban future!

Cities Expo & Symposium is a premier annual event that brings together urban stakeholders, policymakers, investors, civil society representatives, and international partners to address the complexities of urbanization and promote sustainable city development.

Our Mission

We aim to advance the development of Uganda's cities by fostering collaboration, showcasing investment opportunities, and presenting actionable solutions to urban challenges. Our goal is to create planned, inclusive, and resilient cities that contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Our Focus

Building smart, green, resilient, and climate-responsive cities for all.
Showcasing innovative solutions to urban challenges.
Fostering collaboration and knowledge exchange among urban stakeholders.
Promoting investment opportunities in Uganda's cities.
Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Description:
