Whether you’re a business owner facing red tape, a public agency needing targeted support, or a project team seeking alignment with City or County officials, we help you get to yes.

We build consensus. From public counters to hearing rooms, we work directly with architects, engineers, government staff, elected officials, and community stakeholders to bridge the gap between vision and approval. Our team is fluent in both by-right solutions and discretionary entitlements, bringing practical solutions to complex regulatory environments.

What sets us apart? We don’t just interpret policy—we live it. Our team has hands-on experience implementing California’s rapidly evolving housing and land use laws. We actively monitor the Legislature and local agencies to stay ahead of shifting mandates and codes. This real-time awareness gives our clients a crucial advantage: smarter strategies, fewer surprises, and faster progress.

Name: Black August UK LTD
Description:
Our Story
It is incredible how one small idea can grow into something truly special. Black August UK is rooted in the belief that we all have an inherent responsibility to make a meaningful difference in our community. With a variety of active projects  we harness our skills and resources to successfully achieve our goals.



Introducing Black August to the United Kingdom.. The U.K also has its fair share of country based history and events
happening in august.



Join the Black August Movement: Honoring Freedom Fighters in the UK and Beyond

The Black August movement has gained significant momentum worldwide, which now includes the United Kingdom. This movement aims to celebrate, honor and remember freedom fighters throughout history that took place in AUGUST. THE REAL BLACK HISTRORY MONTH. Pay homage to those who have sacrificed their lives for the pursuit of justice, equality, and liberation for the Black community.

In the UK, the Black August we here at Black August UK would like to see movement to increase awareness and participation, with individuals and organizations coming together to pay tribute to these inspirational figures, dates and accomplishments. Events, rallies, and discussions are organized to highlight the contributions these freedom fighters have made to the ongoing struggle against oppression.

 

 

One major platform for the Black August Movement in the UK is the website blackaugustuk.com. This website serves as a hub of information and resources for those interested in participating and learning more about the movement. From historical profiles of key figures to upcoming events and initiatives, blackaugustuk.com provides a comprehensive overview of the movement’s activities.

Black August originated in the United States during the 1970s as a response to the murders of three incarcerated Black revolutionaries: Jonathan Jackson, George Jackson, and Khatari Gaulden.

Name: Convivial
Description:
Convivial ist ein interaktives Beteiligungstool, das Spieler*innen dabei begleitet ihre Ideen auf eine kreative und gemeinschaftliche Weise zu entwickeln und zu visualisieren.

Convivial schafft einen Raum in dem verschiedene Akteure mit unterschiedlichen Erfahrungen und Kommunikationsweisen ihre Ideen auf Augenhöhe entwickeln können. Durch einen strukturierten Aufbau und eine klare Spielanleitung, benötigt Convivial minimale Moderation und ermöglicht Spieler*innen autonom zusammenzuarbeiten. Somit ist es optimal für Großgruppenformate geeignet.

Am Ende des Arbeitsprozesses entstehen zwei greifbare, zielorientierte Ergebnisse, welche die Schlüsselelemente der Idee gebündelt darstellen. Ein ausgefüllter Ideen-Canvas und eine kreative Visualisierung der Idee.

Convivial eignet sich für Projekte mit einem klaren räumlichen oder thematischen Rahmen, in dem ein Bedarf für Zukunftsideen oder Lösungen für konkrete Herausforderungen besteht. Zum Beispiel die Entwicklung von soziale Infrastrukturen in einem Stadtviertel.

Workshops werden in Deutsch und Englisch angeboten. Die Fragestellungen, Sprache, Gestaltung und Dauer können an das jeweilige Projekt angepasst werden.

Interessiert an einer Zusammenarbeit?

Name: Nukanga Network School
Description:
The Big Idea

All economic success depends on successful entrepreneurs, but 50% fail due to prioritizing scale over alignment, resulting in low conversion rates; spending money to launch products that the market doesn’t actually need and 95% of ads getting ignored.

Business failure means economic collapse; billions of people can’t own a home, have weak job security and live in financial stress. No businesses means no jobs, no salary, no sponsors, no investment, no retirement – no society.

For entrepreneurs to be successful and create economic prosperity for all, the market must completely shift focus to maximize conversion rates. To go from quantity to quality, scale to affinity, a numbers game to product-market fit, a random audience to ideal customers, and from blind mass marketing to perfect matchmaking.

Our stories run deep. They are complex and full of emotions such as joy, excitement, anxiety, delight, fear, sorrow, pain, jubilation, regret, success. Humans of Idaho tells the story of ordinary Idahoans accomplishing amazing feats.

Humans of Idaho raises funds for Idahoans to accomplish personal missions or professional goals. 100% of donations raised go directly to featured humans of Idaho! And your donation is tax deductible.

Each month, we feature one Idahoan making a difference in their community. It might be a small business owner seeking to expand their small town enterprise. It could be a painter who immigrated to Idaho as a refugee with dreams of one day showcasing their art in a museum or painting a mural on the side of a local business. It could be your neighbor, a decorated military veteran who was injured protecting our country and now teaches disabled kids to ski at your local mountain. Or it is that local high school student with a clever innovation that needs seed money to get started.

Humans of Idaho tells the stories of our friends, family, neighbors, and colleagues. Come back to see who will be featured next!

Name: The Smith Enterprise
Description: Beginning their  first proof of  concept in 2015 with their first contract being services for  political campaigning, The Smith Enterprise has created a trajectory like no other. In their beginning phase, they started with services like  Public Relations, Marketing, and Campaign Management. Their target clients ranged from Former South Carolina State Senator Glenn Reese, to Presidential candidates like Dr. Willie Wilson and Hilary Clinton. Due to  bandwidth, they were able to successfully scale their  clients goals, while also expanding their niche market and service offerings. In 2017, The Smith Enterprise was offered a new skill set and training opportunity for policy writing and lobbying for the Hemp Farm Bill and the Hemp Farming Program bill in South Carolina. In 2018, The Smith Enterprise relocated to Las Vegas, where they established their business legally, and began devoting their target market to lobbying in the cannabis industry. Some bills that The Smith Enterprise partook in lobbying for include the Assembly Bill 533 in Las Vegas and Proposition 207 in Arizona. Once the pandemic took effect, The Smith Enterprise decided to cease operations and take time for mental wellness. Upon relocating back to Georgia, The Smith Enterprise has secured 15 partnerships in the Georgia community creating over 300 job opportunities, and on average 15 new education programs that promote community wellness and legacy. Since the business anniversary is in March of 2018, The Smith Enterprise is currently in the rebranding and relaunching phase of expansion. March 2024, new services, programs, partnerships, and opportunities will be available exclusively  to the Georgia community.

Our Mission: To provide specialized GIS consulting services that empower small and medium-sized businesses to make data-driven decisions and optimize their operations.
About Us:
At Terraforma GeoSolutions, we believe the power of place belongs in every business. We are a UK-based, independent consultancy specializing in Location Intelligence and Geographic Information Systems (GIS). Our mission is to transform complex geographic data into clear, actionable insights and stunning visual maps that help you solve real-world problems.
We work with clients across diverse sectors, including:
PropTech & Real Estate: Creating location analysis reports and custom maps that sell properties faster and provide a competitive edge.
Logistics & Retail: Optimising routes, analysing consumer demographics, and identifying ideal store locations for greater efficiency and revenue.
Environmental & Planning: Providing affordable environmental and flood risk assessments, and creating compelling maps for planning applications and conservation projects.
We offer a lean, accessible approach, utilising powerful open-source tools like QGIS to deliver high-quality, professional outputs without the high costs of traditional consulting firms. Whether you need a single, branded map for a presentation or a comprehensive report to support a key business decision, we help you turn location into opportunity.
Our Services Include:
Custom Map Production
Location & Site Analysis Reports
Geospatial Data Analysis & Visualisation
Environmental Assessment Reports
Interactive Web Maps
Connect with us to discover how smart mapping can lead to smarter decisions for your business.

Name: AVANTKOFA | Redevelopment & Design Studios
Description:
AVANTKOFA draws from the Sankofa principle—"fly forward, fetch the past"—reminding us that progress requires understanding where we've been. AVANT (before) anchors us in history's lessons, while KOFA propels us toward what's next. Lasting security and prosperity emerge when we honor the past while building for tomorrow.

AVANTKOFA is a redevelopment and design studio specializing in housing, policy innovation, and community infrastructure. We translate policy into buildable plans, crisis into resilience, and vision into outcomes. Our work spans housing and redevelopment strategy, policy design and implementation, crisis resilience and emergency management planning, strategic communications and stakeholder engagement, and capacity-building for organizations navigating complex change.

We approach every project with a community's foundational securities in mind—housing, health, safety, access to information and capital (both human and economic), and the integrity of the built and natural environment. These aren't aspirations. They're prerequisites. Without them, no policy, system, or intervention can deliver lasting outcomes.

Drawing from two decades across public safety, urban planning, housing administration, and community development, we operate with one core belief: lasting security comes from uplifting places and people together. We partner with public sector leaders, community organizations, and mission-driven institutions to build infrastructure—physical, economic, social—that protects and serves the people who live, work, and build in a place, not just those who invest in it.

That's the work. That's the throughline.

Name: Soil Carbon Academy
Description:
About Soil Carbon Academy

At Soil Carbon Academy, we’re on a mission to empower farmers, researchers, and innovators to unlock the full potential of soil as a carbon sink. By combining science, technology, and hands-on field knowledge, we help accelerate the transition toward climate-positive agriculture across Africa and beyond.

Our work focuses on:
	•	Biochar production and application — turning agricultural waste into long-term soil carbon storage.
	•	Soil health education — equipping farmers with practical skills to improve yields while regenerating ecosystems.
	•	Carbon measurement and verification — using data-driven methods to quantify and validate soil carbon sequestration.
	•	Community impact — fostering local value chains that create green jobs and sustainable income opportunities.

Through collaboration, research, and training, Soil Carbon Academy aims to build a new generation of climate-smart farmers and carbon-conscious entrepreneurs who can help restore our planet’s most powerful carbon sink — the soil beneath our feet.

 Learn more, grow more, and join the movement to rebuild the Earth from the ground up.

By improving submission quality and minimizing the risk of longer permit processing times, Apruva helps you avoid additional financing and holding costs. Our service enables you to keep projects on schedule and focus on core development activities rather than administrative challenges.

Ready to take the stress out of permit coordination? Connect with us or visit our website to learn more about how Apruva can help your next project succeed.

Name: Tewin Community Advisory Committee (TCAC)
Description:
The purpose of the Tewin Community Advisory Committee (TCAC) is to facilitate collaboration and the exchange of information between the local community and the Tewin Project Team (the Algonquins of Ontario, their partner Taggart Group and the consulting team), local political representatives, City Council, and City of Ottawa staff.

The Algonquins of Ontario and their partner, Taggart Group, are landowners of the Tewin Project (Tewin). Tewin is an exciting city-building project that will transform land in southeast Ottawa into a sustainable, connected, and complete community founded on Algonquin values. It is comprised of approximately 445 hectares of gross developable land that has recently been added to Ottawa’s Urban Boundary. The Algonquins of Ontario are the stewards of the land and its resources. Tewin is an unparalleled opportunity for the Algonquins of Ontario to participate more fully in Canada’s socio-economic fabric and seize opportunities for community building, sustainability, and economic prosperity. Algonquin principles and teachings will guide all aspects of the planning, consultation, and design in the ongoing development process for Tewin.

Tewin will become a “best-in-class” example of sustainable community development, integrating the Algonquin principles of respect for the earth with the One Planet Living framework. One Planet Living is a vision of a world where we can live happily within the Earth’s resources much like First Nations peoples’ relationship with Mother Earth. It also provides a straightforward framework to achieve this. Tewin will demonstrate how a new holistic vision for community development can address critical challenges like affordability, climate change, housing, and reconciliation.

Name: WOW Tanzania
Description:
Wakefulness Orphans and Widows Tanzania (WOW Tanzania or WOWT) was established as a mainland Tanzania NGO organization, on May 22, 2022, by three co-founders. It was in 2021 when 2 friends, Mrs. Paula Chomba originally from the USA, and Mrs. Betsie Green a native South African, decided to relocate from their homes in France and the UK to open an NGO with entrepreneurship classes and support services that would cater to the specific needs of orphans and widows in Tanzania. Mr. Mike Chomba, Paula’s then fiancé from Zambia joined the NGO shortly thereafter.

We operate a nonprofit facility governed by an active 11-member volunteer board of directors, including the three organization’s founders. We also have three local Tanzanian volunteers serving as a receptionist and field workers. We offer a variety of programs at our center to meet the needs of those we serve. Based in USA River, Arusha we provide entrepreneurial and language training, tutoring, mentoring, exposure to careers in the science and technology sectors, charity, school sponsorships, counseling, and family support to those in need, to create community-conscious/anti-corruption African leaders and innovators for tomorrow.

Having begun the work in Tanzania with orphans at an orphanage in Shagari from June 2021 to April 2022, today, we have registered approximately 80 at-risk children into our program, and are currently in partnership with two schools, Kilimani Primary, and USA River Primary School. After opening our offices at JOBIGs Center in Arusha, in June 2022, we began entrepreneurship training as well as our small loan programs. Since our inception, we have proudly sponsored more than 100 children with school lunches and school uniforms. Other achievements include building a computer lab for Nkoanrua secondary school in Arusha Tanzania and contributing to cementing for building local restrooms.

O principal objetivo é o aprimorar indicadores de felicidade em regiões ou comunidades através de uma abordagem multidisciplinar que contempla inovação, futurismo, desenvolvimento comunitário, mapeamentos e pesquisas através de metodologia autoral, capacitação em inteligência emocional, cocriação de transformações coletivas em espaços físicos compartilhados, mensuração de dados emocionais através de tecnologia exclusiva e análise de dados em tempo real para tomadas de decisão ágeis e assertivas.

As metodologias e tecnologias Cores da Felicidade são regenerativas, não paliativas. Por isso, podem ser adaptadas de acordo com diferentes contextos e aplicadas de maneira cíclica e estruturada na resolução de desafios amplos como a necessidade de articulações contra o agravamento da crise global de saúde mental, superação de impactos sociais e emocionais causados por catástrofes e a crescente onda de violência que assola as comunidades escolares brasileiras.

Atualmente, Cores da Felicidade trabalha ao lado dos setores público e privado na implementação de soluções que tornam as comunidades mais inteligentes e felizes numa construção feita com as pessoas, não apenas para elas. Algumas iniciativas em andamento são direcionadas: 1. à regiões e microrregiões afetadas socioeconômica e emocionalmente por uma catástrofe; 2. prevenção e regeneração da cultura de paz nas comunidades escolares brasileiras.

Our work bridges permaculture principles, ecological restoration, and community-driven urban planning to create spaces where both people and nature thrive.

We believe that the future of sustainable development lies in reconnecting communities with their local ecosystems. From designing food forests and water-smart landscapes to promoting pollinator corridors and soil regeneration, we help residents, organizations, and municipalities create landscapes that provide food security, climate resilience, and accessible green spaces for all.

As advocates for food sovereignty and inclusive design, we prioritize creating disability-friendly gardens and landscapes that welcome people of all abilities and backgrounds. Whether it’s a small urban lot, a public park, or a large-scale community green space, we ensure that everyone can engage with and benefit from nature.

Our Mission:
To empower the people of Richland County to take an active role in shaping regenerative, resilient landscapes that enhance ecological health, support local food systems, and foster a deeper connection to the land and each other.

Our Focus Areas:
 Regenerative Landscape Design
 Sustainable Urban Planning
 Biodiversity & Habitat Restoration
 Food Sovereignty & Community Gardens
 Accessible & Inclusive Green Spaces
 Climate Resilience & Water Management

Through collaborative design processes, educational outreach, and hands-on projects, Mansfield Permaculture works to transform underutilized land into productive, beautiful, and ecologically rich spaces that nurture both people and planet.

Name: VERITAS
Description:
Welcome to VERITAS: Space for Big Ideas

VERITAS is not just a location. It’s a bold experiment in rethinking how we live and work. Located on the historic site of the former Singer sewing machine factory, right on the Elbe River, VERITAS is a place where the past meets the future. With 90,000 sqm of space on a 16-hectare green campus, we’re transforming this iconic industrial site into a living lab for sustainable urban innovation—a true Space for Big Ideas.

When you come to
VERITAS you challenge
your way of life.

VERITAS is a way of living and working, of thinking and acting. Here, we create spaces that invite people to challenge the way we live and work. We host, house, and hone creative thinkers from diverse disciplines—art, science, technology, and business. By fostering collaboration and experimentation, we enable transformative ideas for the future of urban living and working.

Together, we don’t just plan these spaces—we create them, test them, and live and work in them. Inspired by Wittenberge’s legacy of innovation and craftsmanship, we carry forward its spirit of making things that last. At VERITAS, we dare to slow down in times of rapid change, believing that true progress comes from deliberate care and thoughtful action. That’s the VERITAS way: forever evolving.

Our Vision
We aim to develop a sustainable
way of living (and working) that
enriches everyone’s life.

VERITAS thrives on collaboration. We’re seeking dreamers, doers, and visionaries who share our passion for reimagining the way we live and work. Whether you’re an entrepreneur, artist, scientist, or investor, there’s a place for you here.

Name: Gemeinwesenentwicklung Stadtfeld e.V.
Description:
Gemeinwesenentwicklung Stadtfeld: dieser Name ist Programm!
Dahinter verbirgt sich ein Vorhaben, mit welchem Bewegung in ein Stadtgebiet gebracht werden soll, das lange als vernachlässigt und problembelastet galt.

 

Gemeinwesen? - In diesem Wort steckt Gemeinschaft, steht das Bild einer guten, stabilen Nachbarschaft.

Entwicklung? - Darin steckt Bewegung, das heißt Aufbruch und Wachstum hin zu einem Ziel.

 

Mit der angestrebten "Gemeinwesenentwicklung" soll mehr angestossen werden, als Verschönerung von Gebäuden oder Grünflächen. Es geht vielmehr um Impulse für eine Anregung des Nachbarschaftsleben. Denn Bewegung und Veränderung kann nur gemeinschaftlich entstehen.

 

Hinter dieser Vision steht auch ein Konzept. Mit Gemeinwesenarbeit und sozialräumlichen Ansätzen wurden in anderen Hildesheimer Stadtteilen (siehe Drispenstedt und Fahrenheit) gute Erfahrungen gemacht und starke Ergebnisse erzielt. Die Hildesheimer Siedlung Stadtfeld schien ebenfalls sehr geeignet, um mit den aktivierenden Methoden der Gemeinwesenarbeit die Nachbarschaft zu beleben.

 

Dafür braucht es auch stabile Strukturen und ein starkes, eng verknüpftes Netzwerk: denn nicht nur Fachleute sollen beraten und bestimmen. Es sind die Anwohner, die als "Experten" ihres Lebensumfeldes am besten wissen, was gebraucht wird und geschehen sollte.

Name: The Nairobi Physical and Land Use Planning Liaison Committee
Description:
The Nairobi Physical and Land Use Planning Liaison Committee is established pursuant to section 76 of the Physical and Land Use Planning Act, 2019.

The Liaison Committee is composed of professionals from various fields, including Advocates, Physical Planners, Architects, Surveyors among others. 
It is expected that the diversity of Professionals in the committee will enrich the committee's deliberations and in turn the committee will be better equipped to address all facets related to planning and land use within the county.
The committee can also co-opt 5 additional members, by law, if they require more expertise in a dispute.
The committee is made up of 8 members and headed by a Chairperson and is currently domiciled at the City Hall Court room 2 or 3. The City Government of Nairobi supports the committee operations by providing Secretariat, provision of a court room and payment of allowances.

The functions of the committee are found under section 78 of the Act and they include:-
a.) Hearing and determining complaints and claims made in respect to applications submitted to the County.
b.) Hearing appeals against decisions made by the Planning Authority with respect to Physical and Land Use Development Plans in the County.
c.) Advise the County Executive Committee Member in charge of Planning and Land Use in the County.
d.) Handle enforcement disputes.

Name: Plzeň na kole
Description:
Spolek Plzeň na kole fandí jízdnímu kolu coby (přinejmenším) rovnoprávnému prostředku individuální dopravy ve městě a zasazuje se o lepší podmínky pro městskou cyklistiku.

Vznikli jsme v září 2012 jako iniciativa a převzali pořádání cyklojízd od občanského sdružení Děti Země. V roce 2017 jsme se z iniciativy přeměnili na zapsaný spolek.

Plzeň na kole spoluvytváří komunitu lidí, kteří po Plzni jezdí na kole (nebo koloběžce, elektrokoloběžce, jednokolce, …). Jestli se podaří malými či většími kroky zlepšovat bezpečnost a pohodlí městských cyklistek a cyklistů v Plzni, nebude tu spolek nadarmo. Našimi méně viditelnými činnostmi je dlouhodobá komunikace s úřady, policií a představiteli města ohledně budování infrastruktury a zohledňování cyklistů v každodenním provozu.

Kam míříme:

- Usilujeme o kvalitnější a bezpečnější cyklo infrastrukturu v Plzni
- Podporujeme každodenní cyklodopravu
- Propojujeme městské cyklisty

Name: Mend London Ltd
Description:
Mending the connection between people and place
Pioneers in Social Value and Considerate Urbanism: Mend is a leading consultancy dedicated to advancing social sustainability, inclusive placemaking, and responsible urban development. Our innovative approach, grounded in the principles of Considerate Urbanism, empowers communities and stakeholders to create equitable, sustainable cities.

Shaping Socially Sustainable Cities with Mend: At Mend, we are passionate about fostering a care-based urbanism that prioritises empathy, human experience, and sustainability. Through collaboration and innovation, we envision cities that thrive socially, environmentally, and economically.

Transforming Communities and Places Through Responsible Placemaking

Liane is Founder-Director of Mend. She combines her personal interest in the social dynamics of how people live in cities and the future of cities, with her professional skills in strategy, policy, collaboration, and project delivery. Her work with Mend involves using planning, development, design, and procurement processes to make more socially responsible decisions and help generate better social outcomes and urban experiences for stakeholders by seeing the “Community as Client”. Her practitioner experience includes designing and delivering social sustainability and responsible procurement programmes for regeneration schemes and mega urban transport projects, including Crossrail, Bank Station Capacity Upgrade and Tideway. She is particularly skilled in understanding and maximising the interface between infrastructure, urban regeneration, and community development. Liane is also skilled at understanding the practicalities and complexities of delivering on policy ambitions in project environments. 

Experts in creating social value, fostering considerate urbanism, and delivering impactful urban development strategies.

Name: Rajasthan Pravasi Foundation
Description: Rajasthan Pravasi Foundation, Pune is a community-driven initiative aimed at helping Rajasthani families living across India. We understand the emotional and financial burden that comes with the loss of a loved one, especially when far from home. With a small monthly subscription, we ensure that your family receives financial support in case of an untimely passing. The initiative is further backed by the Rajasthani government, offering you peace of mind no matter where life takes you. Beyond financial security, Rajasthan Pravasi Foundation fosters a sense of belonging among Rajasthani families spread across India.

Name: PLANOVATIVE Community Research and Development
Description:
Planovative is a community and regional planning consultancy and business optimization firm serving communities and businesses across British Columbia. Our multidisciplinary team—comprising planners, designers, data scientists, system developers, and community engagement specialists—is dedicated to fostering sustainable, inclusive, and vibrant communities while helping businesses thrive through systematic thinking, strategic planning, innovation, and data-informed and tech-based solutions.

At the heart of our work is a strong belief in the power of data-driven decision-making and meaningful community engagement. By integrating advanced geospatial technologies with human-centered design, we help our clients plan strategically, set clear priorities, and make informed decisions that address local needs and support long-term sustainability.

Planovative’s innovative approach has supported nonprofits, community organizations, and continues to grow through ongoing collaboration with municipalities, provincial agencies, and public service organizations throughout British Columbia. We are proud to help our partners achieve their development goals while honoring local character, promoting equity, and protecting environmental integrity.

Name: Play It Forward (FL)
Description:
Play It Forward is a Central Florida based initiative that acknowledges and addresses the disparity in sports participation across diverse communities. The prohibitive cost of sports equipment often deters enthusiastic young athletes from pursuing their interests. But with Play It Forward, unused sports gear tucked away in garages can find a new home with a child who would love to play but can't because of financial constraints. 

We aim to be the bridge that connects unused sports gear to aspiring athletes. The organization is determined to expand its reach to every community, ensuring that every child, irrespective of their financial background, gets an equal opportunity to enjoy the benefits of sports. By joining hands with Play It Forward, you become part of a transformative movement that promotes sports participation and nurtures the dreams of countless young athletes.

Play It Forward. Pass The Game On. Gear The Future.
